<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623230 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00022: (2021x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `261623230`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623230/2021x+Refresh1+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:26:01.257+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

Magic Collaboration Studio 2021x Refresh1 brings several great functionalities, like the possibility to index cross-resource element usage data so later you could query for resources using certain elements from the modeling tool, easier management of new external user permissions and assignments, and the capability to create a hierarchical category structure in the Teamwork Cloud repository.

The Collaborator app introduces the commit of multiple changes which significantly reduces the number of commits when editing models, the possibility to add hyperlinks to model elements, and further improvements to model editing functionality.

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix available

2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### Global Element Usage Indexing and Search (Technology Preview)

Teamwork Cloud coupled with Elasticsearch engine brings a powerful functionality allowing you to index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of Teamwork Cloud repository and later query these usages through the modeling tool. This enables you to evaluate what impact modifying or deleting certain model elements will have on other models that are using it.

Teamwork Cloud Admin Console is now enriched with a section dedicated to configuration, initiation, and management of resource indexes. You can choose what needs to be indexed, where, and how with just a few clicks!

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Teamwork Cloud Settings application UI allowing you to create and manage index configurations.

#### Nested Categories

The new version of Teamwork Cloud brings an ability to organize your resources in a flexible folder-like manner using nested categories. The hierarchical grouping of categories ensures that your huge repository will not turn into a mess and required resources will be found easier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Nested categories in Teamwork Cloud.

#### Managing User Permissions and Assignments

Now all external users created in Teamwork Cloud upon successful authentication can automatically be given a certain set of permissions and assignments. Once you enable the **Default user permissions template** option, all newly created external users are instantly added to the dedicated Default Template user group. This way you can manage new user permissions and assignments a lot quicker because they simply inherit them from the dedicated user group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A new option in the Settings application allowing you to manage new user permissions and assignments.

#### REST API Change Log

The [REST API change log](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2021xR1/REST+API+Change+Log) concerns the following areas:

- Changes in data types.
- A new query parameter.
- New URLs.

#### Cameo Collaborator

##### Commit of Multiple Changes

This version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud brings a major improvement allowing you to commit changes accumulated while editing a model in one go. This significantly reduces the number of commits and therefore decreases the database growth when editing models.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.

##### Adding Hyperlinks to Model Elements

Now Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud supports cross-references to model elements in all string-type values. You can represent links as element names, representation text, or any custom text. In addition, you can choose whether links are updated when element names change.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding links to model elements in string-type values.

##### New Model Editing Capabilities

We are constantly improving and expanding the model editing in Cameo Collaborator functionality, so this version of the product brings several new features as well. Now you can:

- Create and edit slot values for Instance Specifications.
- Edit bullet lists created by using document templates.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a new value for an Instance Specification.

##### Other

- Now you can organize Cameo Collaborator documents in a flexible folder-like manner using nested categories. The hierarchical grouping of categories will help you keep the repository tidy and find your documents easier. [ATTACHMENT filename='nested_categories.png']
- From this version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud user interface depends on user permissions. For example, the commenting or editing button will be shown in a document only if you have the permissions to comment or edit the document.

##### Key Issues Fixed

- Metric Tables in Cameo Collaborator documents are no longer editable.
- Now heading numbering is not displayed in Cameo Collaborator documents exported to PDF or HTML when the selected scope is Active section or Active section's presentation elements .

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation' space='MCS2021xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='TWCloud2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 4, 2021</h6><p><br /></p><p>Magic Collaboration Studio 2021x Refresh1 brings several great functionalities, like the possibility to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">index cross-resource element usage data so later you could query for resources using certain elements from the modeling tool</span>, easier management of new <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">external user permissions and assignments, and the capability to create a hierarchical category structure in the Teamwork Cloud repository.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Collaborator app introduces the commit of multiple changes which significantly reduces the number of commits when editing models, the possibility to add hyperlinks to model elements, and further improvements to model editing functionality.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="016ec660-7618-4334-b700-f40f33330ab3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021.</p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2><span>Global Element Usage Indexing and Search (Technology Preview)</span></h2><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Teamwork Cloud coupled with Elasticsearch engine brings a powerful functionality allowing you to index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of Teamwork Cloud repository and later query these usages through the modeling tool. This enables you to evaluate what impact modifying or deleting certain model elements will have on other models that are using it.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Teamwork Cloud Admin Console is now enriched with a section dedicated to configuration, initiation, and management of resource indexes. You can choose what needs to be indexed, where, and how with just a few clicks!<br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="indexing_management.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Teamwork Cloud Settings application UI allowing you to create and manage index configurations.</h6><h2>Nested Categories</h2><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The new version of Teamwork Cloud brings an ability to organize your resources in a flexible folder-like manner using nested categories. The hierarchical grouping of categories ensures that your huge repository will not turn into a mess and required resources will be found easier.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_categories_TWC.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Nested categories in Teamwork Cloud.</span></h6><h2>Managing User Permissions and Assignments</h2><p>Now all external users created in Teamwork Cloud upon successful authentication can automatically be given a certain set of permissions and assignments. Once you enable the <strong>Default user permissions template</strong> option, all newly created external users are instantly added to the dedicated Default Template user group. This way you can manage new user permissions and assignments a lot quicker because they simply inherit them from the dedicated user group.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_user_permissions_template.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A new option in the Settings application allowing you to manage new user permissions and assignments.</h6><h2>REST API Change Log</h2><p>The <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2021xR1/REST+API+Change+Log">REST API change log</a> concerns the following areas:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3655db48-2388-45de-8f9d-3e0109aae88a">Changes in data types.</li><li data-uuid="f83a9120-1f89-408b-a0b9-7949e4a765d8">A new query parameter.</li><li data-uuid="5cb1fde6-435a-48c9-aaa6-ae535d705bef">New URLs.</li></ul><h2>Cameo Collaborator</h2><h3>Commit of Multiple Changes</h3><p>This version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud brings a major improvement allowing you to commit changes accumulated while editing a model in one go. This significantly reduces the number of commits and therefore decreases the database growth when editing models.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="committing_changes.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.</h6><p><br /></p><h3>Adding Hyperlinks to Model Elements</h3><p>Now Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud supports cross-references to model elements in all string-type values. You can represent links as element names, representation text, or any custom text. In addition, you can choose whether links are updated when element names change.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlinks_to_elements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adding links to model elements in string-type values.</h6><h3><br />New Model Editing Capabilities</h3><p>We are constantly improving and expanding the model editing in Cameo Collaborator functionality, so this version of the product brings several new features as well. Now you can:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8ecc770f-8a28-4c16-a99a-2daeda4601e7">Create and edit slot values for Instance Specifications.</li><li data-uuid="3a1100c9-dfd0-4202-b194-d400965ff10f">Edit bullet lists created by using document templates.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_values_for_instances.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a new value for an Instance Specification.</h6><h3><br />Other</h3><ul><li data-uuid="8550b39d-28bf-4b63-a560-b994e71f6693"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Now you can organize Cameo Collaborator documents in a flexible folder-like manner using nested categories. The hierarchical grouping of categories will help you keep the repository tidy and find your documents easier.</span><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_categories.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="a8b6b7b2-e695-48e8-a86f-7ea8147a109e">From this version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud user interface depends on user permissions. For example, the commenting or editing button will be shown in a document only if you have the permissions to comment or edit the document.</li></ul><h3><br />Key Issues Fixed</h3><ul><li data-uuid="3a0bdc27-2be1-4c47-afb1-3be2517b132e">Metric Tables in Cameo Collaborator documents are no longer editable.</li><li data-uuid="f7830ed5-33e8-470c-818b-10ae673df00d">Now heading numbering is not displayed in Cameo Collaborator documents exported to PDF or HTML when the selected scope is <strong>Active section</strong> or <strong>Active section's presentation elements</strong>.</li></ul><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="8f641af2-32c1-4253-8da2-19855c9baae2"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2021xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2021x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="4177b499-7cf5-4b0c-bd27-53723be78001"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2021xR1" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud 2021x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623108 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00023: (2021x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `261623108`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623108/2021x+Refresh1+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:21:23.864+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

The 2021x Refresh1 release comes with Behavior to Structure synchronization, Contextual Relationships enhancements, and other improvements. Also, introduces Dymola integration allowing you to run Dymola commands, a possibility to use HTML widgets in UI mockups, a way to terminate streaming Activities by output parameter multiplicity, and new parameters for simulation via command-line. Additionally, performance in large Magic Collaboration Studio projects has been significantly improved.

Moreover, this release adds yet another set of performance, integration, general modeling, and collaboration improvements.

Collaborative modeling has been extended to assess the impact of element modification or deletion in other projects an element is used in, enable users to conveniently organize projects into the nested categories, customize the representation of Magic Collaboration Studio users in the modeling tool, and quickly return to the Merge dialog to change the merge decisions.

To download the latest version, see [Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL2022xR2/pages/127979320/Downloading+installation+files). For further information, check the product documentation.

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available

2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='NMDOC']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

#### SysML Features

##### Behavior to Structure Synchronization

You can now keep your Internal Block diagram (IBD) consistent with Activities with less effort! Our brand new behavior-to-structure synchronization allows you to validate Part Properties in an IBD to check whether Proxy Ports exist and are typed by compatible Interface Blocks based on flows found in Activities. As a result, instead of manually updating IBDs to be consistent with Activities, coherence between them is ensured by automatically detecting inconsistencies and providing the user with the solvers to handle each inconsistency individually.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing IBD based on Activities.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior to structure synchronization' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

##### Contextual Relationships Enhancements

Contextual Relationships have undergone major improvements! From now on, you can create contextual relationships directly in structure tree view tables and represent data in table columns by considering the usage context of the row elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating contextual relationships in tables.

Additionally, for a more compact Internal Block Diagram (IBD) view, you can now represent contextual relationships in the **Element Properties** compartment of the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying contextual relationships in the element compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying contextual relationships' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

##### Other Enhancements

- In earlier versions, the **Show Parts** filter was hard to use in larger projects: only a limited list of connectors was visible; and identifying the connectors was difficult in some cases. This version brings enhancements that will allow easy searching, identification, and selection of all connectors outgoing from the parts. Using this dialog, you can easily filter connectors and choose which ones should be displayed in the table or hidden from it. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Simulation

##### Dymola Integration

Magic Model Analyst 2021x Refresh1 introduces a new tool-to-tool integration with a locally installed Dymola. When integrated, a remote Dymola command interface is plugged as a new “Dymola” language and can be used anywhere expressions are allowed (OpaqueExpressions, OpaqueActions, etc.). You can also exchange variable values, trigger external Modelica model simulation, manipulate data files, export models to FMU, and run any other Dymola command.

To learn more about available built-in commands, read Dymola User Guide or type ‘help’ in the Dymola console.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dymola integration.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Integration with Dymola' space='CST2021xR1']>]]>

##### HTML Widgets in UI Mockups

The previous version of Magic Model Analyst introduced a possibility to integrate HTML widgets into diagrams for simulation. If you have enjoyed this new feature, we have good news - now you can use widgets in UI mockups to improve the visual aspect of UI simulations as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML widgets integrated in a UI mockup.

[Learn more about using widgets in UI mockups >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2021xR1/Integrating+custom+HTML+widgets#IntegratingcustomHTMLwidgets-WidgetsinUImockupsonawebUI)

##### Performance Improvements in Large Magic Collaboration Studio Projects

Model initialization, simulation performance, and memory usage in large projects have been significantly improved (up to 3-4 times).

##### Project Option for Terminating Streaming Activities

Now you can terminate the execution of an Activity with streaming output parameters by specifying the output parameter multiplicity. When the **Terminate Streaming Behaviors by Output Parameter Multiplicity** project option (or Simulation Configuration property) is set to *true*, the execution of a streaming Activity will be terminated when the cumulative number of values posted to its output parameters is equal to the parameter multiplicity upper bound.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The highlighted simulation project option allows you to terminate Activities with streaming output parameters by output parameter multiplicity.

##### New Parameters for Simulation via Command-Line

This version introduces new optional arguments for the "simulate" command. These arguments will allow you to:

- Specify input parameters with their values.
- Specify the properties file with input parameters.
- Specify the properties file defining what output parameters should be obtained after the simulation.
- Specify the properties whose values should be obtained after the simulation.
- Specify the properties file that will store output parameters with their values.

#### Client Side Performance ImprovementsPerformance Improvements

##### **numbering validation**Numbering validation

Project opening now takes less time as a result of boosting numbering validation efficiency. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the project opening speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with0.5M, 1M, 2M, and 5M elements.

##### element selectionElement selection

Further improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the **Select Element** dialog and drop-down lists) performance. The stereotyped element search is now performed approximately 20-200 times faster.

##### **import from another project**Import from another project

Projects can be imported much faster as the import operation now works more like Copy/Paste rather than Merge.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the sample project import speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with 0.5M and 1M elements.

##### **Time consumption on large server projects**Time consumption on large server projects

Report Wizard now utilizes significantly less time on large server projects using the Web Publisher 2.0 template, in comparison with the 2021x version.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart displaying the results of performance testing between SysML project 5M and 10M sizes in the whole elements scope.

##### **ignoring smart package calculation in tables and matrices**Ignoring Smart Package Calculation in Tables and Matrices

Depending on the complexity and the total number of Smart Packages within the specified scope in tables and matrices, significant performance degradation can be caused while building tables and matrices. Since the 2021x Refresh1, the inner Smart Packages are no longer calculated by defaultunless you turn on the******Include Additional Content** option.Consequently, tables and matrices are now built faster. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The contents of a Smart Package that falls within the specified scope is excluded when building a table.

##### **freezing smart package contents**Refreshing Smart Package contents on demand

You can now suspend the automatic Smart Package contents recalculation in the background to enhance performance by simply changing the Smart Package state to Frozen. As a result, elements are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents; however, the dynamic contents can still be refreshed on demand by clicking the button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Refreshing the Smart Package contents on demand.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing contents of smart package' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

Four SysML projects in 2021x that were used to run the performance tests, ranging from 0.5M to 5M in size, are equal in size to projects of earlier versions withsizes ranging from 1M to 10M.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh1 Version News' space='MD2021xR1']Back to Top

#### Integrations

##### Importing Variability Data From the ENOVIA Model Definition Application

Import variability data to your modeling tool project directly from the 3DS ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration pluginis available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ENOVIA model data' space='PLE2021xR1']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

##### Tree View in Select Element Drop-Downs and Quick Find

Quick Find and Type Selection drop-downs have been enhanced with the**Tree**view. In cases when multiple elements share the same name, it will no longer be confusing as to which one to select: all you need to do is switch to the Tree view to identify the element's place in the model hierarchy.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching between the Tree and List views to find the needed element faster.

##### Other Improvements

- The 3DEXPERIENCE menu item groups all available integrations with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. When you install plugins that support integration with the platform, all their available functionalities will be added under the 3DEXPERIENCE menu. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2021xR1']Learn more about authentication with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform >> [ATTACHMENT filename='3dexp_menu.png']
- Since the *UsageInDiagrams* Opaque Behavior is a time-consuming operation (it loads all diagrams - matrices, tables, and maps), it has been deprecated and replaced with the *UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols* Opaque Behavior that searches for symbol usages only. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Built-in operations' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

#### Key Issues Fixed

The following key issues have been fixed with this version:

- Improved performance - Smart Packages that fall within the tracking scope are no longer automatically calculated when the Suspect Links > Show Suspicions command is executed.
- The problem with Java sign-in is solved and the modeling tool now launches on macOS Big Sur without warnings.
- The project update no longer fails when the standard profile has no internal version specified.
- The modeling tool no longer becomes unresponsive when the element search is in progress.
- The diagram is no longer scaled incorrectly on mouse click/drag.
- The filtered changes are now accepted/rejected recursively when performing the merge operation.
- The information in structured expression queries is properly updated when adding projects to Teamwork Cloud.
- The element refactoring no longer fails when the Legend item is applied.
- The applied Legend item adornments are no longer removed after saving a Teamwork Cloud project locally.
- The Diagram Info box in the diagram no longer changes its position after performing the merge operation.
- Action pins no longer remain synced to Activity when the action behavior is unset.
- Associations are no longer moved when centering Classes vertically.
- The modeling tool no longer freezes when publishing large projects to Teamwork Cloud.
- When two different custom prefixes are applied for two different sets of elements, the element renumbering no longer works incorrectly for the set of elements with one custom prefix.

#### Discontinued Products

**CSV Import Plugin**support will be discontinued from the next major version. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2021xR1'] or[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2021xR1'] features will be replacing the CSV Import Plugin.

#### Collaboration

##### Global Element Usage Search

No more accidental Recovered Elements and unexpected surprises when updating Used Project versions! The latest release introduces the global element usage search functionality that allows you to query for the usages of the element you are about to modify or delete. Depending on the server-side configuration, projects using the element in question can be returned either from the entire Teamwork Cloud repository or the chosen custom indexed scope.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Searching for element usages within the Teamwork Cloud repository.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage search' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Nested Categories

Do your projects need to be grouped hierarchically? Are you seeking to organize them in a folder-like manner? We have a solution! You can now organize your projects into nested categories so you can find them later with no difficulty.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Organizing server projects into nested categories.

##### Representation of Magic Collaboration Studio Users

You now have the freedom to customize how individual Magic Collaboration Studio users are represented in the modeling tool (for example, when they lock elements) by simply changing the value of the **Representation of Users** environment option. It is your choice whether a User Name, a Full Name, or an Email address is shown to identify them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Representation of Teamwork Cloud users' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Returning to Merge Dialog

Need to make some adjustments after finishing the merge operation? Not a problem! You can now quickly return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes (e.g., rejected the element creation) by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Returning to the **Merge** dialog to change merge decisions.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Returning to Merge dialog' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Other Magic Collaboration Studio-related Improvements

- Filters in the **Merge** dialog have been redesigned to improve the user experience.
- Updating a server project with local changes in disconnected team collaboration now handles the Used Project branch deletions more effectively, with no errors.

#### Report Wizard

- Groovy Tool now supports use of relative paths, apart from Groovy file names and absolute paths, starting from the template to the Groovy file. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Groovy script tool API' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1 allows specifying DPIs of images in generated reports at three levels: global, template, and object. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting image DPI' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- Warning messages can be enabled and disabled through setting the Report Environment options in addition to editing the config.xml . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling and disabling warning messages' space='MD2021xR1']Find out more about disabling/enabling warning messages through the Report Environment options >>
- The Web Publisher 2.0 template can now show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- An increasingly effective utility of the Presentation Report Designer diagram has been ultimately designed for designing and generating PPTX and ODP templates. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Report Designer diagram' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- With $report.getMetaClass($element) , you are able to acquire a metaclass from a specific stereotype element. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report tool' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The SVG image format is more inclusively supported for text, HTML, DOCS, and DOCBOOK templates for report generation. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generate Output pane' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The Dialog tool has additionally applied the capability of hiding element icons to the Sort and Enable and Selection dialogs. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dialog tool API' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- You can now use both the relative and absolute paths of the image file on your system to display it as a homepage image by utilizing the HomeImage variable of the Web Publisher 2.0 template. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- A new creation of the loadmodule command deployed with the Generate command helps load all unloaded modules while opening MagicDraw projects. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the Generate command to generate reports' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The exported image scaling option for Web Publisher 2.0 reports has become available in Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1, so that you can reduce, maintain, and enlarge the image size percentage. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Cyber Systems Engineer Documentation' space='MCSE2021xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Systems Modeler Documentation' space='CSM2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a571d67-6c72-4e26-ba35-8174d8f06643"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: June 4, 2021</span></h6><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">The 2021x Refresh1 release comes with Behavior to Structure synchronization, Contextual Relationships enhancements, and other improvements. Also, introduces Dymola integration allowing you to run Dymola commands, a possibility to use HTML widgets in UI mockups, a way to terminate streaming Activities by output parameter multiplicity, and new parameters for simulation via command-line. Additionally, performance in large Magic Collaboration Studio projects has been significantly improved.</p><p style="text-align: left;">Moreover, this release adds yet another set of performance, integration, general modeling, and collaboration improvements. </p><p style="text-align: left;">Collaborative modeling has been extended to assess the impact of element modification or deletion in other projects an element is used in, enable users to conveniently organize projects into the nested categories, customize the representation of Magic Collaboration Studio users in the modeling tool, and quickly return to the Merge dialog to change the merge decisions.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To download the latest version, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL2022xR2/pages/127979320/Downloading+installation+files" rel="nofollow">Downloading installation files</a>. For further information, check the product documentation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f05cd43-8016-41be-9133-8cf11cb9d61d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18de0e9e-4a5a-48e6-8b7b-61da880d1f50"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[downloading its plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>SysML Features</h2><h3>Behavior to Structure Synchronization</h3><p>You can now keep your Internal Block diagram (IBD) consistent with Activities with less effort! Our brand new behavior-to-structure synchronization allows you to validate Part Properties in an IBD to check whether Proxy Ports exist and are typed by compatible Interface Blocks based on flows found in Activities. As a result, instead of manually updating IBDs to be consistent with Activities, coherence between them is ensured by automatically detecting inconsistencies and providing the user with the solvers to handle each inconsistency individually.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_sync_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing IBD based on Activities. </h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about behavior to structure synchronization >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Contextual Relationships Enhancements</h3><p>Contextual Relationships have undergone major improvements! From now on, you can create contextual relationships directly in structure tree view tables and represent data in table columns by considering the usage context of the row elements. </p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="contextual_relationships_version_news.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating contextual relationships in tables.</h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Additionally, for a more compact Internal Block Diagram (IBD) view, you can now represent contextual relationships in the <strong>Element Properties</strong> compartment of the element shape.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="contextual_relationships_in_element_properties_compartment.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Displaying contextual relationships in the element compartment. </h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Creating and displaying contextual relationships" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about creating and displaying contextual relationships >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Other Enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="e2625964-ba69-4a36-b5f2-974042d87834"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In earlier versions, the <strong>Show Parts</strong> filter was hard to use in larger projects: only a limited list of connectors was visible; and identifying the connectors was difficult in some cases. This version brings enhancements that will allow easy searching, identification, and selection of all connectors outgoing from the parts. </span>Using this dialog, you can easily filter connectors and choose which ones should be displayed in the table or hidden from it. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Whitebox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_parts.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h2>Simulation</h2><h3>Dymola Integration</h3><p>Magic Model Analyst 2021x Refresh1 introduces a new tool-to-tool integration with a locally installed Dymola. When integrated, a remote Dymola command interface is plugged as a new “Dymola” language and can be used anywhere expressions are allowed (OpaqueExpressions, OpaqueActions, etc.). You can also exchange variable values, trigger external Modelica model simulation, manipulate data files, export models to FMU, and run any other Dymola command.</p><p>To learn more about available built-in commands, read Dymola User Guide or type ‘help’ in the Dymola console.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dymola_integration.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dymola integration.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2021xR1" ri:content-title="Integration with Dymola" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Dymola integration >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><br />HTML Widgets in UI Mockups</h3><p>The previous version of Magic Model Analyst introduced a possibility to integrate HTML widgets into diagrams for simulation. If you have enjoyed this new feature, we have good news - now you can use widgets in UI mockups to improve the visual aspect of UI simulations as well.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="widgets_in_ui_mockups.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">HTML widgets integrated in a UI mockup.</h6><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2021xR1/Integrating+custom+HTML+widgets#IntegratingcustomHTMLwidgets-WidgetsinUImockupsonawebUI">Learn more about using widgets in UI mockups &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><br /></p><h3>Performance Improvements in Large Magic Collaboration Studio Projects</h3><p>Model initialization, simulation performance, and memory usage in large projects have been significantly improved (up to 3-4 times).<br /><br /></p><h3>Project Option for Terminating Streaming Activities</h3><p>Now you can terminate the execution of an Activity with streaming output parameters by specifying the output parameter multiplicity. When the <strong>Terminate Streaming Behaviors by Output Parameter Multiplicity</strong> project option (or Simulation Configuration property) is set to <em>true</em>, the execution of a streaming Activity will be terminated when the cumulative number of values posted to its output parameters is equal to the parameter multiplicity upper bound.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulation_project_option.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The highlighted simulation project option allows you to terminate Activities with streaming output parameters by output parameter multiplicity.</h6><h3>New Parameters for Simulation via Command-Line</h3><p>This version introduces new optional arguments for the &quot;simulate&quot; command. These arguments will allow you to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="db9b2798-a40e-4262-b5d7-11ac4eba2982">Specify input parameters with their values.</li><li data-uuid="6fc91007-c274-4d15-be1d-f710a41bc080">Specify the properties file with input parameters.</li><li data-uuid="ca27b232-bc6a-42c3-a01c-c671f872e7cf">Specify the properties file defining what output parameters should be obtained after the simulation.</li><li data-uuid="e563a433-ebf3-4a75-ae09-4d1558ea5aae">Specify the properties whose values should be obtained after the simulation.</li><li data-uuid="3f2d5e1c-1a5c-4811-8143-54b0b655848c">Specify the properties file that will store output parameters with their values.</li></ul><h2 style="margin-left: 0.0in;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b01e347-2a61-4e5d-8490-69abec03dc58"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Client Side Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Performance Improvements</h2><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0cb18abe-a17e-4c59-900c-5a9d1570f07f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">numbering validation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Numbering validation</h3><p>Project opening now takes less time as a result of boosting numbering validation efficiency.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="optimized_numbering_impact_on_project_opening.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the project opening speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> 0.5M, 1M, 2M, and 5M elements</span>.</h6><h3><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c06172cf-8d8f-4845-8ac3-ec77100f2885"><ac:parameter ac:name="">element selection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Element selection</span></h3><p>Further improvements have been introduced to element selection (<span>i.e.,</span> the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog and drop-down lists) performance. The stereotyped element search is now performed approximately 20-200 times faster.</p><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18498142-30dc-4ce9-a307-d56638b3ef52"><ac:parameter ac:name="">import from another project</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Import from another project</h3><p>Projects can be imported much faster as the import operation now works more like Copy/Paste rather than Merge.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_import_for_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the sample project import speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">0.5M and 1M elements</span>.</h6><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c274bab7-abbc-4b64-84cd-18807cbb9f71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Time consumption on large server projects</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Time consumption on large server projects</h3><p>Report Wizard now utilizes significantly less time on large server projects using the Web Publisher 2.0 template, in comparison with the 2021x version.<ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Whole Elements Scope(1).png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart displaying the results of performance testing between SysML project 5M and 10M sizes in the whole elements scope.</h6><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9b8243d-0327-463e-a277-e563621b89ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="">ignoring smart package calculation in tables and matrices</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Ignoring Smart Package Calculation in Tables and Matrices</h3><p>Depending on the complexity and the total number of Smart Packages within the specified scope in tables and matrices, significant performance degradation can be caused while building tables and matrices. Since the 2021x Refresh1, the inner Smart P<span>ackages are no longer calculated by default </span><span>unless you turn on the<strong> </strong></span><span><strong>Include Additional Content</strong> option.</span><span> </span>Consequently, tables and matrices are now built faster. <br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="include_additional_content.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The contents of a Smart Package that falls within the specified scope is excluded when building a table. </h6><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59909b16-3638-4baf-b499-d9cf7d41c432"><ac:parameter ac:name="">freezing smart package contents</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Refreshing Smart Package contents on demand</h3><p>You can now suspend the automatic Smart Package contents recalculation in the background to enhance performance by simply changing the Smart Package state to Frozen. As a result, elements are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents; however, the dynamic contents can still be refreshed on demand by clicking the button.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_smart_package_content.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Refreshing the Smart Package contents on demand.  </h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Managing contents of smart package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about freezing the contents of Smart Packages >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3af6515-d605-4c36-84e2-cb0f804b6a19"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Four SysML projects in 2021x <span>that were used to run the performance tests,</span> ranging from 0.5M to 5M in size, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">are equal in size to projects of earlier versions with </span>sizes ranging from 1M to 10M. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:link-body><br />Back to Top</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Integrations</h2><h3>Importing Variability Data From the ENOVIA Model Definition Application</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="082a2df7-695e-42d4-a37c-1a837295ee41"><ac:rich-text-body>This functionality is available with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA model data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Product Line Engineering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> plugins.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Import variability data to your modeling tool project directly from the 3DS ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration p</span>lugin<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> is available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin</span>)<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_model_version_variability_new.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA model data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about importing ENOVIA models >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about authenticating with 3DEXPERIENCE platform >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="margin-left: 0.0in;">Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3>Tree View in Select Element Drop-Downs and Quick Find</h3><p>Quick Find and Type Selection drop-downs have been enhanced with the<strong> Tree </strong>view. In cases when multiple elements share the same name, it will no longer be confusing as to which one to select: all you need to do is switch to the Tree view to identify the element's place in the model hierarchy. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="heating_system_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Switching between the Tree and List views to find the needed element faster.</h6><h3>Other Improvements </h3><ul><li data-uuid="4f35afdb-81c8-4009-8dce-817462fed7bf">The <strong>3DEXPERIENCE</strong> menu item groups all available integrations with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. When you install plugins that support integration with the platform, all their available functionalities will be added under the <strong>3DEXPERIENCE</strong> menu. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about authentication with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="116"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dexp_menu.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="03ea5c08-6768-4a67-abe7-ce311e8cb7ef"><p>Since the <em>UsageInDiagrams</em> Opaque Behavior is a time-consuming operation (it loads all diagrams - matrices, tables, and maps), it has been deprecated and replaced with the <em>UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols</em> Opaque Behavior that searches for symbol usages only. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Built-in operations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about built-in operations >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Key Issues Fixed</h2><p>The following key issues have been fixed with this version:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f88d0a87-342d-4b66-aebf-2953b12476ba">Improved performance - Smart Packages that fall within the tracking scope are no longer automatically calculated when the <strong>Suspect Links </strong>&gt; <strong>Show Suspicions</strong> command is executed.</li><li data-uuid="1875440a-0dab-4313-b4a1-44bb7be1ffeb">The problem with Java sign-in is solved and the modeling tool now launches on macOS Big Sur without warnings.</li><li data-uuid="75f6f7f2-708a-4579-ad54-08b03f903a70">The project update no longer fails when the standard profile has no internal version specified.</li><li data-uuid="7d44a432-8a76-4766-86fc-3439986d1393">The modeling tool no longer becomes unresponsive when the element search is in progress.</li><li data-uuid="9602fb9e-7b04-4f20-abe3-f8505c5b1f14">The diagram is no longer scaled incorrectly on mouse click/drag.</li><li data-uuid="ad14f070-232a-4008-9f1f-75e2173df32a">The filtered changes are now accepted/rejected recursively when performing the merge operation.</li><li data-uuid="beef9b29-3e5a-48e1-82c7-b5bff996ca75">The information in structured expression queries is properly updated when adding projects to Teamwork Cloud.</li><li data-uuid="853e5a79-1360-40af-b116-5934d1fcaf06">The element refactoring no longer fails when the Legend item is applied.</li><li data-uuid="8185785e-de0f-457f-98b3-56b61e827fea">The applied Legend item adornments are no longer removed after saving a Teamwork Cloud project locally.</li><li data-uuid="3bf7d67a-d50c-4a2b-a3a6-931c4927af72">The Diagram Info box in the diagram no longer changes its position after performing the merge operation.</li><li data-uuid="7fd6a729-5f4a-42b2-88cc-ed74eef6bc73">Action pins no longer remain synced to Activity when the action behavior is unset.</li><li data-uuid="f05b6f28-6af6-41cf-bb07-8ccec9488862">Associations are no longer moved when centering Classes vertically. </li><li data-uuid="523934aa-3fda-422c-83c7-5d643eba4ac9">The modeling tool no longer freezes when publishing large projects to Teamwork Cloud.</li><li data-uuid="a97dd7f4-3c99-46f0-a335-ab4e0fddfc08">When two different custom prefixes are applied for two different sets of elements, the element renumbering no longer works incorrectly for the set of elements with one custom prefix.</li></ul><h2>Discontinued Products</h2><p><strong>CSV Import Plugin </strong>support will be discontinued from the next major version. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> features <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">will be replacing the CSV Import Plugin</span>.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Collaboration</h2><h3>Global Element Usage Search</h3><p>No more accidental Recovered Elements and unexpected surprises when updating Used Project versions! The latest release introduces the global element usage search functionality that allows you to query for the usages of the element you are about to modify or delete. Depending on the server-side configuration, projects using the element in question can be returned either from the entire Teamwork Cloud repository or the chosen custom indexed scope.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_element_usage_search.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Searching for element usages within the Teamwork Cloud repository.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about global element usage search >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Nested Categories </h3><p>Do your projects need to be grouped hierarchically? Are you seeking to organize them in a folder-like manner? We have a solution! You can now organize your projects into nested categories so you can find them later with no difficulty.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_categories_manage_projects_dialog_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Organizing server projects into nested categories.</h6><h3>Representation of Magic Collaboration Studio Users</h3><p>You now have the freedom to customize how individual Magic Collaboration Studio users are represented in the modeling tool (for example, when they lock elements) by simply changing the value of the <strong>Representation of Users</strong> environment option. It is your choice whether a User Name, a Full Name, or an Email address is shown to identify them. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_users.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Representation of Teamwork Cloud users" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about representation of Teamwork Cloud users >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Returning to Merge Dialog</h3><p>Need to make some adjustments after finishing the merge operation? Not a problem! You can now quickly return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes (e.g., rejected the element creation) by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="returning_to_merge_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Returning to the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog to change merge decisions.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Returning to Merge dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about returning to Merge dialog >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Other Magic Collaboration Studio-related Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="bfeb0188-90d3-4fe5-9a1a-d7136bd0f369"><p>Filters in the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog have been redesigned to improve the user experience. </p></li><li data-uuid="8f178f83-b289-475f-afc9-206b207428b1">Updating a server project with local changes in disconnected team collaboration now handles the Used Project branch deletions more effectively, with no errors. </li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Report Wizard</h2><ul><li data-uuid="261c81f0-f442-4200-992b-7a537aa58db1"><p>Groovy Tool now supports use of relative paths, apart from Groovy file names and absolute paths, starting from the template to the Groovy file. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Groovy script tool API" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to deploy this feature >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="23c135fe-6a48-49df-8c31-16974aa8ab9a">Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1 allows specifying DPIs of images in generated reports at three levels: global, template, and object. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Setting image DPI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about this helpful function >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2d90b3f7-3034-48d3-9b4b-882425f1bdb9">Warning messages can be enabled and disabled through setting the <strong>Report Environment</strong> options in addition to editing the <strong>config.xml</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling warning messages" /><ac:link-body>Find out more about disabling/enabling warning messages through the Report Environment options &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="0ab0e26c-a5cd-4445-a228-4dc299002902">The Web Publisher 2.0 template can now show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to utilize this function >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="54692436-3f65-4a1f-a757-cf4add71d335">An increasingly effective utility of the Presentation Report Designer diagram has been ultimately designed for designing and generating PPTX and ODP templates. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Report Designer diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Discover Presentation Report Designer more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9b982c8c-a711-4ceb-ac58-5e08193f74bf">With <strong>$report.getMetaClass($element)</strong>, you are able to acquire a metaclass from a specific stereotype element. <ac:link ac:anchor="$report.getMetaClass(element)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Report tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn about this function more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="6a1a4355-d423-4478-b20c-f5b06e60da6b">The SVG image format is more inclusively supported for text, HTML, DOCS, and DOCBOOK templates for report generation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more how to achieve this capability through the user interface >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="cac10f60-b1c2-4910-a2db-e08969257ccd">The Dialog tool has additionally applied the capability of hiding element icons to the <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> and <strong>Selection</strong> dialogs. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Discover further detail of its applications >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2c912219-d563-4593-b125-2ce353d212a8">You can now use both the relative and absolute paths of the image file on your system to display it as a homepage image by utilizing the <strong>HomeImage</strong> variable of the Web Publisher 2.0 template. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Know more about how this function works >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="ce122146-7f9e-484d-a14e-80bafa2c2102">A new creation of the <strong>loadmodule</strong> command deployed with the Generate command helps load all unloaded modules while opening MagicDraw projects. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Using the Generate command to generate reports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Get familiar with loading all unloaded modules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="76dc74d5-3407-4d14-b2d7-01e89d436784">The exported image scaling option for Web Publisher 2.0 reports has become available in Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1, so that you can reduce, maintain, and enlarge the image size percentage. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Find out how to make use of this option >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="009beb0d-4c36-4570-90bc-c308c95b99d7"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCSE2021xR1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623381 space=VN version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 00024: (2021x Refresh1) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- page_id: `261623381`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623381/2021x+Refresh1+Magic+Model+Analyst+Cameo+Simulation+Toolkit
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:24:09.833+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

Version 2021x Refresh1 introduces Dymola integration, allowing you to run Dymola commands, a possibility to use HTML widgets in UI mockups, a way to terminate streaming Activities by output parameter multiplicity, and new parameters for simulation via command-line. Additionally, performance in large Teamwork Cloud projects has been significantly improved.

##### Dymola Integration

Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2021x Refresh1 introduces a new tool-to-tool integration with a locally installed Dymola. When integrated, a remote Dymola command interface is plugged as a new “Dymola” language and can be used anywhere expressions are allowed (OpaqueExpressions, OpaqueActions, etc.). You can also exchange variable values, trigger external Modelica model simulation, manipulate data files, export models to FMU, and run any other Dymola command.

To learn more about available built-in commands, read Dymola User Guide or type ‘help’ in the Dymola console.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dymola integration.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Integration with Dymola' space='CST2021xR1']>]]>

##### HTML Widgets in UI Mockups

The previous version of Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduced a possibility to integrate HTML widgets into diagrams for simulation. If you have enjoyed this new feature, we have good news - now you can use widgets in UI mockups to improve the visual aspect of UI simulations as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML widgets integrated in a UI mockup.

[Learn more about using widgets in UI mockups >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CST2021xR1/Integrating+custom+HTML+widgets#IntegratingcustomHTMLwidgets-WidgetsinUImockupsonawebUI)

##### Performance Improvements in Large Teamwork Cloud Projects

Model initialization, simulation performance, and memory usage in large projects have been significantly improved (up to 3-4 times).

##### Improved Text-Based Requirement Verification

Now Cameo Simulation Toolkit can automatically extract intervals from text-based requirements. During simulation, these intervals are converted to requirement constraints and validated. In addition, most known issues related to typical condition term recognition have been solved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Automatic interval extraction from text-based requirements.

##### Project Option for Terminating Streaming Activities

Now you can terminate the execution of an Activity with streaming output parameters by specifying the output parameter multiplicity. When the **Terminate Streaming Behaviors by Output Parameter Multiplicity** project option (or Simulation Configuration property) is set to *true*, the execution of a streaming Activity will be terminated when the cumulative number of values posted to its output parameters is equal to the parameter multiplicity upper bound.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The highlighted simulation project option allows you to terminate Activities with streaming output parameters by output parameter multiplicity.

##### New Parameters for Simulation via Command-Line

This version of Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduces new optional arguments for the "simulate" command. These arguments will allow you to:

- Specify input parameters with their values.
- Specify the properties file with input parameters.
- Specify the properties file defining what output parameters should be obtained after the simulation.
- Specify the properties whose values should be obtained after the simulation.
- Specify the properties file that will store output parameters with their values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Simulating a model from the command-line with specified input parameters and the output parameter file.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Model Analyst Documentation' space='MSI2021xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='CST2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
