<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623006 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00025: (2021x Refresh1) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- page_id: `261623006`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623006/2021x+Refresh1+Magic+Software+Architect+MagicDraw
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:22:16.162+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

The 2021x Refresh1 release adds yet another set of performance, integration, general modeling, and collaboration improvements.

Collaborative modeling has been extended to assess the impact of element modification or deletion in other projects an element is used in, enable users to conveniently organize projects into the nested categories, customize the representation of Magic Collaboration Studio users in the modeling tool, and quickly return to the Merge dialog to change the merge decisions.

To download the latest version, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574), [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information, check the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh1 Version News' space='MD2021xR1'].

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available

2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='NMDOC']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

#### Client Side Performance ImprovementsPerformance Improvements

##### **numbering validation**Numbering validation

Project opening now takes less time as a result of boosting numbering validation efficiency. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the project opening speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with0.5M, 1M, 2M, and 5M elements.

##### element selectionElement selection

Further improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the **Select Element** dialog and drop-down lists) performance. The stereotyped element search is now performed approximately 20-200 times faster.

##### **import from another project**Import from another project

Projects can be imported much faster as the import operation now works more like Copy/Paste rather than Merge.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the sample project import speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with 0.5M and 1M elements.

##### **Time consumption on large server projects**Time consumption on large server projects

Report Wizard now utilizes significantly less time on large server projects using the Web Publisher 2.0 template, in comparison with the 2021x version.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart displaying the results of performance testing between SysML project 5M and 10M sizes in the whole elements scope.

##### **ignoring smart package calculation in tables and matrices**Ignoring Smart Package Calculation in Tables and Matrices

Depending on the complexity and the total number of Smart Packages within the specified scope in tables and matrices, significant performance degradation can be caused while building tables and matrices. Since the 2021x Refresh1, the inner Smart Packages are no longer calculated by defaultunless you turn on the******Include Additional Content** option.Consequently, tables and matrices are now built faster. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The contents of a Smart Package that falls within the specified scope is excluded when building a table.

##### **freezing smart package contents**Refreshing Smart Package contents on demand

You can now suspend the automatic Smart Package contents recalculation in the background to enhance performance by simply changing the Smart Package state to Frozen. As a result, elements are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents; however, the dynamic contents can still be refreshed on demand by clicking the button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Refreshing the Smart Package contents on demand.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing contents of smart package' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

Four SysML projects in 2021x that were used to run the performance tests, ranging from 0.5M to 5M in size, are equal in size to projects of earlier versions withsizes ranging from 1M to 10M.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh1 Version News' space='MD2021xR1']Back to Top

#### Integrations

##### Importing Variability Data From the ENOVIA Model Definition Application

Importvariability datato your modeling tool project directly from the 3DS ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform.The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration pluginis available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ENOVIA model data' space='PLE2021xR1']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

#### miModeling and Infrastructure

##### Tree View in Select Element Drop-Downs and Quick Find

Quick Find and Type Selection drop-downs have been enhanced with the**Tree**view. In cases when multiple elements share the same name, it will no longer be confusing as to which one to select: all you need to do is switch to the Tree view to identify the element's place in the model hierarchy.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching between the Tree and List views to find the needed element faster.

##### Other Feature ImprovementsOther Improvements

- The **3DEXPERIENCE** menu item groups all available integrations with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. When you install plugins that support integration with the platform, all their available functionalities will be added under the **3DEXPERIENCE** menu. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Since the *UsageInDiagrams* Opaque Behavior is a time-consuming operation (it loads all diagrams - matrices, tables, and maps), it has been deprecated and replaced with the *UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols* Opaque Behavior that searches for symbol usages only.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Built-in operations' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh1 Version News' space='MD2021xR1']

#### Key Issues Fixed

The following key issues have been fixed with this version:

- Improved performance - Smart Packages that fall within the tracking scope are no longer automatically calculated when the Suspect Links > Show Suspicions command is executed.
- The problem with Java sign-in is solved and the modeling tool now launches on macOS Big Sur without warnings.
- The project update no longer fails when the standard profile has no internal version specified.
- The modeling tool no longer becomes unresponsive when the element search is in progress.
- The diagram is no longer scaled incorrectly on mouse click/drag.
- The filtered changes are now accepted/rejected recursively when performing the merge operation.
- The information in structured expression queries is properly updated when adding projects to Teamwork Cloud.
- The element refactoring no longer fails when the Legend item is applied.
- The applied Legend item adornments are no longer removed after saving a Teamwork Cloud project locally.
- The Diagram Info box in the diagram no longer changes its position after performing the merge operation.
- Action pins no longer remain synced to Activity when the action behavior is unset.
- Associations are no longer moved when centering Classes vertically.
- The modeling tool no longer freezes when publishing large projects to Teamwork Cloud.
- When two different custom prefixes are applied for two different sets of elements, the element renumbering no longer works incorrectly for the set of elements with one custom prefix.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh1 Version News' space='MD2021xR1']

#### Discontinued Products

**CSV Import Plugin**support will be discontinued from the next major version. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2021xR1'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2021xR1']or[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2021xR1'] features will be replacing the CSV Import Plugin.

#### CollaCollaboration

##### Global Element Usage Search

No more accidental Recovered Elements and unexpected surprises when updating Used Project versions! The latest release introduces the global element usage search functionality that allows you to query for the usages of the element you are about to modify or delete. Depending on the server-side configuration, projects using the element in question can be returned either from the entire Teamwork Cloud repository or the chosen custom indexed scope.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Searching for element usages within the Teamwork Cloud repository.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage search' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Nested Categories

Do your projects need to be grouped hierarchically? Are you seeking to organize them in a folder-like manner? We have a solution! You can now organize your projects into nested categories so you can find them later with no difficulty.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Organizing server projects into nested categories.

##### Representation of Teamwork Cloud Users

You now have the freedom to customize how individual Teamwork Cloud users are represented in the modeling tool (for example, when they lock elements) by simply changing the value of the **Representation of Users** environment option. It is your choice whether a User Name, a Full Name, or an Email address is shown to identify them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Representation of Teamwork Cloud users' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Returning to Merge Dialog

Need to make some adjustments after finishing the merge operation? Not a problem! You can now quickly return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes (e.g., rejected the element creation) by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Returning to the **Merge** dialog to change merge decisions.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Returning to Merge dialog' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Other TWC ImprovementsOther Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements

- Filters in the **Merge** dialog have been redesigned to improve the user experience.
- Updating a server project with local changes in disconnected team collaboration now handles the Used Project branch deletions more effectively, with no errors.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh1 Version News' space='MD2021xR1']

#### other_2Report Wizard

- Groovy Tool now supports use of relative paths, apart from Groovy file names and absolute paths, starting from the template to the Groovy file. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Groovy script tool API' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1 allows specifying DPIs of images in generated reports at three levels: global, template, and object. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting image DPI' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- Warning messages can be enabled and disabled through setting the Report Environment options in addition to editing the config.xml . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling and disabling warning messages' space='MD2021xR1']Find out more about disabling/enabling warning messages through the Report Environment options >>
- The Web Publisher 2.0 template can now show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- An increasingly effective utility of the Presentation Report Designer diagram has been ultimately designed for designing and generating PPTX and ODP templates. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Report Designer diagram' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- With $report.getMetaClass($element) , you are able to acquire a metaclass from a specific stereotype element. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report tool' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The SVG image format is more inclusively supported for text, HTML, DOCS, and DOCBOOK templates for report generation. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generate Output pane' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The Dialog tool has additionally applied the capability of hiding element icons to the Sort and Enable and Selection dialogs. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dialog tool API' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- You can now use both the relative and absolute paths of the image file on your system to display it as a homepage image by utilizing the HomeImage variable of the Web Publisher 2.0 template. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- A new creation of the loadmodule command deployed with the Generate command helps load all unloaded modules while opening MagicDraw projects. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the Generate command to generate reports' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The exported image scaling option for Web Publisher 2.0 reports has become available in Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1, so that you can reduce, maintain, and enlarge the image size percentage. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

#### Documentation

- 
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a571d67-6c72-4e26-ba35-8174d8f06643"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Released on: June 4, 2021</span></h6><p><br /></p><p>The 2021x Refresh1 release adds yet another set of performance, integration, general modeling, and collaboration improvements. </p><p>Collaborative modeling has been extended to assess the impact of element modification or deletion in other projects an element is used in, enable users to conveniently organize projects into the nested categories, customize the representation of Magic Collaboration Studio users in the modeling tool, and quickly return to the Merge dialog to change the merge decisions.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To download the latest version, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link>. Don't forget to give us your feedback on <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574">LinkedIn</a>, <a href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc">Twitter</a>, or <a href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/">Facebook</a>. For further information, check the <ac:link ac:anchor="resources"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[product documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f05cd43-8016-41be-9133-8cf11cb9d61d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18de0e9e-4a5a-48e6-8b7b-61da880d1f50"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[downloading its plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2 style="margin-left: 0.0in;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b01e347-2a61-4e5d-8490-69abec03dc58"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Client Side Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Performance Improvements</h2><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0cb18abe-a17e-4c59-900c-5a9d1570f07f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">numbering validation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Numbering validation</h3><p>Project opening now takes less time as a result of boosting numbering validation efficiency.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="optimized_numbering_impact_on_project_opening.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the project opening speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> 0.5M, 1M, 2M, and 5M elements</span>.</h6><h3><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c06172cf-8d8f-4845-8ac3-ec77100f2885"><ac:parameter ac:name="">element selection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Element selection</span></h3><p>Further improvements have been introduced to element selection (<span>i.e.,</span> the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog and drop-down lists) performance. The stereotyped element search is now performed approximately 20-200 times faster.</p><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18498142-30dc-4ce9-a307-d56638b3ef52"><ac:parameter ac:name="">import from another project</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Import from another project</h3><p>Projects can be imported much faster as the import operation now works more like Copy/Paste rather than Merge.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_import_for_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the sample project import speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">0.5M and 1M elements</span>.</h6><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c274bab7-abbc-4b64-84cd-18807cbb9f71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Time consumption on large server projects</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Time consumption on large server projects</h3><p>Report Wizard now utilizes significantly less time on large server projects using the Web Publisher 2.0 template, in comparison with the 2021x version.<ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Whole Elements Scope(1).png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart displaying the results of performance testing between SysML project 5M and 10M sizes in the whole elements scope.</h6><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9b8243d-0327-463e-a277-e563621b89ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="">ignoring smart package calculation in tables and matrices</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Ignoring Smart Package Calculation in Tables and Matrices</h3><p>Depending on the complexity and the total number of Smart Packages within the specified scope in tables and matrices, significant performance degradation can be caused while building tables and matrices. Since the 2021x Refresh1, the inner Smart P<span>ackages are no longer calculated by default </span><span>unless you turn on the<strong> </strong></span><span><strong>Include Additional Content</strong> option.</span><span> </span>Consequently, tables and matrices are now built faster. <br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="include_additional_content.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The contents of a Smart Package that falls within the specified scope is excluded when building a table. </h6><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59909b16-3638-4baf-b499-d9cf7d41c432"><ac:parameter ac:name="">freezing smart package contents</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Refreshing Smart Package contents on demand</h3><p>You can now suspend the automatic Smart Package contents recalculation in the background to enhance performance by simply changing the Smart Package state to Frozen. As a result, elements are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents; however, the dynamic contents can still be refreshed on demand by clicking the button.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_smart_package_content.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Refreshing the Smart Package contents on demand.  </h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Managing contents of smart package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about freezing the contents of Smart Packages >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3af6515-d605-4c36-84e2-cb0f804b6a19"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Four SysML projects in 2021x <span>that were used to run the performance tests,</span> ranging from 0.5M to 5M in size, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">are equal in size to projects of earlier versions with </span>sizes ranging from 1M to 10M. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:link-body><br />Back to Top</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Integrations</h2><h3>Importing Variability Data From the ENOVIA Model Definition Application</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="082a2df7-695e-42d4-a37c-1a837295ee41"><ac:rich-text-body>This functionality is available with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA model data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Product Line Engineering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> plugins.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>Import </span>variability data<span> to your modeling tool project directly from the 3DS ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. </span>The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration p</span>lugin<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> is available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin</span>)<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_model_version_variability_new.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA model data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about importing ENOVIA models >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about authenticating with 3DEXPERIENCE platform >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86c1e37a-6f77-4e5e-aa46-ea5b738c8c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mi</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3>Tree View in Select Element Drop-Downs and Quick Find</h3><p><span>Quick Find and Type Selection drop-downs have been enhanced with the<strong> Tree </strong>view. In cases when multiple elements share the same name, it will no longer be confusing as to which one to select: all you need to do is switch to the Tree view to identify the element's place in the model hierarchy. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="heating_system_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Switching between the Tree and List views to find the needed element faster.</h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c19cd83-766f-4c50-aeed-406d652bce29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Feature Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements </h3><ul><li data-uuid="ecc6d682-6736-4b35-ba3c-06c3ce0c551f"><span>The <strong>3DEXPERIENCE</strong> menu item groups all available integrations with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. When you install plugins that support integration with the platform, all their available functionalities will be added under the <strong>3DEXPERIENCE</strong> menu. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about authentication with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="116"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dexp_menu.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="091fd512-e52c-498f-b99d-5d5e0f121fb9"><p><span>Since the <em>UsageInDiagrams</em> Opaque Behavior is a time-consuming operation (it loads all diagrams - matrices, tables, and maps), it has been deprecated and replaced with the <em>UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols</em> Opaque Behavior that searches for symbol usages only. </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Built-in operations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about built-in operations >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><br /></p></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Key Issues Fixed</h2><p>The following key issues have been fixed with this version:</p><ul><li data-uuid="60e8c9ba-d77d-4b68-8c02-eb56be8dc8c9">Improved performance - Smart Packages that fall within the tracking scope are no longer automatically calculated when the <strong>Suspect Links </strong>&gt; <strong>Show Suspicions</strong> command is executed.</li><li data-uuid="80f8b7ba-a900-45d0-be59-f594cbe27c64">The problem with Java sign-in is solved and the modeling tool now launches on macOS Big Sur without warnings.</li><li data-uuid="7b144c28-48b6-4213-8bea-17eb0e5c14c7">The project update no longer fails when the standard profile has no internal version specified.</li><li data-uuid="eade9583-5b49-4840-816f-29baf284d3dc">The modeling tool no longer becomes unresponsive when the element search is in progress.</li><li data-uuid="f2da052c-849c-48cc-a65b-cf8121715e23">The diagram is no longer scaled incorrectly on mouse click/drag.</li><li data-uuid="b65e0c4e-f932-4b5c-b3bd-dcce0e321589">The filtered changes are now accepted/rejected recursively when performing the merge operation.</li><li data-uuid="bbd40d0d-fd84-48ad-975f-1db382fca327">The information in structured expression queries is properly updated when adding projects to Teamwork Cloud.</li><li data-uuid="eb7debd7-a4f0-4032-98d2-9ccb0b0a22cf">The element refactoring no longer fails when the Legend item is applied.</li><li data-uuid="de536f2e-9df8-4300-a86a-9ffc9b28748e">The applied Legend item adornments are no longer removed after saving a Teamwork Cloud project locally.</li><li data-uuid="9a3b6434-9bda-490e-91bf-7a821f4867bc">The Diagram Info box in the diagram no longer changes its position after performing the merge operation.</li><li data-uuid="1b4c6225-056b-48b8-ad74-95f5e47f0463">Action pins no longer remain synced to Activity when the action behavior is unset.</li><li data-uuid="a52104bd-51e9-4742-b719-6dbb59108306">Associations are no longer moved when centering Classes vertically. </li><li data-uuid="3a99973b-5ebb-4ff3-aaa2-b5856523b043">The modeling tool no longer freezes when publishing large projects to Teamwork Cloud.</li><li data-uuid="e9248ef5-0964-4051-8fd6-469f5b529376">When two different custom prefixes are applied for two different sets of elements, the element renumbering no longer works incorrectly for the set of elements with one custom prefix.</li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Discontinued Products</h2><p><strong>CSV Import Plugin </strong>support will be discontinued from the next major version. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>or<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> features <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">will be replacing the CSV Import Plugin</span>.</p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cdc64440-0423-40db-a24f-5a98168bd09f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Colla</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h2><h3>Global Element Usage Search</h3><p>No more accidental Recovered Elements and unexpected surprises when updating Used Project versions! The latest release introduces the global element usage search functionality that allows you to query for the usages of the element you are about to modify or delete. Depending on the server-side configuration, projects using the element in question can be returned either from the entire Teamwork Cloud repository or the chosen custom indexed scope.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_element_usage_search.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Searching for element usages within the Teamwork Cloud repository.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about global element usage search >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Nested Categories </h3><p>Do your projects need to be grouped hierarchically? Are you seeking to organize them in a folder-like manner? We have a solution! You can now organize your projects into nested categories so you can find them later with no difficulty.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_categories_manage_projects_dialog_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Organizing server projects into nested categories.</h6><h3>Representation of Teamwork Cloud Users</h3><p>You now have the freedom to customize how individual Teamwork Cloud users are represented in the modeling tool (for example, when they lock elements) by simply changing the value of the <strong>Representation of Users</strong> environment option. It is your choice whether a User Name, a Full Name, or an Email address is shown to identify them. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_users.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Representation of Teamwork Cloud users" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about representation of Teamwork Cloud users >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Returning to Merge Dialog</h3><p>Need to make some adjustments after finishing the merge operation? Not a problem! You can now quickly return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes (e.g., rejected the element creation) by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="returning_to_merge_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Returning to the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog to change merge decisions.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Returning to Merge dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about returning to Merge dialog >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75be0913-582c-4896-a96b-e24c38bb7234"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other TWC Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="4feec5fb-91d8-4c13-9fa6-f709e538914d"><p>Filters in the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog have been redesigned to improve the user experience. </p></li><li data-uuid="d1cf2839-f707-48da-8688-9491c8d47a40">Updating a server project with local changes in disconnected team collaboration now handles the Used Project branch deletions more effectively, with no errors. </li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74cc6fe7-fd0c-4ce1-a388-af5f40a7c3d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">other_2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Report Wizard</h2><ul><li data-uuid="6430d119-caa4-470a-a302-a861abf304ca"><p style="text-align: left;">Groovy Tool now supports use of relative paths, apart from Groovy file names and absolute paths, starting from the template to the Groovy file. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Groovy script tool API" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to deploy this feature >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="2197126a-67bb-4d56-9f1c-f41ef65bc50f">Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1 allows specifying DPIs of images in generated reports at three levels: global, template, and object. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Setting image DPI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about this helpful function >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="ef7078ff-243a-4bea-8802-51b56440c00e">Warning messages can be enabled and disabled through setting the <strong>Report Environment</strong> options in addition to editing the <strong>config.xml</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling warning messages" /><ac:link-body>Find out more about disabling/enabling warning messages through the Report Environment options &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="5d11f668-981f-43c9-bf3d-d7430eabdb7e">The Web Publisher 2.0 template can now show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to utilize this function >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f9e011b8-a9f7-45f2-b18a-72324a0a14c4">An increasingly effective utility of the Presentation Report Designer diagram has been ultimately /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Discover Presentation Report Designer more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="07207c04-abc8-495f-9e3b-1717cbe95753">With <strong>$report.getMetaClass($element)</strong>, you are able to acquire a metaclass from a specific stereotype element. <ac:link ac:anchor="$report.getMetaClass(element)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Report tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn about this function more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="fbec8d61-9c70-4995-af46-d1d12d439827">The SVG image format is more inclusively supported for text, HTML, DOCS, and DOCBOOK templates for report generation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more how to achieve this capability through the user interface >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="6344ebe9-9588-44a7-92d9-9fe78931a24d">The Dialog tool has additionally applied the capability of hiding element icons to further detail of its applications >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="c079f1dc-09a3-47b4-a9f7-1673dd6e1159">You can now use both the relative and absolute paths of the image Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1, so that you can reduce, maintain, and enlarge the image size percentage. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623050 space=VN version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 00026: (2021x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `261623050`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623050/2021x+Refresh1+Magic+Systems+of+Systems+Architect+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:20:41.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

The 2021x Refresh1 release introduces further performance and scalability upgrades in key product areas: compatibility modes, report generation, metrics calculation, integration, general modeling, and collaboration improvements. Furthermore, new tables and usability improvements are here to make your modeling experience better. Additionally, this version introduces a technology preview of ArchiMate model import.

This release also comes with Behavior to Structure synchronization, Contextual Relationships enhancements, and other improvements.

Collaborative modeling has been extended to assess the impact of element modification or deletion in other projects an element is used in, enable users to conveniently organize projects into the nested categories, customize the representation of Magic Collaboration Studio users in the modeling tool, and quickly return to the Merge dialog to change the merge decisions.

To download the latest version, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574), [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information, check the product documentation.

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available

2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='NMDOC']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

#### UAF Features

##### msPerformance Improvements in UAF

In this version, we continued improving the performance with a major focus on compliance mode, reports, and UAF metrics.

**Compliance Modes**

Turning on the BPMN or SysML compliance modes in large projects now takes less time than in earlier versions.

| Feature | 2021x | 2021x Refresh 1 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN Compliance Mode (UAF) | > 60 min | 9 min 27s |
| SysML Compliance Mode (UPDM) | > 60 min | 3 min |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Report**

The report generation was optimized so it takes less time for large projects. In the optimization process, the tables, matrices, and relation maps were excluded from the scope of the Used in Diagrams column. MS Word supports files only up to 512 MB, therefore we do not recommend generating a report with the entire project as its scope.

The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.

| Feature | 2021x Refresh 1 |
| --- | --- |
| All views report (XL project) | 5 min 30s |
| Dictionary report | 31s |
| Summary and Overview report | 15s |
| Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet | 2 min 11s |

**UAF Metrics**

The metrics calculation was also optimized and runs smoothly in the larger projects. However, we do not recommend selecting the entire project as a scope for calculating metrics.

The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.

| Feature | 2021x | 2021x Refresh 1 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| UAF metrics (1M) | Low memory | 21.719 s |
| UAF metrics (10M) | Low memory | 190.35 s |
| UPDM2 metrics (1M) | Low memory | 42.564 s |
| UPDM2 metrics (10M) | Low memory | 56.109 s |

##### Diagrams and Tables

- The Hierarchical Typical Measurements tables were enhanced and no longer hold the status of technology preview. New hierarchical display modes were implemented. You can also generate predefined reports for these tables. Also, having the Hierarchical Typical Measurements table filled out makes it easy to create the Hierarchical Actual Measurements table by simply clicking the Evaluate Measurements button. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAFP2021xR1']>]]>
- The **Hierarchical Actual Measurements** tables are available for all domains. The hierarchical table for actual measurements allows working with the actual measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing Actual Property Sets and define values . When you add a Property Set to the table, all existing Actual Property Sets will be collected recursively according to the defined scope. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAFP2021xR1']>]]>
- The Actual Project Milestone Summary Table (Pj-Rm) depicts all actual project milestones in the model. Adding the actual project milestones into the table allows you to see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table' space='UAFP2021xR1']>]]>
- The new **Strategic Typical Deployment** tables are now available in all frameworks. Two new wizards come along with this new type of table. Use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAFP2021xR1']**to create a table and the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAFP2021xR1']** to add resources to the table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAFP2021xR1']Learn more >> [ATTACHMENT filename='typical_resources_deployment.png']

##### Usability Improvements

- If you need to see the original name, set the Show Original Name of Measurement symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurements' space='UAFP2021xR1']Learn more >>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurements' space='UAFP2021xR1'][IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can create a Model Library package for each UAF domain.
- A new system property is implemented to help with the migration of server projects. There are two cases regarding the migration of **UAF and UPDM** projects:
  - If you are performing UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UPDM environment, only the project version will be migrated.
  - The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the magicdraw.properties (or cea.properties ) file and change the value of the system property -[Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to](http://Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to).uaf\=false to True. By default, the value of this property is False, which means that your UPDM projects will not be migrated. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migrating projects' space='TWCloud2021xR1']>]]>

##### ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)

In this version, the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project is implemented. As it is only a technology preview for this version, the imported element mapping is only partial. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing from ArchiMate' space='UAFP2021xR1']>>]]>

#### SysML Features

##### Behavior to Structure Synchronization

You can now keep your Internal Block diagram (IBD) consistent with Activities with less effort! Our brand new behavior-to-structure synchronization allows you to validate Part Properties in an IBD to check whether Proxy Ports exist and are typed by compatible Interface Blocks based on flows found in Activities. As a result, instead of manually updating IBDs to be consistent with Activities, coherence between them is ensured by automatically detecting inconsistencies and providing the user with the solvers to handle each inconsistency individually.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing IBD based on Activities.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior to structure synchronization' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

##### Contextual Relationships Enhancements

Contextual Relationships have undergone major improvements! From now on, you can create contextual relationships directly in structure tree view tables and represent data in table columns by considering the usage context of the row elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating contextual relationships in tables.

Additionally, for a more compact Internal Block Diagram (IBD) view, you can now represent contextual relationships in the **Element Properties** compartment of the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying contextual relationships in the element compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying contextual relationships' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

##### Other enhancements

- In earlier versions, the **Show Parts** filter in the ICD Whitebox tables was hard to use in larger projects: only a limited list of connectors was visible; and identifying the connectors was difficult in some cases. This version brings enhancements that will allow easy searching, identification, and selection of all connectors outgoing from the parts. Using this dialog, you can easily filter connectors and choose which ones should be displayed in the table or hidden from it. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Performance Improvements

- **numbering validationNumbering validation**. Project opening now takes less time as a result of boosting numbering validation efficiency. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the project opening speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with 0.5M, 1M, 2M, and 5M elements.

- element selection Element selection . Further improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the Select Element dialog and drop-down lists) performance. The stereotyped element search is now performed approximately 20-200 times faster.

- **import from another projectImport from another project**. Projects can be imported much faster as the import operation now works more like Copy/Paste rather than Merge. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the sample project import speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with 0.5M and 1M elements.

- **Time consumption on large server projectsTime consumption on large server projects**. Report Wizard now utilizes significantly less time on large server projects using the Web Publisher 2.0 template, in comparison with the 2021x version.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] A chart displaying the results of performance testing between SysML project 5M and 10M sizes in the whole elements scope.
- **ignoring smart package calculation in tables and matricesIgnoring Smart Package Calculation in Tables and Matrices**. Depending on the complexity and the total number of Smart Packages within the specified scope in tables and matrices, significant performance degradation can be caused while building tables and matrices. Since the 2021x Refresh1, the inner Smart Packages are no longer calculated by default unless you turn on the******Include Additional Content** option. Consequently, tables and matrices are now built faster. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The contents of a Smart Package that falls within the specified scope is excluded when building a table.
- **freezing smart package contentsRefreshing Smart Package contents on demand**. You can now suspend the automatic Smart Package contents recalculation in the background to enhance performance by simply changing the Smart Package state to Frozen. As a result, elements are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents; however, the dynamic contents can still be refreshed on demand by clicking the button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Refreshing the Smart Package contents on demand.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing contents of smart package' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

Four SysML projects in 2021x that were used to run the performance tests, ranging from 0.5M to 5M in size, are equal in size to projects of earlier versions withsizes ranging from 1M to 10M.

#### Integrations

##### Importing Variability Data From the ENOVIA Model Definition Application

Import variability data to your modeling tool project directly from the 3DS ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration pluginis available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ENOVIA model data' space='PLE2021xR1']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

[Back to top](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CEA2021x/2021x+Version+News#id-2021xVersionNews-top)

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

##### Tree View in Select Element Drop-Downs and Quick Find

Quick Find and Type Selection drop-downs have been enhanced with the**Tree**view. In cases when multiple elements share the same name, it will no longer be confusing as to which one to select: all you need to do is switch to the Tree view to identify the element's place in the model hierarchy.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching between the Tree and List views to find the needed element faster.

##### Other Feature ImprovementsOther improvements

- The 3DEXPERIENCE menu item groups all available integrations with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. When you install plugins that support integration with the platform, all their available functionalities will be added under the 3DEXPERIENCE menu. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2021xR1']Learn more about authentication with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform >> [ATTACHMENT filename='3dexp_menu.png']
- Since the *UsageInDiagrams* Opaque Behavior is a time-consuming operation (it loads all diagrams - matrices, tables, and maps), it has been deprecated and replaced with the *UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols* Opaque Behavior that searches for symbol usages only. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Built-in operations' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

#### Key Issues Fixed

The following key issues have been fixed with this version:

- Improved performance - Smart Packages that fall within the tracking scope are no longer automatically calculated when the Suspect Links > Show Suspicions command is executed.
- The problem with Java sign-in is solved and the modeling tool now launches on macOS Big Sur without warnings.
- The project update no longer fails when the standard profile has no internal version specified.
- The modeling tool no longer becomes unresponsive when the element search is in progress.
- The diagram is no longer scaled incorrectly on mouse click/drag.
- The filtered changes are now accepted/rejected recursively when performing the merge operation.
- The information in structured expression queries is properly updated when adding projects to Teamwork Cloud.
- The element refactoring no longer fails when the Legend item is applied.
- The applied Legend item adornments are no longer removed after saving a Teamwork Cloud project locally.
- The Diagram Info box in the diagram no longer changes its position after performing the merge operation.
- Action pins no longer remain synced to Activity when the action behavior is unset.
- Associations are no longer moved when centering Classes vertically.
- The modeling tool no longer freezes when publishing large projects to Teamwork Cloud.
- When two different custom prefixes are applied for two different sets of elements, the element renumbering no longer works incorrectly for the set of elements with one custom prefix.

#### Discontinued Products

**CSV Import Plugin**support will be discontinued from the next major version. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2021xR1'] or[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2021xR1'] features will be replacing the CSV Import Plugin.

#### Collaboration

##### Global Element Usage Search

No more accidental Recovered Elements and unexpected surprises when updating Used Project versions! The latest release introduces the global element usage search functionality that allows you to query for the usages of the element you are about to modify or delete. Depending on the server-side configuration, projects using the element in question can be returned either from the entire Teamwork Cloud repository or the chosen custom indexed scope.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Searching for element usages within the Teamwork Cloud repository.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage search' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Nested Categories

Do your projects need to be grouped hierarchically? Are you seeking to organize them in a folder-like manner? We have a solution! You can now organize your projects into nested categories so you can find them later with no difficulty.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Organizing server projects into nested categories.

##### Representation of Teamwork Cloud Users

You now have the freedom to customize how individual Teamwork Cloud users are represented in the modeling tool (for example, when they lock elements) by simply changing the value of the **Representation of Users** environment option. It is your choice whether a User Name, a Full Name, or an Email address is shown to identify them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Representation of Teamwork Cloud users' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Returning to Merge Dialog

Need to make some adjustments after finishing the merge operation? Not a problem! You can now quickly return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes (e.g., rejected the element creation) by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Returning to the **Merge** dialog to change merge decisions.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Returning to Merge dialog' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Other TWC ImprovementsOther Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements

- Filters in the **Merge** dialog have been redesigned to improve the user experience.
- Updating a server project with local changes in disconnected team collaboration now handles the Used Project branch deletions more effectively, with no errors.

###### Report Wizard

- Groovy Tool now supports use of relative paths, apart from Groovy file names and absolute paths, starting from the template to the Groovy file. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Groovy script tool API' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1 allows specifying DPIs of images in generated reports at three levels: global, template, and object. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting image DPI' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- Warning messages can be enabled and disabled through setting the Report Environment options in addition to editing the config.xml . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling and disabling warning messages' space='MD2021xR1']Find out more about disabling/enabling warning messages through the Report Environment options >>
- The Web Publisher 2.0 template can now show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- An increasingly effective utility of the Presentation Report Designer diagram has been ultimately designed for designing and generating PPTX and ODP templates. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Report Designer diagram' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- With $report.getMetaClass($element) , you are able to acquire a metaclass from a specific stereotype element. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report tool' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The SVG image format is more inclusively supported for text, HTML, DOCS, and DOCBOOK templates for report generation. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generate Output pane' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The Dialog tool has additionally applied the capability of hiding element icons to the Sort and Enable and Selection dialogs. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dialog tool API' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- You can now use both the relative and absolute paths of the image file on your system to display it as a homepage image by utilizing the HomeImage variable of the Web Publisher 2.0 template. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- A new creation of the loadmodule command deployed with the Generate command helps load all unloaded modules while opening MagicDraw projects. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the Generate command to generate reports' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>
- The exported image scaling option for Web Publisher 2.0 reports has become available in Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1, so that you can reduce, maintain, and enlarge the image size percentage. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation' space='MSOSA2021xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation' space='CEA2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
