Additionally, this version introduces a technology preview of ArchiMate model import.</p><p>This release also comes with Behavior to Structure synchronization, Contextual Relationships enhancements, and other improvements. </p><p>Collaborative modeling has been extended to assess the impact of element modification or deletion in other projects an element is used in, enable users to conveniently organize projects into the nested categories, customize the representation of Magic Collaboration Studio users in the modeling tool, and quickly return to the Merge dialog to change the merge decisions.</p><p>To download the latest version, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link>. Don't forget to give us your feedback on <a class="external-link" href="https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574" rel="nofollow">LinkedIn</a>, <a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc" rel="nofollow">Twitter</a>, or <a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/" rel="nofollow">Facebook</a>. For further information, check the product documentation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f05cd43-8016-41be-9133-8cf11cb9d61d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 2 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18de0e9e-4a5a-48e6-8b7b-61da880d1f50"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh1 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[downloading its plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>UAF Features</h2><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="560bb9ac-56c6-4acc-aac0-510dfb495e71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">ms</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Performance Improvements in UAF</h3><p>In this version, we continued improving the performance with a major focus on compliance mode, reports, and UAF metrics.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Compliance Modes</strong></p><p>Turning on the BPMN or SysML compliance modes in large projects now takes less time than in earlier versions. </p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th><p>Feature</p></th><th><p>2021x</p></th><th><p>2021x Refresh 1</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>BPMN Compliance Mode (UAF)</td><td>&gt; 60 min</td><td>9 min 27s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>SysML Compliance Mode (UPDM)</td><td>&gt; 60 min</td><td>3 min</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compliance_mode.png" /></ac:image></p><p><strong>Report</strong></p><p>The report generation was optimized so it takes less time for large projects. In the optimization process, the tables, matrices, and relation maps were excluded from the scope of the Used in Diagrams column. MS Word supports files only up to 512 MB, therefore we do not recommend generating a report with the entire project as its scope.</p><p>The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 407.0px;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 0.0px;" /><col class="" style="width: 0.0px;" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th><p>Feature</p></th><th><p>2021x Refresh 1</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>All views report (XL project)</td><td>5 min 30s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Dictionary report</td><td>31s</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Summary and  Overview report</td><td colspan="1">15s </td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet </td><td colspan="1">2 min 11s</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>UAF Metrics</strong></p><p>The metrics calculation was also optimized and runs smoothly in the larger projects. However, we do not recommend selecting the entire project as a scope for calculating metrics.</p><p>The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th><p>Feature</p></th><th><p>2021x</p></th><th><p>2021x Refresh 1</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>UAF metrics (1M)</td><td>Low memory</td><td>21.719 s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>UAF metrics (10M)</td><td>Low memory</td><td>190.35 s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>UPDM2 metrics (1M)</td><td>Low memory</td><td>42.564 s</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">UPDM2 metrics (10M)</td><td colspan="1">Low memory</td><td colspan="1">56.109 s</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><br />Diagrams and Tables</h3><ul><li data-uuid="491b6259-72e5-46fb-ba73-4acd00c2cc22">The <strong>Hierarchical Typical Measurements</strong> tables were enhanced and no longer hold the status of technology preview. New hierarchical display modes were implemented. You can also generate predefined reports for these tables. Also, having the Hierarchical Typical Measurements table filled out makes it easy to create the Hierarchical Actual Measurements table by simply clicking the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button.  <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="807a8f5a-6bcc-409c-a3f2-b1502faf4dda"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Hierarchical Actual Measurements</strong> tables are available for all domains. The hierarchical table for actual measurements allows working with the actual measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing Actual Property Sets and </span>define values<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. When you add a Property Set to the table, all existing Actual Property Sets will be collected recursively according to the defined scope. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="b65a171a-34d7-445a-893b-01a8c76f1274">The<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Actual</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Project</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Milestone Summary</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Table (Pj-Rm)</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> depicts all actual project milestones in the model. Adding the actual project milestones into the table allows you to see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="29f46397-52e8-400b-9aa6-e1c107d64c86"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The new <strong>Strategic Typical Deployment</strong> tables are now available in all frameworks. Two new wizards come along with this new type of table. Use the  <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link> </strong>to create a table and the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> to add resources to the table. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram" /><ac:link-body>Learn more &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Usability Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="0bb6b86c-1a7e-4c54-8569-991d3dbc4fad"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you need to see the original name, set the </span><strong>Show Original Name of Measurement</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Measurements" /><ac:link-body>Learn more &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Measurements" /><ac:link-body><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="original_measurement_name_VN.png" /></ac:image></ac:link-body></ac:link></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="4d4f3299-0ce8-4b89-b318-311ea0ad9657"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can create a Model Library package for each UAF domain.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="61f94fdc-86e3-440b-8db1-91d5e6428150"><p>A new system property is implemented to help with the migration of server projects. There are two cases regarding the migration of <strong>UAF and UPDM</strong> projects:</p><ul><li>If you are performing UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UPDM environment, only the project version will be migrated.</li><li>The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the <em>magicdraw.properties</em> (or <em>cea.properties</em>) file and change the value of the system property <em>-<a href="http://Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to">Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to</a>.uaf\=false</em> to True. By default, the value of this property is False, which means that your UPDM projects will not be migrated.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2021xR1" ri:content-title="Migrating projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><h3>ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)</h3><p>In this version, the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project is implemented. As it is only a technology preview for this version, the imported element mapping is only partial. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NATO-Architecture-Framework-horizontal-white-background.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>SysML Features</h2><h3>Behavior to Structure Synchronization</h3><p>You can now keep your Internal Block diagram (IBD) consistent with Activities with less effort! Our brand new behavior-to-structure synchronization allows you to validate Part Properties in an IBD to check whether Proxy Ports exist and are typed by compatible Interface Blocks based on flows found in Activities. As a result, instead of manually updating IBDs to be consistent with Activities, coherence between them is ensured by automatically detecting inconsistencies and providing the user with the solvers to handle each inconsistency individually.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_sync_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing IBD based on Activities. </h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about behavior to structure synchronization >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Contextual Relationships Enhancements</h3><p>Contextual Relationships have undergone major improvements! From now on, you can create contextual relationships directly in structure tree view tables and represent data in table columns by considering the usage context of the row elements. </p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="contextual_relationships_version_news.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating contextual relationships in tables.</h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Additionally, for a more compact Internal Block Diagram (IBD) view, you can now represent contextual relationships in the <strong>Element Properties</strong> compartment of the element shape.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="contextual_relationships_in_element_properties_compartment.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Displaying contextual relationships in the element compartment. </h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Creating and displaying contextual relationships" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about creating and displaying contextual relationships >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Other enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="5bd68b01-5fab-496f-b30d-50df857c2198"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In earlier versions, the <strong>Show Parts</strong> filter in the ICD Whitebox tables was hard to use in larger projects: only a limited list of connectors was visible; and identifying the connectors was difficult in some cases. This version brings enhancements that will allow easy searching, identification, and selection of all connectors outgoing from the parts. </span>Using this dialog, you can easily filter connectors and choose which ones should be displayed in the table or hidden from it. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Whitebox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_parts.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="margin-left: 0.0in;">Performance Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="628b1ee2-42e3-4248-8bf2-0eb81c126e89"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0cb18abe-a17e-4c59-900c-5a9d1570f07f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">numbering validation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Numbering validation</strong>. Project opening now takes less time as a result of boosting numbering validation efficiency.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="optimized_numbering_impact_on_project_opening.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the project opening speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with 0.5M, 1M, 2M, and 5M elements.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="a4e79c77-dbb1-4c8d-8006-d03d436572f0"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c06172cf-8d8f-4845-8ac3-ec77100f2885"><ac:parameter ac:name="">element selection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Element selection</strong>. Further improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog and drop-down lists) performance. The stereotyped element search is now performed approximately 20-200 times faster.</li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="0c2c9f6d-7d59-439b-b436-374a07e4925d"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18498142-30dc-4ce9-a307-d56638b3ef52"><ac:parameter ac:name="">import from another project</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Import from another project</strong>. Projects can be imported much faster as the import operation now works more like Copy/Paste rather than Merge.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_import_for_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the sample project import speed in 2021x and 2021x Refresh1 SysML projects with 0.5M and 1M elements.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="b27218fa-c486-4c7c-b6a9-74b6c27d1d5b"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c274bab7-abbc-4b64-84cd-18807cbb9f71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Time consumption on large server projects</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Time consumption on large server projects</strong>. Report Wizard now utilizes significantly less time on large server projects using the Web Publisher 2.0 template, in comparison with the 2021x version.<ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Whole Elements Scope(1).png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart displaying the results of performance testing between SysML project 5M and 10M sizes in the whole elements scope.</h6></li><li data-uuid="97dd129d-ce31-417a-92ef-bee53ac13343"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9b8243d-0327-463e-a277-e563621b89ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="">ignoring smart package calculation in tables and matrices</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Ignoring Smart Package Calculation in Tables and Matrices</strong>. Depending on the complexity and the total number of Smart Packages within the specified scope in tables and matrices, significant performance degradation can be caused while building tables and matrices. Since the 2021x Refresh1, the inner Smart Packages are no longer calculated by default unless you turn on the<strong> </strong><strong>Include Additional Content</strong> option. Consequently, tables and matrices are now built faster. <br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="include_additional_content.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The contents of a Smart Package that falls within the specified scope is excluded when building a table. </h6></li><li data-uuid="613f402f-53b7-4acb-9d52-6495347d2323"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59909b16-3638-4baf-b499-d9cf7d41c432"><ac:parameter ac:name="">freezing smart package contents</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Refreshing Smart Package contents on demand</strong>. You can now suspend the automatic Smart Package contents recalculation in the background to enhance performance by simply changing the Smart Package state to Frozen. As a result, elements are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents; however, the dynamic contents can still be refreshed on demand by clicking the button.</p></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_smart_package_content.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Refreshing the Smart Package contents on demand.  </h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Managing contents of smart package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about freezing the contents of Smart Packages >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3af6515-d605-4c36-84e2-cb0f804b6a19"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Four SysML projects in 2021x that were used to run the performance tests, ranging from 0.5M to 5M in size, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">are equal in size to projects of earlier versions with </span>sizes ranging from 1M to 10M. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Integrations</h2><h3>Importing Variability Data From the ENOVIA Model Definition Application</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="082a2df7-695e-42d4-a37c-1a837295ee41"><ac:rich-text-body>This functionality is available with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA model data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Product Line Engineering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> plugins.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Import variability data to your modeling tool project directly from the 3DS ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration p</span>lugin<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> is available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin</span>)<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_model_version_variability_new.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA model data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about importing ENOVIA models >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about authenticating with 3DEXPERIENCE platform >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: right;"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CEA2021x/2021x+Version+News#id-2021xVersionNews-top">Back to top</a></p><h2>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3>Tree View in Select Element Drop-Downs and Quick Find</h3><p>Quick Find and Type Selection drop-downs have been enhanced with the<strong> Tree </strong>view. In cases when multiple elements share the same name, it will no longer be confusing as to which one to select: all you need to do is switch to the Tree view to identify the element's place in the model hierarchy. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="heating_system_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Switching between the Tree and List views to find the needed element faster.</h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c19cd83-766f-4c50-aeed-406d652bce29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Feature Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other improvements </h3><ul><li data-uuid="cc2a8367-b882-428b-aaff-7a1f14898405">The <strong>3DEXPERIENCE</strong> menu item groups all available integrations with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. When you install plugins that support integration with the platform, all their available functionalities will be added under the <strong>3DEXPERIENCE</strong> menu. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about authentication with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="116"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dexp_menu.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="b40017c4-a007-42e8-af77-1be26e8f8c0a"><p>Since the <em>UsageInDiagrams</em> Opaque Behavior is a time-consuming operation (it loads all diagrams - matrices, tables, and maps), it has been deprecated and replaced with the <em>UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols</em> Opaque Behavior that searches for symbol usages only. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Built-in operations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about built-in operations >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ul><h2>Key Issues Fixed</h2><p>The following key issues have been fixed with this version:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2ee45d56-df29-47b7-a9cf-6a44524c34ad">Improved performance - Smart Packages that fall within the tracking scope are no longer automatically calculated when the <strong>Suspect Links </strong>&gt; <strong>Show Suspicions</strong> command is executed.</li><li data-uuid="b029eaf1-7ef0-4b63-993d-8acefd25e550">The problem with Java sign-in is solved and the modeling tool now launches on macOS Big Sur without warnings.</li><li data-uuid="17d58247-cd64-4310-8b69-8161faf3eac7">The project update no longer fails when the standard profile has no internal version specified.</li><li data-uuid="412a059d-dfb4-4e58-b91c-797c4e27ab53">The modeling tool no longer becomes unresponsive when the element search is in progress.</li><li data-uuid="409987e3-e3a6-4e98-8e97-c5d0cf7f284f">The diagram is no longer scaled incorrectly on mouse click/drag.</li><li data-uuid="fac412a8-1c37-47ac-8500-3429dc66ab38">The filtered changes are now accepted/rejected recursively when performing the merge operation.</li><li data-uuid="f0c0421b-54d1-4504-94c2-e2b7fd201d7f">The information in structured expression queries is properly updated when adding projects to Teamwork Cloud.</li><li data-uuid="6f38966d-bb13-4fa9-a76e-7cb48761246c">The element refactoring no longer fails when the Legend item is applied.</li><li data-uuid="0003ba45-05ba-479d-90a2-f057a9fb1d5b">The applied Legend item adornments are no longer removed after saving a Teamwork Cloud project locally.</li><li data-uuid="5f27e3ac-2698-47ff-bf0d-b1b831d730ac">The Diagram Info box in the diagram no longer changes its position after performing the merge operation.</li><li data-uuid="fe88fb6e-4d6b-4a7c-96ec-a91543072953">Action pins no longer remain synced to Activity when the action behavior is unset.</li><li data-uuid="a8b05b10-c3e2-4be4-951a-1d3919ceaa5f">Associations are no longer moved when centering Classes vertically. </li><li data-uuid="2e3d7d10-734d-442f-b920-ed6c57a7581a">The modeling tool no longer freezes when publishing large projects to Teamwork Cloud.</li><li data-uuid="cf5a52b3-2a78-40ad-81bb-77c6132925e7">When two different custom prefixes are applied for two different sets of elements, the element renumbering no longer works incorrectly for the set of elements with one custom prefix.</li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Discontinued Products</h2><p><strong>CSV Import Plugin </strong>support will be discontinued from the next major version. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> features <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">will be replacing the CSV Import Plugin</span>.</p><h2>Collaboration</h2><h3>Global Element Usage Search</h3><p>No more accidental Recovered Elements and unexpected surprises when updating Used Project versions! The latest release introduces the global element usage search functionality that allows you to query for the usages of the element you are about to modify or delete. Depending on the server-side configuration, projects using the element in question can be returned either from the entire Teamwork Cloud repository or the chosen custom indexed scope.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_element_usage_search.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Searching for element usages within the Teamwork Cloud repository.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about global element usage search >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Nested Categories </h3><p>Do your projects need to be grouped hierarchically? Are you seeking to organize them in a folder-like manner? We have a solution! You can now organize your projects into nested categories so you can find them later with no difficulty.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_categories_manage_projects_dialog_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Organizing server projects into nested categories.</h6><h3>Representation of Teamwork Cloud Users</h3><p>You now have the freedom to customize how individual Teamwork Cloud users are represented in the modeling tool (for example, when they lock elements) by simply changing the value of the <strong>Representation of Users</strong> environment option. It is your choice whether a User Name, a Full Name, or an Email address is shown to identify them. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_users.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Representation of Teamwork Cloud users" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about representation of Teamwork Cloud users >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Returning to Merge Dialog</h3><p>Need to make some adjustments after finishing the merge operation? Not a problem! You can now quickly return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes (e.g., rejected the element creation) by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="returning_to_merge_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Returning to the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog to change merge decisions.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Returning to Merge dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about returning to Merge dialog >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75be0913-582c-4896-a96b-e24c38bb7234"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other TWC Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="7669c6fe-d219-4a0f-b3e7-5becdff0d388"><p>Filters in the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog have been redesigned to improve the user experience. </p></li><li data-uuid="e05dcaff-fe95-462a-9b11-869ee35ac2af">Updating a server project with local changes in disconnected team collaboration now handles the Used Project branch deletions more effectively, with no errors. </li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h5>Report Wizard</h5><ul><li data-uuid="1833ab6b-b1e8-4fec-be94-840b4eaee476"><p>Groovy Tool now supports use of relative paths, apart from Groovy file names and absolute paths, starting from the template to the Groovy file. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Groovy script tool API" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to deploy this feature >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="5be69b89-1f2f-4aaa-904b-a2288ac2797c">Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1 allows specifying DPIs of images in generated reports at three levels: global, template, and object. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Setting image DPI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about this helpful function >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="cb8a3c4d-d28d-447c-a512-a67aac83200d">Warning messages can be enabled and disabled through setting the <strong>Report Environment</strong> options in addition to editing the <strong>config.xml</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling warning messages" /><ac:link-body>Find out more about disabling/enabling warning messages through the Report Environment options &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d6f5d36e-55c4-461e-b4df-516d4dfa2e96">The Web Publisher 2.0 template can now show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to utilize this function >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="0f016b38-f9d3-4084-8652-0b31dd71d39b">An increasingly effective utility of the Presentation Report Designer diagram has been ultimately designed for designing and generating PPTX and ODP templates. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Report Designer diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Discover Presentation Report Designer more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="4775033f-3c6e-423b-aefe-0b63d560b041">With <strong>$report.getMetaClass($element)</strong>, you are able to acquire a metaclass from a specific stereotype element. <ac:link ac:anchor="$report.getMetaClass(element)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Report tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn about this function more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="3d7c921a-dd7f-428b-9fb3-2ec59ac69f14">The SVG image format is more inclusively supported for text, HTML, DOCS, and DOCBOOK templates for report generation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more how to achieve this capability through the user interface >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="8ad7f91a-36a5-4109-b08e-ef921e83f0c1">The Dialog tool has additionally applied the capability of hiding element icons to the <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> and <strong>Selection</strong> dialogs. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Discover further detail of its applications >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f40e6fa7-bfd1-4fe0-9e8a-2717352543a5">You can now use both the relative and absolute paths of the image file on your system to display it as a homepage image by utilizing the <strong>HomeImage</strong> variable of the Web Publisher 2.0 template. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Know more about how this function works >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="a6de10b2-7212-48e0-9332-ff466738485b">A new creation of the <strong>loadmodule</strong> command deployed with the Generate command helps load all unloaded modules while opening MagicDraw projects. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Using the Generate command to generate reports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Get familiar with loading all unloaded modules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9356ab5d-589c-4375-b90a-aebea3941446">The exported image scaling option for Web Publisher 2.0 reports has become available in Report Wizard 2021x Refresh1, so that you can reduce, maintain, and enlarge the image size percentage. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Find out how to make use of this option >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="147f9b0e-3a9a-4b5c-a534-0e6d6ef40915"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSOSA2021xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Systems of Systems Architect 2021x refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="85001443-ad02-486d-af4c-7f175a78c7e2"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CEA2021xR1" ri:content-title="Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2021x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623424 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00027: (2021x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering

- page_id: `261623424`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623424/2021x+Refresh1+Product+Line+Engineering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T11:59:03.547+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

This release introduces the Variability Data import from the ENOVIA Model Definition application, the Replace and Rewire variation point, new compatibility matrices, and the Feature Impact relation map.

To download the latest MagicDraw version, see [Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2022xR2/Downloading+installation+files). Don't forget to give us your feedback on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574), [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information, check the [product documentation](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/PLE2021xR1/overview).

##### Importing Variability Data From ENOVIA Model Definition Application

Import variability data to your modeling tool project directly from the ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration pluginis available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ENOVIA model data' space='PLE2021xR1']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2021xR1']>]]>

##### Replace and Rewire Variation Point

Replace and Rewire is a new variation point implemented in this version. This variation point kind can be applied to the parts (structural modeling case) and actions (behavioral modeling case). The Replace and Rewire variation point kind functions similarly to the Element Property variation point; however, in addition to changing the part type or behavior of a CallBehaviorAction (the “Replace” part), it also rewires the connectors (structural modeling case) and object flows (behavioral modeling case) connected to the ports of the part and pins of the action (the “Rewire” part).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining system model using variation points' space='PLE2021xR1']>]]>

##### New Compatibility Matrices

To properly work with the Replace and Rewire variation point, the following additional information is needed: information about compatible types (blocks, activities that can replace each other to some degree); and compatibility information between the input and output features of those types (ports – for blocks; parameters – for activities).

There are two predefined matrices to help you conveniently declare the compatibilities:

- **Type Compatibility Matrix**. Use this matrix to declare the type compatibility. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **Input Output Compatibility Matrix**. Use this matrix to declare the compatibility between ports. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compatibility Matrices' space='PLE2021xR1']>]]>

##### Feature Impact Relation Map

The predefined Feature Impact Relation Map helps you identify the model elements affected by the features in the feature model and to understand how they are affected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Feature Impact Relation Map' space='PLE2021xR1']>]]>

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Line Engineering' space='PLE2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 4, 2021</h6><p><br /></p><p>This release introduces the Variability Data import from the ENOVIA Model Definition application, the Replace and Rewire variation point, new compatibility matrices, and the Feature Impact relation map.</p><p>To download the latest MagicDraw version, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2022xR2/Downloading+installation+files" rel="nofollow">Downloading installation files</a>. Don't forget to give us your feedback on <a class="external-link" href="https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574">LinkedIn</a>, <a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc">Twitter</a>, or <a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/">Facebook</a>. For further information, check the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/PLE2021xR1/overview" rel="nofollow">product documentation</a>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Importing Variability Data From ENOVIA Model Definition Application</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Import variability data to your modeling tool project directly from the ENOVIA Model Definition application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. The 3DEXPERIENCE ENOVIA Model Definition Integration p</span>lugin<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> is available for your modeling tool (together with the Product Line Engineering plugin</span>)<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="117"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_model_version_variability.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="d_and_d.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA model data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about importing ENOVIA models >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about authenticating with 3DEXPERIENCE platform >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Replace and Rewire Variation Point</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Replace and Rewire is a new variation point implemented in this version. This variation point kind can be applied to the parts (structural modeling case) and actions (behavioral modeling case). The Replace and Rewire variation point kind functions similarly to the Element Property variation point; however, in addition to changing the part type or behavior of a CallBehaviorAction (the “Replace” part), it also rewires the connectors (structural modeling case) and object flows (behavioral modeling case) connected to the ports of the part and pins of the action (the “Rewire” part).</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="before_rewire_new.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Defining system model using variation points" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>New Compatibility Matrices</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To properly work with the Replace and Rewire variation point, the following additional information is needed: information about compatible types (blocks, activities that can replace each other to some degree); and compatibility information between the input and output features of those types (ports – for blocks; parameters – for activities). </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">There are two predefined matrices to help you conveniently declare the compatibilities:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="edfbabd2-4614-4de5-9ae9-ac9645b13449"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Type Compatibility Matrix</strong>. Use this matrix to declare the type compatibility.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="type_compatibility_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li data-uuid="1b0df2b4-6cc1-4e2f-a4c9-5dbe9363a661"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Input Output Compatibility Matrix</strong>. Use this matrix to declare the compatibility between ports.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inout_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><br /></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Compatibility Matrices" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Feature Impact Relation Map</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The predefined Feature Impact Relation Map helps you identify the model elements affected by the features in the feature model and to understand how they are affected.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="feature_impact_relation_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Feature Impact Relation Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="7bd255e0-91f1-4119-bb94-7f4515ac5be7"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR1" ri:content-title="Product Line Engineering" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Product Line Engineering 2021x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623357 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: (2021x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin

- page_id: `261623357`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623357/2021x+Refresh1+SysML+v1+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T11:59:01.333+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

The 2021x Refresh1 release introduces Behavior to Structure synchronization, Contextual Relationships enhancements, and other improvements.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574),[Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information, see the product.

##### Behavior to Structure Synchronization

You can now keep your Internal Block diagram (IBD) consistent with Activities with less effort! Our brand new behavior-to-structure synchronization allows you to validate Part Properties in an IBD to check whether Proxy Ports exist and are typed by compatible Interface Blocks based on flows found in Activities. As a result, instead of manually updating IBDs to be consistent with Activities, coherence between them is ensured by automatically detecting inconsistencies and providing the user with the solvers to handle each inconsistency individually.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing IBD based on Activities.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior to structure synchronization' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

##### Contextual Relationships Enhancements

Contextual Relationships have undergone major improvements! From now on, you can create contextual relationships directly in structure tree view tables and represent data in table columns by considering the usage context of the row elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating contextual relationships in tables.

Additionally, for a more compact Internal Block Diagram (IBD) view, you can now represent contextual relationships in the **Element Properties** compartment of the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying contextual relationships in the element compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying contextual relationships' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

##### Other improvements

- In earlier versions, the **Show Parts** filter was hard to use in larger projects: only a limited list of connectors was visible; and identifying the connectors was difficult in some cases. This version brings enhancements that will allow easy searching, identification, and selection of all connectors which are connected to a part. Using this dialog, you can easily filter connectors and choose which ones should be displayed in the table or hidden from it. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: June 4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">, 2021</span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-link">The 2021x Refresh1 release introduces Behavior to Structure synchronization, Contextual Relationships enhancements, and other improvements.</span></p><p><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest version, see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. Don't forget to give us your feedback on </span><a class="external-link" href="https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574" rel="nofollow">LinkedIn</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, </span><a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc" rel="nofollow">Twitter</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, or </span><a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/" rel="nofollow">Facebook</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. For further information, see the product </span><ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Behavior to Structure Synchronization</h3><p>You can now keep your Internal Block diagram (IBD) consistent with Activities with less effort! Our brand new behavior-to-structure synchronization allows you to validate Part Properties in an IBD to check whether Proxy Ports exist and are typed by compatible Interface Blocks based on flows found in Activities. As a result, instead of manually updating IBDs to be consistent with Activities, coherence between them is ensured by automatically detecting inconsistencies and providing the user with the solvers to handle each inconsistency individually.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_sync_version_news.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing IBD based on Activities. </h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about behavior to structure synchronization >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Contextual Relationships Enhancements</h3><p>Contextual Relationships have undergone major improvements! From now on, you can create contextual relationships directly in structure tree view tables and represent data in table columns by considering the usage context of the row elements. </p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="contextual_relationships_version_news.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating contextual relationships in tables.</h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Additionally, for a more compact Internal Block Diagram (IBD) view, you can now represent contextual relationships in the <strong>Element Properties</strong> compartment of the element shape.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="contextual_relationships_in_element_properties_compartment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Displaying contextual relationships in the element compartment. </h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Creating and displaying contextual relationships" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about creating and displaying contextual relationships >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Other improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="95cc762a-c367-443c-b060-48f4b515e738"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In earlier versions, the <strong>Show Parts</strong> filter was hard to use in larger projects: only a limited list of connectors was visible; and identifying the connectors was difficult in some cases. This version brings enhancements that will allow easy searching, identification, and selection of all connectors which are connected to a part. </span>Using this dialog, you can easily filter connectors and choose which ones should be displayed in the table or hidden from it. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Whitebox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_parts.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Documentation</span></h3><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR1" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2021x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623436 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00029: (2021x Refresh1) UAF Plugin

- page_id: `261623436`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623436/2021x+Refresh1+UAF+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T11:59:04.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh1) 2021x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

The 2021x Refresh1 release introduces further performance and scalability upgrades in key product areas: compatibility modes, report generation, and metrics calculation. Furthermore, new tables and usability improvements are here to make your modeling experience better.

Additionally, this version introduces a technology preview of ArchiMate model import.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574),[Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information,check theproduct documentation.

#### Performance Improvements

In this version, we continued improving the performance with a major focus on compliance mode, reports, and UAF metrics.

##### Compliance Modes

Turning on the BPMN or SysML compliance modes in large projects now takes less time than in earlier versions.

| Feature | 2021x | 2021x Refresh 1 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN Compliance Mode (UAF) | > 60 min | 9 min 27s |
| SysML Compliance Mode (UPDM) | > 60 min | 3 min |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Report

The report generation was optimized so it takes less time for large projects. In the optimization process, the tables, matrices, and relation maps were excluded from the scope of the Used in Diagrams column. MS Word supports files only up to 512 MB, therefore we do not recommend generating a report with the entire project as its scope.

The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.

| Feature | 2021x Refresh 1 |
| --- | --- |
| All views report (XL project) | 5 min 30s |
| Dictionary report | 31s |
| Summary and Overview report | 15s |
| Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet | 2 min 11s |

##### UAF Metrics

The metrics calculation was also optimized and runs smoothly in the larger projects. However, we do not recommend selecting the entire project as a scope for calculating metrics.

The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.

| Feature | 2021x | 2021x Refresh 1 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| UAF metrics (1M) | Low memory | 21.719 s |
| UAF metrics (10M) | Low memory | 190.35 s |
| UPDM2 metrics (1M) | Low memory | 42.564 s |
| UPDM2 metrics (10M) | Low memory | 56.109 s |

#### Diagrams and Tables

- The Hierarchical Typical Measurements tables were enhanced and no longer hold the status of technology preview. New hierarchical display modes were implemented. You can also generate predefined reports for these tables. Also, having the Hierarchical Typical Measurements table filled out makes it easy to create the Hierarchical Actual Measurements table by simply clicking the Evaluate Measurements button. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAFP2021xR1']>]]>
- The **Hierarchical Actual Measurements** tables are available for all domains. The hierarchical table for actual measurements allows working with the actual measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing Actual Property Sets and define values . When you add a Property Set to the table, all existing Actual Property Sets will be collected recursively according to the defined scope. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAFP2021xR1']>]]>
- The Actual Project Milestone Summary Table (Pj-Rm) depicts all actual project milestones in the model. Adding the actual project milestones into the table allows you to see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table' space='UAFP2021xR1']>]]>
- The new **Strategic Typical Deployment** tables are now available in all frameworks. Two new wizards come along with this new type of table. Use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAFP2021xR1']**to create a table and the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAFP2021xR1']** to add resources to the table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAFP2021xR1']Learn more >> [ATTACHMENT filename='typical_resources_deployment.png']

#### Usability Improvements

- If you need to see the original name, set the Show Original Name of Measurement symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurements' space='UAFP2021xR1']Learn more >>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurements' space='UAFP2021xR1'][IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can create a Model Library package for each UAF domain.
- A new system property is implemented to help with the migration of server projects. There are two cases regarding the migration of **UAF and UPDM** projects:
  - If you are performing UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UPDM environment, only the project version will be migrated.
  - The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the magicdraw.properties (or cea.properties ) file and change the value of the system property -[Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to](http://Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to).uaf\=false to True. By default, the value of this property is False, which means that your UPDM projects will not be migrated. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migrating projects' space='TWCloudTWRT']>]]>

#### ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)

In this version, the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project is implemented. As it is only a technology preview for this version, the imported element mapping is only partial. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing from ArchiMate' space='UAFP2021xR1']>>]]>

#### Documetnation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Plugin Documentation' space='UAFP2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: June 4, <span>2021</span></span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The 2021x Refresh1 release introduces further performance and scalability upgrades in key product areas: compatibility modes, report generation, and metrics calculation. For further information, </span>check the<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>product documentation<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Performance Improvements</h2><p>In this version, we continued improving the performance with a major focus on compliance mode, reports, and UAF metrics.</p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Compliance Modes</h3><p>Turning on the BPMN or SysML compliance modes in large projects now takes less time than in earlier versions. </p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th><p>Feature</p></th><th><p>2021x</p></th><th><p>2021x Refresh 1</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>BPMN Compliance Mode (UAF)</td><td>&gt; 60 min</td><td>9 min 27s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>SysML Compliance Mode (UPDM)</td><td>&gt; 60 min</td><td>3 min</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compliance_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Report</h3><p>The report generation was optimized so it takes less time for large projects. In the optimization process, the tables, matrices, and relation maps were excluded from the scope of the Used in Diagrams column. MS Word supports files only up to 512 MB, therefore we do not recommend generating a report with the entire project as its scope.</p><p>The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 407.0px;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 0.0px;" /><col class="" style="width: 0.0px;" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th><p>Feature</p></th><th><p>2021x Refresh 1</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>All views report (XL project)</td><td>5 min 30s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Dictionary report</td><td>31s</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Summary and  Overview report</td><td colspan="1">15s </td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet </td><td colspan="1">2 min 11s</td></tr></tbody></table><h3>UAF Metrics</h3><p>The metrics calculation was also optimized and runs smoothly in the larger projects. However, we do not recommend selecting the entire project as a scope for calculating metrics.</p><p>The following table shows the testing, performed with the scope of ~ 5000 elements, results.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th><p>Feature</p></th><th><p>2021x</p></th><th><p>2021x Refresh 1</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>UAF metrics (1M)</td><td>Low memory</td><td>21.719 s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>UAF metrics (10M)</td><td>Low memory</td><td>190.35 s</td></tr><tr class=""><td>UPDM2 metrics (1M)</td><td>Low memory</td><td>42.564 s</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">UPDM2 metrics (10M)</td><td colspan="1">Low memory</td><td colspan="1">56.109 s</td></tr></tbody></table><h2><br />Diagrams and Tables</h2><ul><li data-uuid="a0d09e0f-3a46-4570-a36b-950ddd35b0c0">The <strong>Hierarchical Typical Measurements</strong> tables were enhanced and no longer hold the status of technology preview. New hierarchical display modes were implemented. You can also generate predefined reports for these tables. Also, having the Hierarchical Typical Measurements table filled out makes it easy to create the Hierarchical Actual Measurements table by simply clicking the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button.  <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="5b7262fd-fda1-43fa-a7b1-18465467042a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Hierarchical Actual Measurements</strong> tables are available for all domains. The hierarchical table for actual measurements allows working with the actual measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing Actual Property Sets and </span>define values<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. When you add a Property Set to the table, all existing Actual Property Sets will be collected recursively according to the defined scope. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="61bd2a37-9da2-48a6-ad29-7feb8672903a">The<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Actual</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Project</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Milestone Summary</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Table (Pj-Rm)</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> depicts all actual project milestones in the model. Adding the actual project milestones into the table allows you to see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="e3284eea-84fb-4bd1-81bf-1b1c73ab4199"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">T<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">he new <strong>Strategic Typical Deployment</strong> tables are now available in all frameworks. Two new wizards come along with this new type of table. Use the  <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link> </strong>to create a table and the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> to add resources to the table</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram" /><ac:link-body>Learn more &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h2>Usability Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="1f7daef8-08d9-4268-aeb5-1a0c70ea439d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you need to see the original name, set the </span><strong>Show Original Name of Measurement</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Measurements" /><ac:link-body>Learn more &gt;&gt;<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Measurements" /><ac:link-body><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="original_measurement_name_VN.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:link-body></ac:link></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="84a6a7c3-9420-4658-8175-44e2351b8ba4"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>You can create a Model Library package for each UAF domain.</span><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="5a457ea3-7178-40fd-811a-73c518850153"><p>A new system property is implemented to help with the migration of server projects. There are two cases regarding the migration of <strong>UAF and UPDM</strong> projects:</p><ul><li>If you are performing UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UPDM environment, only the project version will be migrated.</li><li>The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the <em>magicdraw.properties</em> (or <em>cea.properties</em>) file and change the value of the system property <em>-<a href="http://Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to">Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to</a>.uaf\=false</em> to True. By default, the value of this property is False, which means that your UPDM projects will not be migrated.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloudTWRT" ri:content-title="Migrating projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><h2>ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)</h2><p>In this version, the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project is implemented. As it is only a technology preview for this version, the imported element mapping is only partial. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1100"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NATO-Architecture-Framework-horizontal-white-background.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh1) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Documetnation</h2><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR1" ri:content-title="UAF Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF Plugin 2021x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623447 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00030: (2021x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin

- page_id: `261623447`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 4, 2021

##### Discontinued Products

**CSV Import Plugin**support will be discontinued from the next major version. [Excel/CSV Sync](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2021xR1/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files) or[Excel/CSV Import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2021xR1/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files) featureswill replace the CSV Import Plugin.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation' space='UPDM2P2021xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: June 4, 2021</span></h6><p><br /></p><h3>Discontinued Products</h3><p><strong>CSV Import Plugin<span> </span></strong>support will be discontinued from the next major version. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2021xR1/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files" style="text-decoration: none;">Excel/CSV Sync</a> or<a style="text-decoration: none;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2021xR1/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files"> Excel/CSV Import</a> features<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">will replace the CSV Import Plugin</span>.</p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="ad25668f-7abf-4580-920f-2d2171c8d79b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2021xR1" ri:content-title="UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UPDM 2 Plugin 2021x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul>
