<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623704 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00043: (2021x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- page_id: `261623704`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623704/2021x+Refresh2+Magic+Software+Architect+MagicDraw
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T18:11:38.694+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: December 3, 2021

The 2021x Refresh2 introduces performance improvements to both the client-side and Teamwork Cloud-side as well as integration, general modeling, and collaborative modeling enhancements.

On the integrations side, ReqIF Import/Export improvements come into effect. For general modeling, this release presents improvements to Structured Expressions.

Finally, collaborative modeling has been extended to enable users to automatically clone used projects together with the main project.

To download the latest MagicDraw version, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574), [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/).

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 available

2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 available

Due to Cassandra 3.11 approaching its end of life, the 2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 has been released on April 21, 2023. This HotFix addresses the issue by supporting Cassandra 4 and introduces a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 5' space=''].

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 has been released on July 15, 2022, for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It introduces a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4' space=''].

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

#### Client side performance improvementsClient-side Performance Improvements

##### **Dependency matrices**Dependency Matrices

Experience enhanced in matrix building performance. From now on, large matrices are built up to 20 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the SysML Allocation Matrix building time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects.

##### **Find performance**Find

Now you can perform a search by phrase in the model 40-70 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1. This is applicable to**Find** and **Find and Replace** dialogs as well as the Find operation used in Structured Expressions (e.g., Derived Properties, Smart Packages, Tables, Matrices, etc.)[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the search by phrase time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects.

##### Element selectionElement selection dialog

Further performance improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the **Select Element** dialog and drop-down lists). The element search can now be performed instantly when using **Filter By Package Imports** for context-specific type selection.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh2 Version News' space='MD2021xR2']

#### TWC performance improvementsTeamwork Cloud-related Performance Improvements

##### Performance Improvements Under Slow Network

Performance improvements have been implemented to optimize working with Teamwork Cloud projects containing a set of used projects in slow network environments (ping time >115 ms).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']A chart comparing the Project Load, Project Commit, and Project Update operations speed in slow network environments.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh2 Version News' space='MD2021xR2']

#### Integrations

##### ReqIF Import/Export Improvements

Further enhancements have been implemented for the ReqIF import/export plugin. A new project option **Store ReqIF Metadata Information** has been introduced to ensure ReqIF standard-compliant round-trip of data between the modeling tool and any other ReqIF standard-compliant tool. Once enabled, element IDs found in ReqIF files are retained when such files are imported into the modeling tool. As a result, elements updated in the modeling tool and exported back to external ReqIF-compliant tools are no longer treated as new.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project option to store the ReqIF metadata in the model.

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

##### Structured Expressions Improvements

Long-awaited improvements have landed! Working with Structured Expressions is now more convenient and less confusing.

- The Comparison operations, such as Equals, GreaterThan, and LessThan, can consume a set containing a single element, for instance, as returned by Metachain Navigation and Simple Navigation operations
- In cases you cannot find the needed property for the Property Test operation, you can now show the full list of properties instead of limiting it to properties applicable to the contextual element type only.
- Now all template operations (Simple Navigation, Metachain Navigation, Find, and Implied Relation) are represented in italic to clearly indicate that they are not specified. As a result, constant double-checks and manual removals of empty operations are no longer needed.
- You can now apply changes to the Parameter name without the fear of breaking its usages within the Structured Expression specified in the Opaque Behavior body.

Stay tuned, more enhancements are coming in future versions!

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2021xR2']>]]>

##### Other Improvements

- From now on, suspect links in used projects are ignored when performing the suspect links analysis, meaning that the scope of the analysis is narrowed down to only relevant elements.
- The **Notification Window** is no longer opened automatically when opening projects and switching between or creating new projects since the**Notification Window** status (open/closed) is now respected for each project individually.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh2 Version News' space='MD2021xR2']

#### CollaCollaboration

##### Cloning Used Projects

Project cloning has been upgraded! For more effective working with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can now clone used projects together with the main project automatically by simply selecting a checkbox.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Cloning used projects together with the main project.

##### Other TWC ImprovementsOther Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements

- For security reasons, you can now add an argument to the properties file of the modeling tool to hide logging in to Teamwork Cloud with the username/password.
- From now on, you can access the properties of the selected version much quicker since the Version Properties button has been added to the toolbar and the shortcut menu of the Content History and element History panels.
- The Package permissions dialogs have been improved for usability purposes.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh2 Version News' space='MD2021xR2']

#### Report Wizard

- In this version, Report Wizard comes with an additional capability of getting derived property value by using $report.getStereotypeProperty() and $report.getStereotypePropertyString() .
- With $report.isTypeOf(element, stereotypeName) for checking a super type of the specific element, you are now able to check whether an element is a subtype of the specified stereotype. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report tool' space='MD2021xR2']>]]>
- The new Includes other files option in the Export dialog allows you to include related files of an exported template into the same MRZIP file. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Management Pane' space='MD2021xR2']>]]>
- You can now customize page orientation and margin in double or String values with $pageprop.setPortrait($top, $bottom, $left, $right) and $pageprop.setLanscape($top, $bottom, $left, $right) functions. You can also set page orientation to portrait or landscape with $pageprop.setPortrait() and $pageprop.setLandscape() functions for generated reports. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pageprop tool' space='MD2021xR2']>]]>
- The current Velocity Template Language has been upgraded from Version 1.6.2 to 2.2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Velocity templating language' space='MD2021xR2']>]]>

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Software Architect Documentation' space='MSA2021xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2021xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a571d67-6c72-4e26-ba35-8174d8f06643"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Released on: December 3, 2021</span></h6><p><br /></p><p>The 2021x Refresh2 introduces performance improvements to both the client-side and Teamwork Cloud-side as well as integration, general modeling, and collaborative modeling enhancements.</p><p>On the integrations side, ReqIF Import/Export improvements come into effect. For general modeling, this release presents improvements to Structured Expressions.</p><p>Finally, collaborative modeling has been extended to enable users to automatically clone used projects together with the main project.</p><p>To download the latest MagicDraw version, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link>. Don't forget to give us your feedback on <a class="external-link" href="https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574" rel="nofollow">LinkedIn</a>, <a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc" rel="nofollow">Twitter</a>, or <a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/" rel="nofollow">Facebook</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da40baa9-fbfb-4c05-b36d-c79d329e3663"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to Cassandra 3.11 approaching its end of life, the 2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 has been released on April 21, 2023. This HotFix addresses the issue by supporting Cassandra 4 and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>introduces</span> a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 5" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 5 News]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4600b85-5de4-406b-9863-835fad23c6ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 has been released on July 15, 2022, for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>It introduces</span> a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 News]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24987ff8-45af-4ac1-94a6-557522e9f2da"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db067032-4b80-4082-8013-98824ca3ade2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[downloading its plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5cb0ce0-ea4d-49e5-942e-eb94c9eb9e18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Client side performance improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Client-side Performance Improvements</h2><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5505d40-ddb0-4c0b-87f5-ad347798519c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Dependency matrices</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Dependency Matrices</h3><p>Experience enhanced in matrix building performance. From now on, large matrices are built up to 20 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="matrix_building_time.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">A chart comparing the SysML Allocation Matrix building time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects.</span></h6><h3><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77a2cb76-c31a-4efb-b321-72d0813cb8cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Find performance</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Find</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Now you can perform a search by phrase in the model 40-70 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This is applicable to<strong> Find</strong> and <strong>Find and Replace</strong> dialogs as well as the Find operation used in Structured Expressions (e.g., Derived Properties, Smart Packages, Tables, Matrices, etc.)</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="searching_by_phrase_version_news (1).png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">A chart comparing the search by phrase time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects. </span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf37f240-4630-4458-8511-ff82b730a846"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Element selection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Element selection dialog</h3><p>Further performance improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog and drop-down lists). The element search can now be performed instantly when using <strong>Filter By Package Imports</strong> for context-specific type selection. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3f6af67-33a2-4155-b96a-40ef30f4b304"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC performance improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud-related Performance Improvements</h2><h3>Performance Improvements Under Slow Network</h3><p>Performance improvements have been implemented to optimize working with Teamwork Cloud projects containing a set of used projects in slow network environments (ping time &gt;115 ms).  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_load_new.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_commit_new.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_update_new.png" /></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">A chart comparing the Project Load, Project Commit, and Project Update operations speed in slow network environments.</span></h6><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);"><ac:link ac:anchor="t"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h2>Integrations</h2><h3>ReqIF Import/Export Improvements</h3><p>Further enhancements have been implemented for the ReqIF import/export plugin. A new project option <strong>Store ReqIF Metadata Information</strong> has been introduced to ensure ReqIF standard-compliant round-trip of data between the modeling tool and any other ReqIF standard-compliant tool. Once enabled, element IDs found in ReqIF files are retained when such files are imported into the modeling tool. As a result, elements updated in the modeling tool and exported back to external ReqIF-compliant tools are no longer treated as new.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="store_reqif_metadata_information.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">The project option to store the ReqIF metadata in the model. </span></h6><h2>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3>Structured Expressions Improvements</h3><p>Long-awaited improvements have landed! Working with Structured Expressions is now more convenient and less confusing.</p><ul><li data-uuid="2d3308d4-f1cd-4f04-8607-67533d55ea56">The Comparison operations, such as Equals, GreaterThan, and LessThan, can consume a set containing a single element, for instance, as returned by Metachain Navigation and Simple Navigation operations</li><li data-uuid="e654787f-1a45-4a1a-8f02-80fd7bd201e0">In cases you cannot find the needed property for the Property Test operation, you can now show the full list of properties instead of limiting it to properties applicable to the contextual element type only.</li><li data-uuid="159e8f02-5562-49f6-8971-c9b2cd2259eb">Now all template operations (Simple Navigation, Metachain Navigation, Find, and Implied Relation) are represented in italic to clearly indicate that they are not specified. As a result, constant double-checks and manual removals of empty operations are no longer needed.</li><li data-uuid="a5a9295a-0b80-4183-8593-8019e937a757"><p>You can now apply changes to the Parameter name without the fear of breaking its usages within the Structured Expression specified in the Opaque Behavior body. </p></li></ul><p>Stay tuned, more enhancements are coming in future versions!</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Structured Expressions>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Other Improvements </h3><ul><li data-uuid="98e316c7-ddbc-41b6-a93f-081047b8c4db"><p>From now on, suspect links in used projects are ignored when performing the suspect links analysis, meaning that the scope of the analysis is narrowed down to only relevant elements. </p></li><li data-uuid="32e3f21a-aca4-4833-9d93-01de0ec09c32"><p>The <strong>Notification Window</strong> is no longer opened automatically when opening projects and switching between or creating new projects since the<strong> Notification Window</strong> status (open/closed) is now respected for each project individually. </p></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="t"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b09d8201-2485-4b24-8757-4c1ce9d8b556"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Colla</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h2><h3>Cloning Used Projects</h3><p>Project cloning has been upgraded! For more effective working with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can now clone used projects together with the main project automatically by simply selecting a checkbox.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_cloning_version_news (1).png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Cloning used projects together with the main project.</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92b28fa0-ea68-4451-9b24-c55b92b594a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other TWC Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="c24e4677-64f3-4a01-aeb9-90a64a07d2e2">For security reasons, you can now add an argument to the properties file of the modeling tool to hide logging in to Teamwork Cloud with the username/password.</li><li data-uuid="898285a4-e228-4f76-96c0-c78aed687d9c">From now on, you can access the properties of the selected version much quicker since the <strong>Version Properties</strong> button has been added to the toolbar and the shortcut menu of the Content History and element History panels.</li><li data-uuid="0ef1d257-3563-47a9-88b8-5450039ded07"><p>The Package permissions dialogs have been improved for usability purposes.</p></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Report Wizard</h2><ul><li data-uuid="e6a09dd7-1560-4abf-ada8-88d204b9c825">In this version, Report Wizard comes with an additional capability of getting derived property value by using <strong>$report.getStereotypeProperty()</strong> and <strong>$report.getStereotypePropertyString()</strong>. </li><li data-uuid="21002aaf-f410-4627-9f91-02991e0d771d">With<strong> $report.isTypeOf(element, stereotypeName) </strong>for checking a super type of the specific element, you are now able to check whether an element is a subtype of the specified stereotype. <ac:link ac:anchor="$report.isTypeOf(element,stereotypeName)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Report tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Get familiarized with this report tool >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="533d3c50-fba5-4304-9c06-27f2b8036b68">The new <strong>Includes other files</strong> option in the <strong>Export</strong> dialog allows you to include related files of an exported template into the same MRZIP file. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Template Management Pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the Template Management pane >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="216b0673-b93c-4618-83b8-e608a2ceb3e5">You can now customize page orientation and margin in double or String values with <strong>$pageprop.setPortrait($top, $bottom, $left, $right)</strong> and <strong>$pageprop.setLanscape($top, $bottom, $left, $right)</strong> functions. You can also set page orientation to portrait or landscape with <strong>$pageprop.setPortrait()</strong> and <strong>$pageprop.setLandscape()</strong> functions for generated reports.  <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Pageprop tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to customize the margin and set page orientation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="3bf6a9de-824e-4ead-9b52-f4f2484fa035">The current Velocity Template Language has been upgraded from Version 1.6.2 to 2.2. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Velocity templating language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Find out more about the velocity templating language >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="4bad8a5e-ede5-4852-92e3-e6b3adf66aee"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSA2021xR2" ri:content-title="Magic Software Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Software Architect 2021 Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="103e6b42-9bcc-40d1-824a-e22ae9491ddb"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="MagicDraw Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw 2021x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623755 space=VN version=4 -->
## PAGE 00044: (2021x Refresh2) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `261623755`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261623755/2021x+Refresh2+Magic+Systems+of+Systems+Architect+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-10-14T16:21:08.408+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: December 3, 2021

The release of 2021x Refresh2 introduces the UAF 1.2 Plugin as a technology preview to support UAF 1.2. In this version, the UAF 1.2 Profile support (however it is supported only in the UAF framework), migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2, and UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements were implemented. Furthermore, the ArchiMate (technology preview) brings more element mapping options.

The Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization has undergone further improvements and now allows to completely synchronize the entire structure model with the behavior model. Additionally, the Requirements verification can now be performed directly in the Requirement Table. Moreover, the enhancements for Modelica allow importing Modelica files as well as extending the model generated as a Modelica code, using a Comment element with the «ModelicaExtension» stereotype.

The performance improvements to both the client-side and Teamwork Cloud-side as well as integration, general modeling, and collaborative modeling enhancements.

Finally, collaborative modeling has been extended to enable users to automatically clone used projects together with the main project.

To download the latest version, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574), [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information, check the product documentation.

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 available

2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 available

Due to Cassandra 3.11 approaching its end of life, the 2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 has been released on April 21, 2023. This HotFix addresses the issue by supporting Cassandra 4 and introduces a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 5' space=''].

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 has been released on July 15, 2022, for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It introduces a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4' space=''].

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### NOTE: 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832' space='FAQ'].

Also, see the Knowledge Base article at [https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e](https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e).

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

#### UAF Features

##### UAF 1.2 Support (Technology Preview)

In 2021x Refresh 2, the UAF 1.2 support is introduced as a technology preview.

For this release, the separate UAF 1.2 Plugin is included in Cameo Enterprise Architecture and Magic Systems of Systems Architect modeling tools. This plugin is only for preview purposes and should **NOT** be used in production.

What is implemented for this release:

- UAF 1.2 Profile support, however, it is supported only in the UAF framework
- Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements:
  - Architecture Management Domain
  - Improvements in Strategic and Services Domains (clarify semantics, add new concepts, improve exposition)
  - Support of Value Streams
  - Risk becomes a cross-cutting construct

>]]>

The development will continue for the upcoming releases and will focus on the fully UAF 1.2 support for DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0 MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks and migration from UPDM to UAF 1.2.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']UAF 1.2 grid. 
(Click the image to enlarge)

##### ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)

In this version, we continued working on the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project. The imported element mapping is still only partial, but more mapping options were implemented. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing from ArchiMate' space='UAFP2021xR2']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### SysML Features

##### Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization

The Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization has undergone further improvements to enable you to completely synchronize the entire structure model with the behavior model. First, the set of validation rules has been extended by introducing the brand new validation rules for the detection of missing Connectors and Item Flows. That is not all! You can now automatically build the structure model according to defined Activities and, at the same time, generate an Internal Block Diagram by executing a single-click command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Checking the consistency of the Internal Block Diagram with Activities and then handling detected inconsistencies using solvers.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior to structure synchronization' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']>]]>

##### Requirements Verification in Table

The Requirements verification can now be performed directly in the Requirement Table. The analysis is carried out by evaluating whether the value of the property satisfying the Requirement is within a range of lower and upper bounds extracted from the Requirement text. Additionally, the automatically calculated margin value helps to determine how close the system model is to fulfilling Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Performing the Requirements verification analysis in the Requirement Table.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirements verification' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']>]]>

##### Modelica Enhancements

###### Importing from Modelica file

In this version, the Modelica import was enhanced. Previously, only Modelica Library import was available. Now you can import the Modelica file (*.mo) content into the single selected Block of your SysML model. During the import, context-specific initial values can be updated or missing classifiers can be created. Furthermore, connector, property, and port changes are also supported.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modelica import' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']>]]>

###### Extending models with Comments

You can extend the model generated as a Modelica code, using a Comment element with the «ModelicaExtension» stereotype. The Modelica Extension content is exported to the Modelica file together with the model. As a result, the code is generated depending on where the Comment is attached.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending models with Comments' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']>]]>

##### Other Enhancements

- The Autowiring command was renamed Autowire Parts. You can find this command in the IBD diagram toolbar, Tools menu.
- The Autowire Parts , Delegate Port(s) , and Behavior to Structure Synchronization commands have been grouped under Tools in the diagram toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='delegate ports_btos_autowire_under_tools.png']
- You can now easily check if the Item Flow realized by the Object Flow is compatible with Pin types in a SysML Activity Diagram.
- You can now easily check the sent Signal compatibility with the Proxy Port specified in the **On Port** property of the Send Signal action or the compatibility of the accepted Signal with the Proxy Port specified in the **Port** property of the Accept Event action in a SysML Activity Diagram.

#### Performance Improvements

##### Client-side Performance Improvements

- **Dependency matricesDependency Matrices**. Experience enhanced in matrix building performance. From now on, large matrices are built up to 20 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] A chart comparing the SysML Allocation Matrix building time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects.
- **Find performanceFind**. Now you can perform a search by phrase in the model 40-70 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1. This is applicable to**Find** and **Find and Replace** dialogs as well as the Find operation used in Structured Expressions (e.g., Derived Properties, Smart Packages, Tables, Matrices, etc.)[IMAGE alt='' src=''] A chart comparing the search by phrase time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects.
- Element selection Element selection dialog . Further performance improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the Select Element dialog and drop-down lists). The element search can now be performed instantly when using Filter By Package Imports for context-specific type selection.

##### Teamwork Cloud-related Performance Improvements

**Performance Improvements Under Slow Network**

Performance improvements have been implemented to optimize working with Teamwork Cloud projects containing a set of used projects in slow network environments (ping time >115 ms).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']A chart comparing the Project Load, Project Commit, and Project Update operations speed in slow network environments.

#### Integrations

##### Effectivity Import for TRM Requirements

Requirements from the TRM application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform can be imported/synchronized into the modeling tools using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo DataHub Documentation' space='CDH2021xR2']. With 2021x Refresh2, DataHub can also import the effectivity markings of the requirements as equivalent variation points in the modeling tools.

The PLE mechanisms (variant highlight, variant realization transformation) can then be applied to these variable requirements.

As a prerequisite for effectivity import, Model Version variability and configurations have to be imported beforehand. [Learn more about importing variability data >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/PLE2021xR2/2021x+Refresh1+Version+News#id-2021xRefresh1VersionNews-ImportingVariabilityDataFromENOVIAModelDefinitionApplication)

**Note.** The effectivity import is unidirectional. You can import from the 3DEXPERIENCE platform to the modeling tools.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ENOVIA Effectivity Expressions as Variation Points' space='PLE2021xR2']>]]>

##### ReqIF Import/Export Improvements

Further enhancements have been implemented for the ReqIF import/export plugin. A new project option **Store ReqIF Metadata Information** has been introduced to ensure ReqIF standard-compliant round-trip of data between the modeling tool and any other ReqIF standard-compliant tool. Once enabled, element IDs found in ReqIF files are retained when such files are imported into the modeling tool. As a result, elements updated in the modeling tool and exported back to external ReqIF-compliant tools are no longer treated as new.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project option to store the ReqIF metadata in the model.

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

##### Structured Expressions Improvements

Long-awaited improvements have landed! Working with Structured Expressions is now more convenient and less confusing.

- The Comparison operations, such as Equals, GreaterThan, and LessThan, can consume a set containing a single element, for instance, as returned by Metachain Navigation and Simple Navigation operations
- In cases you cannot find the needed property for the Property Test operation, you can now show the full list of properties instead of limiting it to properties applicable to the contextual element type only.
- Now all template operations (Simple Navigation, Metachain Navigation, Find, and Implied Relation) are represented in italic to clearly indicate that they are not specified. As a result, constant double-checks and manual removals of empty operations are no longer needed.
- You can now apply changes to the Parameter name without the fear of breaking its usages within the Structured Expression specified in the Opaque Behavior body.

Stay tuned, more enhancements are coming in future versions!

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2021xR2']>]]>

##### Other Improvements

- From now on, suspect links in used projects are ignored when performing the suspect links analysis, meaning that the scope of the analysis is narrowed down to only relevant elements.
- The **Notification Window** is no longer opened automatically when opening projects and switching between or creating new projects since the**Notification Window** status (open/closed) is now respected for each project individually.

#### Collaboration

##### Cloning Used Projects

Project cloning has been upgraded! For more effective working with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can now clone used projects together with the main project automatically by simply selecting a checkbox.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Cloning used projects together with the main project.

##### Other Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements

- For security reasons, you can now add an argument to the properties file of the modeling tool to hide logging in to Teamwork Cloud with the username/password.
- From now on, you can access the properties of the selected version much quicker since the Version Properties button has been added to the toolbar and the shortcut menu of the Content History and element History panels.
- The Package permissions dialogs have been improved for usability purposes.

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation' space='MSOSA2021xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation' space='CEA2021xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a571d67-6c72-4e26-ba35-8174d8f06643"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: December 3, 2021</h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The release of 2021x Refresh2 introduces the UAF 1.2 Plugin as a technology preview to support UAF 1.2. In this version, the UAF 1.2 Profile support (however it is supported only in the UAF framework), migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2, and UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements were implemented. Furthermore, the ArchiMate (technology preview) brings more element mapping options.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">T<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">he Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization has undergone further improvements and now allows to completely synchronize the entire structure model with the behavior model. Additionally, the Requirements verification can now be performed directly in the Requirement Table. Moreover, the enhancements for Modelica allow importing Modelica files as well as extending the model generated as a Modelica code, using a Comment element with the «ModelicaExtension» stereotype.</span></span></p><p>The performance improvements to both the client-side and Teamwork Cloud-side as well as integration, general modeling, and collaborative modeling enhancements.</p><p>Finally, collaborative modeling has been extended to enable users to automatically clone used projects together with the main project.</p><p>To download the latest version, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link>. Don't forget to give us your feedback on <a class="external-link" href="https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574" rel="nofollow">LinkedIn</a>, <a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc" rel="nofollow">Twitter</a>, or <a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/" rel="nofollow">Facebook</a>. For further information, check the product documentation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da40baa9-fbfb-4c05-b36d-c79d329e3663"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to Cassandra 3.11 approaching its end of life, the 2021x Refresh2 HotFix 5 has been released on April 21, 2023. This HotFix addresses the issue by supporting Cassandra 4 and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>introduces</span> a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 5" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 5 News]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4600b85-5de4-406b-9863-835fad23c6ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 has been released on July 15, 2022, for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>It introduces</span> a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 4 News]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24987ff8-45af-4ac1-94a6-557522e9f2da"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF2 has been released on March 4, 2022. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF2 addresses Log4j 1.x and 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db067032-4b80-4082-8013-98824ca3ade2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2021x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the Apache Log4j vulnerability issues, the 2021x Refresh2 HF1 has been released on December 22, 2021. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The HF1 addresses Log4j 2.x versions.</span></p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="CATIA Magic and No Magic products affected by Log4j vulnerability - CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-44832" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Also, see the Knowledge Base article at <a href="https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e">https://kb.dsxclient.3ds.com/mashup-ui/page/resultqai?id=QA00000102301e</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[downloading its plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>UAF Features</h2><h3>UAF 1.2 Support (Technology Preview)</h3><p>In 2021x Refresh 2, the UAF 1.2 support is introduced as a technology preview.</p><p>For this release, the separate UAF 1.2 Plugin is included in Cameo Enterprise Architecture and Magic Systems of Systems Architect modeling tools. This plugin is only for preview purposes and should <u><strong>NOT</strong></u> be used in production.</p><p>What is implemented for this release:</p><ul><li data-uuid="06940c6e-ae68-4685-b5ca-5cc02893df95">UAF 1.2 Profile support, however, it is supported only in the UAF framework</li><li data-uuid="d6b30b48-0519-443b-8453-8c90d42677ef">Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2</li><li data-uuid="b2b1574c-66e5-4d16-b0ac-2ebb09eb3b90">UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements:<ul><li>Architecture Management Domain</li><li>Improvements in Strategic and Services Domains (clarify semantics, add new concepts, improve exposition)</li><li>Support of Value Streams</li><li>Risk becomes a cross-cutting construct</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="UAF12P2021xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See the UAF 1.2 Plugin documentation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The development will continue for the upcoming releases and will focus on the fully UAF 1.2 support for DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0 MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks and migration from UPDM to UAF 1.2.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png" /></ac:image>UAF 1.2 grid.<br />(Click the image to enlarge)</h6><h3>ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)</h3><p>In this version, we continued working on the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project. The imported element mapping is still only partial, but more mapping options were implemented. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NATO-Architecture-Framework-horizontal-white-background.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h2>SysML Features</h2><h3>Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization</h3><p>The Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization has undergone further improvements to enable you to completely synchronize the entire structure model with the behavior model. First, the set of validation rules has been extended by introducing the brand new validation rules for the detection of missing Connectors and Item Flows. That is not all! You can now automatically build the structure model according to defined Activities and, at the same time, generate an Internal Block Diagram by executing a single-click command. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="btos_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Checking the consistency of the Internal Block Diagram with Activities and then handling detected inconsistencies using solvers.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about behavior to structure synhronization>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Requirements Verification in Table</h3><p>The Requirements verification can now be performed directly in the Requirement Table. The analysis is carried out by evaluating whether the value of the property satisfying the Requirement is within a range of lower and upper bounds extracted from the Requirement text. Additionally, the automatically calculated margin value helps to determine how close the system model is to fulfilling Requirements.  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirements_verification_version_news.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Performing the Requirements verification analysis in the Requirement Table. </h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Requirements verification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Requirements verification>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><h3>Modelica Enhancements</h3><h4>Importing from Modelica file</h4><p>In this version, the Modelica import was enhanced. Previously, only Modelica Library import was available. Now you can import the Modelica file (*.mo) content into the single selected Block of your SysML model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">During the import, context-specific initial values can be updated or missing classifiers can be created.</span> Furthermore, connector, property, and port changes are also supported.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Modelica import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Modelica import >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4>Extending models with Comments</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can extend the model generated as a Modelica code, using a Comment element with the «ModelicaExtension» stereotype. The Modelica Extension content is exported to the Modelica file together with the model. <span>As a result, the code is generated depending on where the Comment is attached.</span></span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_extension_to_block_and_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Extending models with Comments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about extending the model >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Other Enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="117a0bf4-d076-4b33-91e6-03b4153ae20c">The Autowiring command was renamed Autowire Parts. You can find this command in the IBD diagram toolbar, Tools menu.</li><li data-uuid="9b1f0d95-0091-4490-afd7-7bf93bd607d9">The <strong>Autowire Parts</strong>, <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong>, and <strong>Behavior to Structure Synchronization</strong> commands have been grouped under Tools in the diagram toolbar.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delegate ports_btos_autowire_under_tools.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="82db25bc-7fa5-4fe7-a663-648ba0661280"><p>You can now easily check if the Item Flow realized by the Object Flow is compatible with Pin types in a SysML Activity Diagram.</p></li><li data-uuid="ce031bf0-7963-438a-a652-8a93d5ace5f6"><p>You can now easily check the sent Signal compatibility with the Proxy Port specified in the <strong>On Port</strong> property of the Send Signal action or the compatibility of the accepted Signal with the Proxy Port specified in the <strong>Port</strong> property of the Accept Event action in a SysML Activity Diagram.</p></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="margin-left: 0.0in;">Performance Improvements</h2><h3>Client-side Performance Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="a57836ea-a062-4bb1-a823-b1b6e9fefd75"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5505d40-ddb0-4c0b-87f5-ad347798519c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Dependency matrices</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Dependency Matrices</strong>. Experience enhanced in matrix building performance. From now on, large matrices are built up to 20 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="matrix_building_time.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">A chart comparing the SysML Allocation Matrix building time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects.</span></h6></li><li data-uuid="2f1c10d4-9151-4d44-87f8-5e16c7af81ff"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77a2cb76-c31a-4efb-b321-72d0813cb8cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Find performance</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Find</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. Now you can perform a search by phrase in the model 40-70 times faster compared to 2021x Refresh1. <span>This is applicable to<strong> Find</strong> and <strong>Find and Replace</strong> dialogs as well as the Find operation used in Structured Expressions (e.g., Derived Properties, Smart Packages, Tables, Matrices, etc.)</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="searching_by_phrase_version_news (1).png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">A chart comparing the search by phrase time in 2021x Refresh1 and 2021x Refresh2 projects. </span></h6></li><li data-uuid="20ce4859-0c09-4c7d-a00b-8cae8da7d8e9"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf37f240-4630-4458-8511-ff82b730a846"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Element selection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Element selection dialog</strong>. Further performance improvements have been introduced to element selection (i.e., the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog and drop-down lists). The element search can now be performed instantly when using <strong>Filter By Package Imports</strong> for context-specific type selection. </li></ul><h3>Teamwork Cloud-related Performance Improvements</h3><p><strong>Performance Improvements Under Slow Network</strong></p><p>Performance improvements have been implemented to optimize working with Teamwork Cloud projects containing a set of used projects in slow network environments (ping time &gt;115 ms).  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_load_new.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_commit_new.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_update_new.png" /></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">A chart comparing the Project Load, Project Commit, and Project Update operations speed in slow network environments.</span></h6><h2>Integrations</h2><h3>Effectivity Import for TRM Requirements</h3><p>Requirements from the TRM application on the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">3DEXPERIENCE platform</span> can be imported/synchronized into the modeling tools using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2021xR2" ri:content-title="Cameo DataHub Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo DataHub]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. With 2021x Refresh2, DataHub can also import the effectivity markings of the requirements as equivalent variation points in the modeling tools.</p><p>The PLE mechanisms (variant highlight, variant realization transformation) can then be applied to these variable requirements.</p><p>As a prerequisite for effectivity import, Model Version variability and configurations have to be imported beforehand. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/PLE2021xR2/2021x+Refresh1+Version+News#id-2021xRefresh1VersionNews-ImportingVariabilityDataFromENOVIAModelDefinitionApplication">Learn more about importing variability data &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><strong>Note.</strong> The effectivity import is unidirectional. You can import from the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">3DEXPERIENCE</span> platform to the modeling tools.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2021xR2" ri:content-title="Importing ENOVIA Effectivity Expressions as Variation Points" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about importing effectivity >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>ReqIF Import/Export Improvements</h3><p>Further enhancements have been implemented for the ReqIF import/export plugin. A new project option <strong>Store ReqIF Metadata Information</strong> has been introduced to ensure ReqIF standard-compliant round-trip of data between the modeling tool and any other ReqIF standard-compliant tool. Once enabled, element IDs found in ReqIF files are retained when such files are imported into the modeling tool. As a result, elements updated in the modeling tool and exported back to external ReqIF-compliant tools are no longer treated as new.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="store_reqif_metadata_information.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The project option to store the ReqIF metadata in the model. </span></h6><h2>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3>Structured Expressions Improvements</h3><p>Long-awaited improvements have landed! Working with Structured Expressions is now more convenient and less confusing.</p><ul><li data-uuid="c426fff0-61ab-4999-afdb-c33bf64e7a59">The Comparison operations, such as Equals, GreaterThan, and LessThan, can consume a set containing a single element, for instance, as returned by Metachain Navigation and Simple Navigation operations</li><li data-uuid="b046246d-3648-41f8-bc18-671e73d07505">In cases you cannot find the needed property for the Property Test operation, you can now show the full list of properties instead of limiting it to properties applicable to the contextual element type only.</li><li data-uuid="f4604b5d-9829-43af-ad37-770488281a5e">Now all template operations (Simple Navigation, Metachain Navigation, Find, and Implied Relation) are represented in italic to clearly indicate that they are not specified. As a result, constant double-checks and manual removals of empty operations are no longer needed.</li><li data-uuid="d54bd482-dc4d-47b4-8e0b-d801b338b1ce"><p>You can now apply changes to the Parameter name without the fear of breaking its usages within the Structured Expression specified in the Opaque Behavior body. </p></li></ul><p>Stay tuned, more enhancements are coming in future versions!</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Structured Expressions>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Other Improvements </h3><ul><li data-uuid="7e3da952-0386-4704-9814-0f9deb2ee19e"><p>From now on, suspect links in used projects are ignored when performing the suspect links analysis, meaning that the scope of the analysis is narrowed down to only relevant elements. </p></li><li data-uuid="07e15088-1a04-4a78-b541-5edac9e94084"><p>The <strong>Notification Window</strong> is no longer opened automatically when opening projects and switching between or creating new projects since the<strong> Notification Window</strong> status (open/closed) is now respected for each project individually.</p></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Collaboration</h2><h3>Cloning Used Projects</h3><p>Project cloning has been upgraded! For more effective working with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can now clone used projects together with the main project automatically by simply selecting a checkbox.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_cloning_version_news (1).png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Cloning used projects together with the main project.</span></h6><h3>Other Teamwork Cloud-related Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="78306022-0e7b-48a5-8776-94754f219456">For security reasons, you can now add an argument to the properties file of the modeling tool to hide logging in to Teamwork Cloud with the username/password.</li><li data-uuid="7fa0e91b-b837-4fed-abef-cd8817dee4db">From now on, you can access the properties of the selected version much quicker since the <strong>Version Properties</strong> button has been added to the toolbar and the shortcut menu of the Content History and element History panels.</li><li data-uuid="e85cf0e8-9261-4d24-a951-27baac05570f"><p>The Package permissions dialogs have been improved for usability purposes.</p></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="eb7b72d7-c166-40a5-8887-897bf8512a3e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSOSA2021xR2" ri:content-title="Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Systems of Systems Architect 2021x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="908dfe3d-e740-4ed1-b4ea-ff4ae4fe4e65"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CEA2021xR2" ri:content-title="Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2021x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624186 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00045: (2021x Refresh2) Product Line Engineering

- page_id: `261624186`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624186/2021x+Refresh2+Product+Line+Engineering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:42:48.381+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: December 3, 2021

To download the latest MagicDraw version, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL2022xR2/pages/127979320/Downloading+installation+files). Don't forget to give us your feedback on[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574),[Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information,check the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Line Engineering' space='PLE2021xR2'].

##### Effectivity Import for TRM Requirements

Requirements from the TRM application on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform can be imported/synchronized into the modeling tools using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo DataHub Documentation' space='CDH2021xR2']. With 2021x Refresh2, DataHub can also import the effectivity markings of the requirements as equivalent variation points in the modeling tools.

The MagicDraw PLE mechanisms (variant highlight, variant realization transformation) can then be applied to these variable requirements.

As a prerequisite for effectivity import, Model Version variability and configurations have to be imported beforehand. [Learn more about importing variability data >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/PLE2021xR2/2021x+Refresh1+Version+News#id-2021xRefresh1VersionNews-ImportingVariabilityDataFromENOVIAModelDefinitionApplication)

**Note.** The effectivity import is unidirectional. You can import from the 3DEXPERIENCE platform to the modeling tools.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ENOVIA Effectivity Expressions as Variation Points' space='PLE2021xR2']>]]>

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Line Engineering' space='PLE2021xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624112 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00046: (2021x Refresh2) SysML v1 Plugin

- page_id: `261624112`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624112/2021x+Refresh2+SysML+v1+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:42:45.577+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: December 3, 2021

In the 2021x Refresh2 release, the Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization has undergone further improvements and now allows to completely synchronize the entire structure model with the behavior model. Additionally, the Requirements verification can now be performed directly in the Requirement Table. Moreover, the enhancements for Modelica allow importing Modelica files as well as extending the model generated as a Modelica code, using a Comment element with the «ModelicaExtension» stereotype.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574),[Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information, see the product.

##### Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization

The Behavior-to-Structure Synchronization has undergone further improvements to enable you to completely synchronize the entire structure model with the behavior model. First, the set of validation rules has been extended by introducing the brand new validation rules for the detection of missing Connectors and Item Flows. That is not all! You can now automatically build the structure model according to defined Activities and, at the same time, generate an Internal Block Diagram by executing a single-click command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Checking the consistency of the Internal Block Diagram with Activities and then handling detected inconsistencies using solvers.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior to structure synchronization' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']>]]>

##### Requirements Verification in Table

The Requirements verification can now be performed directly in the Requirement Table. The analysis is carried out by evaluating whether the value of the property satisfying the Requirement is within a range of lower and upper bounds extracted from the Requirement text. Additionally, the automatically calculated margin value helps to determine how close the system model is to fulfilling Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Performing the Requirements verification analysis in the Requirement Table.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirements verification' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']Learn more about Requirements verification>>

##### Modelica Enhancements

**Importing from Modelica file**

In this version, the Modelica import was enhanced. Previously, only Modelica Library import was available. Now you can import the Modelica file (*.mo) content into the single selected Block of your SysML model. During the import, context-specific initial values can be updated or missing classifiers can be created. Furthermore, connector, property, and port changes are also supported.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modelica import' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']>]]>

**Extending models with Comments**

You can extend the model generated as a Modelica code, using a Comment element with the «ModelicaExtension» stereotype. The Modelica Extension content is exported to the Modelica file together with the model. As a result, the code is generated depending on where the Comment is attached.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending models with Comments' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']>]]>

##### Other Enhancements

- The Autowiring command was renamed Autowire Parts. You can find this command in the IBD diagram toolbar, Tools menu.
- The Autowire Parts , Delegate Port(s) , and Behavior to Structure Synchronization commands have been grouped under Tools in the diagram toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='delegate ports_btos_autowire_under_tools.png']
- You can now easily check if the Item Flow realized by the Object Flow is compatible with Pin types in a SysML Activity Diagram.
- You can now easily check the sent Signal compatibility with the Proxy Port specified in the **On Port** property of the Send Signal action or the compatibility of the accepted Signal with the Proxy Port specified in the **Port** property of the Accept Event action in a SysML Activity Diagram.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2021xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can now automatically build the structure model according to defined Activities and, at the same time, generate an Internal Block Diagram by executing a single-click command. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27fa6b18-6861-466b-b64c-986e1ea8a1e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Zuphq7gFL1w" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="btos_version_news.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Checking the consistency of the Internal Block Diagram with Activities and then handling detected inconsistencies using solvers.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about behavior to structure synhronization>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Requirements Verification in Table</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Requirements verification can now be performed directly in the Requirement Table. The analysis is carried out by evaluating whether the value of the property satisfying the Requirement is within a range of lower and upper bounds extracted from the Requirement text. Previously, only Modelica Library import was available. Now you can import the Modelica file (*.mo) content into the single selected Block of your SysML model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">During the import, context-specific initial values can be updated or missing classifiers can be created.</span> Furthermore, connector, property, and port changes are also supported.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Modelica import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Modelica import >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><strong>Extending models with Comments</strong></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can extend the model generated as a Modelica code, using a Comment element with the «ModelicaExtension» stereotype.
