You can find this command in the IBD diagram toolbar, Tools menu.</li><li data-uuid="17082ae6-7be1-42c8-89fd-2d8edca5490f">The <strong>Autowire Parts</strong>, <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong>, and <strong>Behavior to Structure Synchronization</strong> commands have been grouped under Tools in the diagram toolbar.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delegate ports_btos_autowire_under_tools.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="a7a4ee90-219b-4662-abc9-6c208614c0f0"><p>You can now easily check if the Item Flow realized by the Object Flow is compatible with Pin types in a SysML Activity Diagram.</p></li><li data-uuid="74b0d0f2-6d33-4ac0-bead-54ffbe26e577"><p>You can now easily check the sent Signal compatibility with the Proxy Port specified in the <strong>On Port</strong> property of the Send Signal action or the compatibility of the accepted Signal with the Proxy Port specified in the <strong>Port</strong> property of the Accept Event action in a SysML Activity Diagram.</p></li></ul><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="945daa58-ea73-42a2-86e4-eb228c8b6e5b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021xR2" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2021x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624198 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: (2021x Refresh2) UAF Plugin

- page_id: `261624198`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624198/2021x+Refresh2+UAF+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:42:49.418+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x Refresh2) 2021x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: December 3, 2021

The release of 2021x Refresh2 introduces the UAF 1.2 Plugin as a technology preview to support UAF 1.2. In this version, the UAF 1.2 Profile support (however it is supported only in the UAF framework), migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2, and UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements were implemented. Furthermore, the ArchiMate (technology preview) brings more element mapping options.

To download the latest MagicDraw version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. Don't forget to give us your feedback on[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574),[Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc), or[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/). For further information,check the.

##### UAF 1.2 Support (Technology Preview)

In 2021x Refresh 2, the UAF 1.2 support is introduced as a technology preview.

For this release, the separate UAF 1.2 Plugin is included in Cameo Enterprise Architecture and Magic Systems of Systems Architect modeling tools. This plugin is only for preview purposes and should **NOT** be used in production.

What is implemented for this release:

- UAF 1.2 Profile support, however, it is supported only in the UAF framework
- Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements:
  - Architecture Management Domain
  - Improvements in Strategic and Services Domains (clarify semantics, add new concepts, improve exposition)
  - Support of Value Streams
  - Risk becomes a cross-cutting construct

>]]>

The development will continue for the upcoming releases and will focus on the fully UAF 1.2 support for DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0 MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks and migration from UPDM to UAF 1.2.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.2 grid. 
(Click the image to enlarge)

##### ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)

In this version, we continued working on the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project. The imported element mapping is still only partial, but more mapping options were implemented. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing from ArchiMate' space='UAFP2021xR2']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Plugin Documentation' space='UAFP2021xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: December 3, <span>2021</span></span></h6><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The release of 2021x Refresh2 introduces the UAF 1.2 Plugin as a technology preview to support UAF 1.2. In this version, the UAF 1.2 Profile support (however it is supported only in the UAF framework), migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2, and UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements were implemented. Furthermore, the ArchiMate (technology preview) brings more element mapping options.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest MagicDraw version, see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. Don't forget to give us your feedback on </span><a class="external-link" href="https://www.linkedin.com/groups/3736574" rel="nofollow">LinkedIn</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, </span><a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc" rel="nofollow">Twitter</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, or </span><a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/" rel="nofollow">Facebook</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. For further information, </span>check the<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[product documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>UAF 1.2 Support (Technology Preview)</h3><p>In 2021x Refresh 2, the UAF 1.2 support is introduced as a technology preview.</p><p>For this release, the separate UAF 1.2 Plugin is included in Cameo Enterprise Architecture and Magic Systems of Systems Architect modeling tools. This plugin is only for preview purposes and should <u><strong>NOT</strong></u> be used in production.</p><p>What is implemented for this release:</p><ul><li data-uuid="68c14a5c-39d2-4179-8e30-db3f7b69ef34">UAF 1.2 Profile support, however, it is supported only in the UAF framework</li><li data-uuid="996d408b-5560-4d31-9538-6cd473aaaacc">Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2</li><li data-uuid="466f9ea9-ca39-46cf-b9f5-469be489c1f4">UAF Grid and Metamodel Improvements:<ul><li>Architecture Management Domain</li><li>Improvements in Strategic and Services Domains (clarify semantics, add new concepts, improve exposition)</li><li>Support of Value Streams</li><li>Risk becomes a cross-cutting construct</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="UAF12P2021xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See the UAF 1.2 Plugin documentation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The development will continue for the upcoming releases and will focus on the fully UAF 1.2 support for DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0 MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks and migration from UPDM to UAF 1.2.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">UAF 1.2 grid.<br />(Click the image to enlarge)</h6><h3>ArchiMate Model Import (Technology Preview)</h3><p>In this version, we continued working on the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project. The imported element mapping is still only partial, but more mapping options were implemented. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.1 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR2" ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1100"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NATO-Architecture-Framework-horizontal-white-background.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x Refresh2) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="85f01092-0b1a-4118-b4cd-43315f3d5168"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021xR2" ri:content-title="UAF Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF Plugin 2021x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622684 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00050: (2021x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `261622684`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622684/2021x+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:19:29.393+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2021x brings not only a number of new functionalities but also multiple improvements to the features that you already know. With a new version of the product, you can save Cameo Collaborator comments as UML elements in your project, export documents to PDF and HTML formats, add new values to a project right from a Cameo Collaborator document, and more.

##### Saving Comments as Model Elements

In the previous version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you could work with Collaborator comments in a modeling tool by using the comments panel. However, comments were stored in a Cameo Collaborator document, so you could not use all the benefits of your modeling tool capabilities. In this version of the product, it is possible to save Cameo Collaborator comments as model elements in a project and work with them as you would with any other UML elements. In addition, comments are automatically synchronized between your project and a Cameo Collaborator document every time you commit changes to the server or update the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example shows how you can create comments as model elements and work with them in a modeling tool.

This new functionality opens endless possibilities allowing you to:

- Create new textual Cameo Collaborator comments and reply to existing ones right in a modeling tool.
- Modify comments, e.g., change comment status, priority, and even the commented element in the comment Specification window. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_specification.png']
- Validate if all comments have been created correctly and have all the required metadata by using the Collaborator Comments in Project validation suite.
- Create comment reports displaying Cameo Collaborator comments and their detailed information, such as an author, status, priority, etc. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_report.png']
- Calculate metrics allowing you to see how many comments are created and resolved or create your own metrics. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_metrics.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x LTR Version News' space='CC4TWC2021x']

##### Exporting Documents to PDF and HTML

In this release of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, we are happy to present a new functionality allowing you to export documents to PDF and HTML formats. This makes information exchange a lot easier because you can save a Cameo Collaborator document as a file and share it via email or any file sharing system. Also, customize a document file by adding a cover page and the table of contents, or choosing what information should be exported. You can export the whole document or just a part of it, such as selected tables, paragraphs, or a specific view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example shows a Cameo Collaborator document exported to the PDF format. Only an active document section has been exported.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x LTR Version News' space='CC4TWC2021x']

##### New Model Editing Capabilities in Cameo Collaborator

The model editing in Cameo Collaborator functionality has been extended even further. Now you can create values for stereotype tags, the Documentation property, and other textual properties (except for slot values) right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool to add new values to your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example shows how to create a new stereotype tag value right in a Cameo Collaborator document.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x LTR Version News' space='CC4TWC2021x']

##### Disk Space Usage Improvements

In this release of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the number of elements in documents has been significantly reduced and now Cameo Collaborator documents take 40-50% less space in the Teamwork Cloud server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This chart demonstrates significantly decreased disk space usage in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 20.0 after publishing projects of different sizes.

##### Improved Publishing Performance

In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2021x, the publishing performance has been significantly improved and now takes less time as shown in the charts below. In addition, the amount of memory you need to allocate to your modeling tool for publishing has decreased.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This chart compares the publishing time between product versions 19.0 SP4 and 2021x.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### In this chart, you can see that updating Collaborator documents takes less time compared to the earlier versions of the product.

##### Other

- Email notification templates have been redesigned and now have a completely new modern look. In addition, you can edit these templates to customize email notification appearance. [ATTACHMENT filename='email_notification.png']
- If you are new to working with custom document templates, check our new Cameo Collaborator Template Samples project wich you can find in your modeling tool installation directory . It provides different document template samples and gives step by step instructions on how to create them.
- Now subscribing to email notifications is more convenient because you can do it right from the Resources application. [ATTACHMENT filename='subscribing_to_email_notifications.png']

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='CC4TWC2021x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Now you can create values for stereotype tags, the Documentation property, and other textual properties (except for slot values) right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool to add new values to your model.</p><p><br /></p><p class="item" style="text-align: center;"><span class="name" style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);" title="Feature Description List"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_new_values.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 class="item" style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);">This example shows how to create a new stereotype tag value right in a Cameo Collaborator document.</span></h6><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="2021x LTR Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Disk Space Usage Improvements</h3><p>In this release of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the number of elements in documents has been significantly reduced and now Cameo Collaborator documents take 40-50% less space in the Teamwork Cloud server.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="disk_space_usage_after_publishing.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This chart demonstrates significantly decreased disk space usage in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 20.0 after publishing projects of different sizes.</h6><h3>Improved Publishing Performance</h3><p>In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2021x, the publishing performance has been significantly improved and now takes less time as shown in the charts below. It provides different document template samples and gives step by step instructions on how to create them.</span></li><li data-uuid="5c7b9362-ed30-4f8b-a2b3-43ef1e3c0562">Now subscribing to email notifications is more convenient because you can do it right from the Resources application.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="subscribing_to_email_notifications.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="c8cf2eea-3585-44c0-915d-7ceeccc9561d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2021x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><p />

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622710 space=VN version=3 -->
## PAGE 00051: (2021x) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `261622710`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622710/2021x+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:18:43.414+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

Cameo DataHub 2021x has now been ultimately innovated to the next level of DOORS and DOORS Next Generation Data Source, module, and External Link implementation and integration with Enovia traceable Requirement management, as noticeably development in their co-working components through opening another Data Source, ancestors derived from customizing filtered Requirements, the hierarchy of synchronized links and module artifact open in a new tab, and helpful External Link display and navigation. Furthermore, performance regarding DOORS synchronization, actions done with DHLinks, and working with large models has been significantly improved. Get it today on [nomagic.com](http://www.nomagic.com/) or contact your sales representative, and don't forget to give us your feedback on [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc) or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/nminc.co). Also, please check the latest and additional .

##### Integration with Enovia Traceable Requirement Management (TRM)

The latest application to be supported by Cameo DataHub is Enovia Traceable Requirement Management (TRM). TRM is the requirement management tool on the 3DEXPERENCE platform. All data copy and synchronization features of Cameo DataHub that work with DOORS and CSV files are also available for TRM Data Sources. Moreover, Single Sign On and SAML for passing authorization credentials to the platform are supported. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Data Sources for Enovia TRM' space='CDH2021x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The ENOVIA Traceable Requirements Management driver is available in the Add Data Source dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The 3DEXPERIENCE login dialog prior to creating the ENOVIA Traceable Requirements Management Data Source.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The ENOVIA Traceable Requirements Management Data Source is created after successful login.

##### DOORS Next Generation components listed in DataHub Explorer

When connecting with the DOORS Next Generation Data Source, DataHub now lists components under the project in Cameo DataHub Explorer, while the main Data Source tree still displays artifacts from default components. You can also open another component as another Data Source. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening Components as a Data Source' space='CDH2021x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening another component as another Data Source with components listed under the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The component after open as another Data Source.

##### Links and the hierarchy in DOORS View Data Source supported

When the DOORS View Data Source is created, it can now display its links that can also be synchronized.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A DOORS View Data Source is created and shows its links in the hierarchy.

Moreover, you can customize filtered Requirements in the View Data Source to show all of their ancestors in the tree hierarchy. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying ancestors of filtered Requirements in DOORS View Data Source' space='CDH2021x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the Show ancestors command to display all ancestors of the filtered Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All ancestors of the filtered Requirements are displayed.

##### A new way of opening the module artifact of DOORS Next Generation

For IBM Engineering Requirements Management with DOORS Next Generation, you will be able to create a module, add artifacts, and modify the hierarchy of those artifacts displayed and synchronized to the model. The DOORS Next Generation module artifact can also be open as a separate tree in a new tab by using the **Open module in new window** command. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Module artifacts of DOORS Next Generation' space='CDH2021x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the Open module in new window command to open a DOORS Next Generation module artifact in a new tab.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The selected DOORS Next Generation module artifact is open in a new tab.

##### Synchronizing DOORS External Links that can navigate to related model elements

After Requirements are synchronized from DOORS, they can have relationships, e.g., **Satisfy** or **Derive**, with other elements in the model. In this version, DataHub can synchronize these relationships back to DOORS and create External Links that help display and navigate to those relationships with related model elements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Synchronizing DOORS External Links' space='CDH2021x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating and updating External Links to other elements with relationships in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### External Links display and navigate to relationships with related model elements.

##### DOORS synchronization performance improvement

DataHub has made the behavior of retrieving data from the DOORS module more efficient. The currently working module can now remain open in read-only mode to help reduce time workload. With this new adjustment, DOORS synchronization performance has been enhanced by 40%.

##### Performance improved on the actions done with DHLinks

The performance of DataHub actions through all DHLinks at the root node is now significantly improved, including **Removing DHLinks**, **Clear Suspect status**, **Update pending**, and **Check Changes**.

##### Overall performance improved when using DataHub on large models

Now DataHub can be deployed on projects with 5 to 10 million size with less time required when used on large models.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A line chart displaying the performance of DataHub 19.0 SP4 and 2021x operations measured in seconds with large models and sample Requirements.

##### Key issues fixed

- No error is displayed when selecting DOORS, DOORS Next Generation, Enovia, or TRM nodes with the OSLC preview tab open.
- It is now possible to synchronize the changes of moved elements in MagicDraw to the TRM side.
- The OAuth Verification page can now be displayed normally, required that the certificate has been installed to Java and trusted root certification authorities.

#### INFO: Information

Information

You can check [the list](https://support.nomagic.com/secure/IssueNavigator.jspa?reset=true&&pid=10082&resolution=7&fixfor=11490&sorter/field=issuekey&sorter/order=DESC&sorter/field=priority&sorter/order=DESC) of publicly available issues or your own reported issues fixed in Cameo DataHub 2021x.

**Note**: You will be required to login. Use the same username and password as for[www.nomagic.com](https://www.magicdraw.com/main.php?ts=login&cmd_show_login=1&show_confirm=1&menu=login).

##### resourcesDocumentation

- Cameo DataHub 2021x

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c56b327-fe27-4089-8113-c912c4c28ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: February 12, 2021</h6><p><br /></p><p>Cameo DataHub 2021x has now been ultimately innovated to the next level of DOORS and DOORS Next Generation Data Source, module, and External Link implementation and integration with Enovia traceable Requirement management, as noticeably development in their co-working components through opening another Data Source, ancestors derived from customizing filtered Requirements, the hierarchy of synchronized links and module artifact open in a new tab, and helpful External Link display and navigation. Furthermore, performance regarding DOORS synchronization, actions done with DHLinks, and working with large models has been significantly improved. Get it today on <a href="http://www.nomagic.com/">nomagic.com</a> or contact your sales representative, and don't forget to give us your feedback on <a href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc">Twitter</a> or <a href="https://www.facebook.com/nminc.co">Facebook</a>. Also, please check the latest <ac:link ac:anchor="resources"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and additional <ac:link ac:anchor="resources" />.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Integration with Enovia Traceable Requirement Management (TRM)</h3><p>The latest application to be supported by Cameo DataHub is Enovia Traceable Requirement Management (TRM). TRM is the requirement management tool on the 3DEXPERENCE platform. All data copy and synchronization features of Cameo DataHub that work with DOORS and CSV files are also available for TRM Data Sources. Moreover, Single Sign On and SAML for passing authorization credentials to the platform are supported. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2021x" ri:content-title="Adding Data Sources for Enovia TRM" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Find out more about adding Data Sources for Enovia TRM >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Enovia Add Data Source Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">The ENOVIA Traceable Requirements Management driver is available in the Add Data Source dialog.</span></h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Embedded Browser.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">The 3DEXPERIENCE login dialog prior to creating the ENOVIA Traceable Requirements Management Data Source. </span></h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Enovia Data Source.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The ENOVIA Traceable Requirements Management Data Source is created after successful login.</h6><h3>DOORS Next Generation components listed in DataHub Explorer</h3><p>When connecting with the DOORS Next Generation Data Source, DataHub now lists components under the project in Cameo DataHub Explorer, while the main Data Source tree still displays artifacts from default components. You can also open another component as another Data Source. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2021x" ri:content-title="Opening Components as a Data Source" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about opening a Component as a new Data Source >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening Components as Another Data Sources.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Opening another component as another Data Source with components listed under the project.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Architecture Component After Open as Another Data Source.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The component after open as another Data Source.</h6><h3>Links and the hierarchy in DOORS View Data Source supported</h3><p>When the DOORS View Data Source is created, it can now display its links that can also be synchronized.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DOORS View Data Source Created.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A DOORS View Data Source is created and shows its links in the hierarchy.</h6><p>Moreover, you can customize filtered Requirements in the View Data Source to show all of their ancestors in the tree hierarchy. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2021x" ri:content-title="Displaying ancestors of filtered Requirements in DOORS View Data Source" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to display ancestors of Requirements in the DOORS View Data Source >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using Show Ancestors Command.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Using the Show ancestors command to display all ancestors of the filtered Requirements.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="All Ancestors of the Filtered Requirements Displayed.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All ancestors of the filtered Requirements are displayed.</h6><h3>A new way of opening the module artifact of DOORS Next Generation</h3><p>For IBM Engineering Requirements Management with DOORS Next Generation, you will be able to create a module, add artifacts, and modify the hierarchy of those artifacts displayed and synchronized to the model. The DOORS Next Generation module artifact can also be open as a separate tree in a new tab by using the <strong>Open module in new window</strong> command. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2021x" ri:content-title="Module artifacts of DOORS Next Generation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to open the module artifact of DOORS Next Generation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Module Open2.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Using the Open module in new window command to open a DOORS Next Generation module artifact in a new tab.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Module Open3.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The selected DOORS Next Generation module artifact is open in a new tab.</h6><h3>Synchronizing DOORS External Links that can navigate to related model elements</h3><p>After Requirements are synchronized from DOORS, they can have relationships, e.g., <strong>Satisfy</strong> or <strong>Derive</strong>, with other elements in the model. In this version, DataHub can synchronize these relationships back to DOORS and create External Links that help display and navigate to those relationships with related model elements. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2021x" ri:content-title="Synchronizing DOORS External Links" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more how to synchronize DOORS External Links >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="External Link.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating and updating External Links to other elements with relationships in the model.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="External Link2.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">External Links display and navigate to relationships with related model elements.</h6><h3>DOORS synchronization performance improvement</h3><p>DataHub has made the behavior of retrieving data from the DOORS module more efficient. The currently working module can now remain open in read-only mode to help reduce time workload. With this new adjustment, DOORS synchronization performance has been enhanced by 40%.</p><h3>Performance improved on the actions done with DHLinks</h3><p>The performance of DataHub actions through all DHLinks at the root node is now significantly improved, including <strong>Removing DHLinks</strong>, <strong>Clear Suspect status</strong>, <strong>Update pending</strong>, and <strong>Check Changes</strong>.</p><h3>Overall performance improved when using DataHub on large models</h3><p>Now DataHub can be deployed on projects with 5 to 10 million size with less time required when used on large models.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart1.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">A line chart displaying the performance of DataHub 19.0 SP4 and 2021x operations measured in seconds with large models and sample Requirements.</span></h6><h3>Key issues fixed</h3><ul><li data-uuid="a6960d68-8373-4fcc-b540-30800879a423">No error is displayed when selecting DOORS, DOORS Next Generation, Enovia, or TRM nodes with the OSLC preview tab open.</li><li data-uuid="2afac7bc-0925-484c-b9b5-ec3fca18966a">It is now possible to synchronize the changes of moved elements in MagicDraw to the TRM side.</li><li data-uuid="3000ab23-a02f-475b-940e-647ec05e3b04">The OAuth Verification page can now be displayed normally, required that the certificate has been installed to Java and trusted root certification authorities.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e227cf2-f858-478e-b88f-040b3a66b118"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">You can check <a href="https://support.nomagic.com/secure/IssueNavigator.jspa?reset=true&amp;&amp;pid=10082&amp;resolution=7&amp;fixfor=11490&amp;sorter/field=issuekey&amp;sorter/order=DESC&amp;sorter/field=priority&amp;sorter/order=DESC">the list</a> of publicly available issues or your own reported issues fixed in Cameo DataHub 2021x.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Note</strong>: You will be required to login. Use the same username and password as for </span><a href="https://www.magicdraw.com/main.php?ts=login&amp;cmd_show_login=1&amp;show_confirm=1&amp;menu=login">www.nomagic.com</a>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b45a5d9-f7c7-4214-8ad7-87de79d13d91"><ac:parameter ac:name="">resources</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="790da175-625a-4474-a65b-226622b115d1"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CDH2021x/Cameo+DataHub+Documentation">Cameo DataHub 2021x</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622708 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00053: (2021x) Concept Modeler Plugin

- page_id: `261622708`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622708/2021x+Concept+Modeler+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-09T14:19:53.041+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

##### New capabilities

- OWL export for concept models can now be automated using MagicDraw macros or the command line.
- A concept model can now be exported to description logic (DL) notation in HTML or LaTeX.

##### New concept model library for well known ontologies

- CCM now includes a ready-to-use library of concept models corresponding to the following ontologies:
  - Dublin core metadata initiative (DCMI) properties and classes from the terms and elements 1.1 namespaces
  - The World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) recommended Resource Description Framework (RDF), RDF Schema (RDFS), and Web Ontology Language 2 (OWL 2) reference ontologies
  - Basic Formal Ontology (BFO) 2020 (ISO/IEC 21838-2)
  - The Open Biological and Biomedical Ontology (OBO) Foundry recommended Basic Formal Ontology (BFO) 2 upper level ontology
  - The Common Core Ontologies (CCO) 1.2
- The DCMI and W3C ontologies are now available in every new project by default.
- The BFO 2, the CCO and the BFO 2020 ontologies can be added to any CCM project as needed.
- When importing a user ontology, the CCM will now detect and add any references to the model elements from the standard library.
- Templates are now available for creating a project based on BFO, CCO, or DOLCE

##### New samples

- A 'Fair Trade Coffee' concept model provides a rudimentary definition of a fair trade coffee plantation.
- A 'Jurisdiction' concept model provides a jurisdiction-dependent definition of a child worker for the 'Fair Trade Coffee' model.

##### Usability improvements

- Freeze IRIs or Unfreeze IRIs context menu items now appear instantaneously, even for large models.
- Freeze IRIs and Unfreeze IRIs operations now display a progress bar.
- The Concept Modeling Diagrams section now appears at the beginning in the Create Diagrams context menu for «Concept Model» or «Model» packages.
- The containment tree now offers a context menu item that loads the profiles needed to make a Concept Modeling project.
- Upon creating a Concept Model using the "Create Concept Model" context menu item, the generated name is selected for editing.
- The «Annotation Property» stereotype is now visible when it is applied to a property.
- An «Equivalent Class» generalization is now drawn as an oblique line.
- Dragging a named property onto another property now displays a clearer tooltip of the available menu items.
- Importing and exporting individuals has now graduated to be a non-experimental feature.
- Selecting the top node in the property selection step of the information model transformation wizard now selects all its children recursively.

##### OWL import improvements

- An OWL objectOneOf restriction is now interpreted as an unnamed «Individual List» Class.
- A clearer error message is now shown if an owl:imports statement cannot be resolved.

##### OWL export improvements

- A «Same As» stereotyped dependency is now exported as an OWL SameIndividual axiom.
- A «Different From» stereotyped dependency is now exported as a pairwise OWL DifferentIndividuals axiom.

##### OWL export improvements

- Exporting an unnamed Association Class now emits a warning that the class will be ignored.

##### Model understandability improvements

- «Restriction» and «External Reference» stereotypes no longer clutter diagrams when initially applied to an element.

##### Natural Language Glossary improvements

- The project option for "Include property definitions in the Natural Language Glossary" is now set to true for new projects.
- The "Model-Generated Definition" subsection now includes definitions for unions, intersections, individual lists, domains, ranges, and «Self» restrictions.
- The "Model-Generated Definition" subsection now has updated definitions for complements, disjoints, equivalent classes, and restrictions.
- Each role entry definition now shows applicable domain, range, inverse, subproperty, subproperty chain, equivalent properties, and property characteristics.

##### Bug fixes

- Exporting an association with a datatype on one end no longer fails with an exception.
- Empty intersections or unions are no longer exported.
- An unnamed «Anything» class now shows the correct generated class description.
- An import summary message is no longer intermixed with other import notifications.
- Opening a project no longer results in a NullPointerException due to a missing 'Preferred annotation property' project option.
- Stepping back to the beginning of the information model transformation wizard now shows the selected information model package.
- Adding a new subproperty now updates the Subproperty Tree.
- A subclass is no longer exported as part of a union when a complete generalization set exists and its generalization is not part of the set.
- The Concept Modeling Support Library is now added to the project when creating a concept modeling diagram in a non-CCM project.
- The Create Concept Model context menu item no longer shows under a read-only package, a «Concept Model» package, or a «Model» package.
- Importing a named subproperty with a range of owl:Thing resulting in a min 0 cardinality no longer emits a warning that the cardinality is meaningless.
- Individuals from indirectly imported ontologies are no longer ignored.
- Round tripping a redundant qualified cardinality restriction through CCM is no longer optimized away.
- Adding the UPCM library during migration of a project on TWC no longer fails.
- An annotation on a foreign element no longer automatically moves into the same package as the annotated element.
- Importing a full disjoint mesh over a class's subclasses now results in a Generalization Set marked {incomplete,disjoint}.
- Automatically generated association end property names are no longer duplicated within an owning element.
- Converting from the «Superclass Intersection» notation no longer applies the «Necessary & Sufficient» stereotype to an «Equivalent Class» generalization.
- Importing from OWL a datatype property that has an anonymous list of literals as its range no longer re-exports as a named list of literals to OWL.
- Generated descriptions of anonymous classes in the containment tree are now correctly updated when the model changes.
- Invalid keyboard shortcuts no longer clutter 'Create Concept Model' and 'Export Model to OWL' context menu items.

You can check[the list](https://support.nomagic.com/secure/IssueNavigator.jspa?reset=true&&pid=10280&resolution=7&fixfor=11489&sorter/field=issuekey&sorter/order=DESC&sorter/field=priority&sorter/order=DESC)of publicly available issues or your own reported issues fixed in Cameo Concept Modeler Plugin 19.0 SP4. 
**Note:**You will be required to login. Use the same username and password as for[www.nomagic.com](https://www.magicdraw.com/main.php?ts=login&cmd_show_login=1&show_confirm=1&menu=login).

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Concept Modeler Documentation' space='CCMP2021x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Web Ontology Language 2 (OWL 2) reference ontologies</li><li>Basic Formal Ontology (BFO) 2020 (ISO/IEC 21838-2)</li><li>The Open Biological and Biomedical Ontology (OBO) Foundry recommended Basic Formal Ontology (BFO) 2 upper level ontology</li><li>The Common Core Ontologies (CCO) 1.2</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="70e005b7-b862-44a3-be68-9907fe9de5cb">The DCMI and W3C ontologies are now now offers a context menu item that loads the profiles needed to make a Concept Modeling project.</li><li data-uuid="f76a02a0-3104-4c62-aaa1-7acad781cf72">Upon creating a Concept Model using the &quot;Create Concept Model&quot; context menu item, the generated name is selected for editing.</li><li data-uuid="8aa2f74c-cb33-4bff-8b37-35f349a619e8">The «Annotation Property» stereotype is now visible when it is applied to a property.</li><li data-uuid="e6e14327-53e3-4984-9e47-46aafc53cab3">An «Equivalent Class» generalization is now drawn as an oblique line.</li><li data-uuid="cd3a0103-fbb3-4796-b0fc-3b4e63187e99">Dragging a named property onto another property now displays a clearer tooltip of the available menu items.</li><li Language Glossary&quot; is now set to true for new projects.</li><li data-uuid="35f11b1f-79de-42b0-afec-61e258f3742f">The &quot;Model-Generated Definition&quot; subsection now includes definitions for unions, intersections, individual lists, domains, ranges, and «Self» restrictions.</li><li data-uuid="5c3ab33c-bfc6-41a2-8805-8f10e6146792">The &quot;Model-Generated Definition&quot; subsection now domain, range, inverse, subproperty, subproperty chain, equivalent properties, and property characteristics.</li></ul><h3>Bug fixes</h3><ul><li data-uuid="ae003060-b543-4294-9a5d-e7e883ea2e61">Exporting an association with a datatype on one end no longer fails with an exception.</li><li data-uuid="5f87a6c8-0f53-4803-9101-2fd77af75dfa">Empty intersections or unions are no longer exported.</li><li data-uuid="17cd7106-21b7-4f3f-88e7-dcf4bd86a7c5">An unnamed «Anything» class now shows the correct generated class description.</li><li data-uuid="2ec85ecc-1147-4c12-9736-961205005789">An import summary message is no longer intermixed with other import notifications.</li><li data-uuid="8a32202c-3d8a-4926-a76e-b20c8d216cf2">Opening a project no longer results in a NullPointerException due to a missing 'Preferred annotation property' project option.</li><li data-uuid="3a6f241f-88c3-4f4b-84c3-1e6630667a5a">Stepping back to the beginning of the information model transformation wizard now shows the selected information model package.</li><li data-uuid="daa397f1-5f33-4694-ba31-12cffb417396">Adding a new subproperty now updates the Subproperty Tree.</li><li data-uuid="8ffe9507-4479-4e95-b68f-5220364396fd">A subclass is no longer exported as part of a union when a complete generalization set exists and its generalization is not part of the set.</li><li data-uuid="91c1c402-c769-4fe0-87bd-5561db80169c">The Concept Modeling Support Library is now added to the project when creating a concept modeling diagram in a non-CCM project.</li><li data-uuid="2d7332fe-6479-4aac-b0cf-edf789ba860a">The Create Concept Model context menu item no longer shows under a read-only package, a «Concept Model» package, or a «Model» package.</li><li data-uuid="0b611169-5bae-4a18-959c-be95cef262ed">Importing a named subproperty with a range of owl:Thing resulting in a min 0 cardinality no longer emits a warning that the cardinality is meaningless.</li><li data-uuid="0dc2b158-ad48-42ce-81e5-5ebe4b06ef3c">Individuals from indirectly imported ontologies are no longer ignored.</li><li data-uuid="77ce8da5-48ab-4d2b-a512-8959f1d1cc7b">Round tripping a redundant qualified cardinality restriction through CCM is no longer optimized away.</li><li data-uuid="bb6cf462-67eb-4430-b477-d9f24511c531">Adding the UPCM library during migration of a project on TWC no longer fails.</li><li data-uuid="681fa2c5-c677-442e-948c-b1069f52e7ba">An annotation on a foreign element no longer automatically moves into the same package as the annotated element.</li><li data-uuid="2fd0d63a-67a3-4d82-9ee5-e26433309b49">Importing a full disjoint mesh over a class's subclasses now results in a Generalization Set marked {incomplete,disjoint}.</li><li data-uuid="2654d00d-f15f-4591-b037-61a4154087bb">Automatically generated association end property names are no longer duplicated within an owning element.</li><li data-uuid="a36c28ad-a3a1-458f-9eae-165e79f62acf">Converting from the «Superclass Intersection» notation no longer applies the «Necessary &amp; Sufficient» stereotype to an «Equivalent Class» generalization.</li><li data-uuid="87c45edd-44f6-4e98-b966-8b94e6f61709">Importing from OWL a datatype property that has an anonymous list of literals as its range no longer re-exports as a named list of literals to OWL.</li><li data-uuid="42b8bff2-14a8-4de0-83db-f375f02b9373">Generated descriptions of anonymous classes in the containment tree are now correctly updated when the model changes.</li><li data-uuid="05c76e9c-aec5-494a-809a-c92d3d3222fb">Invalid keyboard shortcuts no longer clutter 'Create Concept Model' and 'Export Model to OWL' context menu items.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5995f486-71ef-4847-9ef2-71545df1f999"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">You can check </span><a href="https://support.nomagic.com/secure/IssueNavigator.jspa?reset=true&amp;&amp;pid=10280&amp;resolution=7&amp;fixfor=11489&amp;sorter/field=issuekey&amp;sorter/order=DESC&amp;sorter/field=priority&amp;sorter/order=DESC">the list</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> of publicly available issues or your own reported issues fixed in Cameo Concept Modeler Plugin 19.0 SP4. </span><br /><strong>Note:</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> You will be required to login. Use the same username and password as for </span><a href="https://www.magicdraw.com/main.php?ts=login&amp;cmd_show_login=1&amp;show_confirm=1&amp;menu=login">www.nomagic.com</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="9c90e515-9555-4143-b92e-22ec01d96a24"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CCMP2021x" ri:content-title="Cameo Concept Modeler Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Concept Modeler Plugin 2021x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622814 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00054: (2021x) Magic Alf Analyst / Alf Plugin

- page_id: `261622814`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622814/2021x+Magic+Alf+Analyst+Alf+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-09T14:19:55.995+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### top

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

The Alf Plugin Version 2021x eliminates the need for Receptions when sending or accepting Signals using Alf and provides bug fixes.

##### Sending and accepting Signals without the need for Receptions

The Alf language specification requires that the target of a Signal-send expression be a Reception and that the context Class of an accept statement has Receptions for all accepted Signals. However, the use of Receptions are optional in UML, and their use is no longer required in Foundational UML (fUML) 1.5 for executable models. Therefore, the Alf Plugin now implements a language extension that allows sending and accepting Signals without the need of using Expressions. For a Signal send, the name of the Signal must be visible without qualification, which may require that the Signal (or its containing Package) be imported. In an accept statement, Signals may be referenced using qualified names, if their simple names are not directly visible.

The new capability is particularly useful for sending Signals through Ports. A Signal send can simply target a Port using any visible Signal to be sent out through that Port. Note, however, that the Port must still have a type, generally either an Interface or a Class with a required Interface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Sending Signals without the need for receptions.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Making invocations through ports' space='ALFP2021x']>]]>

##### Bug fixes

The following bugs have been fixed in the Alf Plugin 2021x:

- A confusing error message related to an illegal data value update.
- A potential exception due to a concurrency conflict when repainting the UI.

In addition, the Alf compiler has been updated to Alf Reference Implementation v1.1.0k.

You can check [the list](https://support.nomagic.com/secure/IssueNavigator.jspa?reset=true&&pid=10082&resolution=7&fixfor=11490&sorter/field=issuekey&sorter/order=DESC&sorter/field=priority&sorter/order=DESC) of publicly available issues or your own reported issues fixed in Alf Plugin 2021x. 
**Note:** You will be required to login. Use the same username and password as for [www.nomagic.com](https://www.magicdraw.com/main.php?ts=login&cmd_show_login=1&show_confirm=1&menu=login).

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Alf Analyst Documentation' space='MAF2021x']
- Alf Plugin 2021x

##### Other resources

- Modeling Community Blogs
- Modeling Community Forum
- Webinars
- FAQ

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Therefore, the Alf Plugin now implements a language extension that allows sending and accepting Signals without the need of using Expressions. In an accept statement, Signals may be referenced using qualified names, if their simple names are not directly visible.</p><p>The new capability is particularly useful for sending Signals through Ports. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622640 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00055: (2021x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `261622640`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622640/2021x+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:19:12.483+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

The 2021x release takes a huge leap towards its enterprise readiness. It comes with major improvements in the ability to better handle and manage large amounts of data. Efficient collaboration is now ensured even under extremely high server load while keeping the disk footprint at a very minimum.

Enhancements to Teamwork Cloud RBAC mechanism now allows managing access rights in bulk via category-level permissions or at a fine-grained level via branch-level permissions.

A brand new Resource Usage Map web application can help you quickly analyze large amounts of inter-linked server projects and investigate potentially problematic usage areas.

Cameo Collaborator web application brings not only a number of new functionalities but also multiple improvements to the features that you already know. With a new version of the product, you can save Cameo Collaborator comments as UML elements in your project, export documents to PDF and HTML formats, add new values to a project right from a Cameo Collaborator document, and more.

#### Performance Improvements in TWC Core

##### TWC Disk Footprint Improvements

Major TWC disk footprint-related changes are presented in this release. TWC 2021x requires twice as less disk space for the same amount of projects with no history and up to 7 times less memory for projects with existing history compared to TWC 19.0 SP4. In addition to this achievement, the disk size growth is cut nearly 5 times in TWC 2021x compared to TWC 19.0 SP4 for those projects users work actively on.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing TWC disk footprint between different modeling tool versions.

- A database of 50 projects with 200 commits each was used to run the tests.
- Project sizes ranged between 400k and 2.2M elements (average project size being 700k elements).

##### TWC Scalability Improvements

TWC 2021x delivers significant improvements in scalability when handling a large amount of concurrent users actively working with server projects. In particular, project commit and update operation performance under high load remains stable even with 200 simultaneous users on a single node setup. The following charts depict combined commit and update times from a single user's perspective going up to 200 concurrent users repository-wise.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Combined commit and update times on a single node setup.

Additionally, TWC's internal load balancer has been tweaked to achieve higher effectiveness when under extremely large loads of up to 400 concurrent users.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Combined commit and update times on a 3 node TWC and Cassandra cluster.

#### Resource Usage Map

We are proud to present Resource Usage Map - a new web application that comes with Teamwork Cloud 2021x. Resource Usage Map allows you to create graphical representations of Teamwork Cloud project usages, analyze dependencies among projects in the Teamwork Cloud repository, and identify potentially problematic usage areas.

In Resource Usage Map you can:

- Create usage maps for one or multiple resources.
- Quickly access the resource usage map of an open project right from a modeling tool.
- Identify three types of suspicious usages: inconsistent version usages, cyclic usages, and unused resources.
- Use filters to choose what types of usages you want to see in a usage map.
- Search for resources in a large usage map and view their details in the Resource pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Resource Usage Map application displaying the usage map of five selected resources.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuring Resource Usage Map URL' space='MD2021x']>]]>

#### Category-Level Permissions

Now you can manage Teamwork Cloud access rights more effectively by using category-level permissions. When role scope is category-specific, the role permissions apply to all resources stored in the selected category.This means that new resources added to the category automatically inherit the role with all its assignments. This can save a lot of your time because you no longer have to assign new resources inside the category manually.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### You can assign a category to a specific role the same way you assign resources.

#### Branch-Level Permissions

Teamwork Cloud 2021x allows you to limit the scope of a role assigned to a user or user group not only to specific resources but to specific branches of a resource as well. For example, you can allow an engineer to work only on a specific branch while prohibiting access to any other resource branches he is not supposed to edit. This enables a strict branch-per-feature methodology resulting in better work discipline among different team members.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Specifying branch-level permissions.

#### LDAP Server Configuration Changes

An LDAP server query for searching, retrieving, and importing users has been split into Users query and User groups query for more accurate search results when querying an LDAP server. It is important that you update User and User groups queries after upgrading your Teamwork Cloud instance to version 20.0.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the LDAP server configuration page.

#### REST API Change Log

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='REST API Change Log' space='TWCloud2021x'] concerns the following areas:

- Changes in data types.
- URLs relocated.
- New URLs.
- API documentation (for more clarified information).

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x LTR Version News' space='CC4TWC2021x']

#### Cameo Collaborator

##### Saving Comments as Model Elements

In the previous version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you could work with Collaborator comments in a modeling tool by using the comments panel. However, comments were stored in a Cameo Collaborator document, so you could not use all the benefits of your modeling tool capabilities. In this version of the product, it is possible to save Cameo Collaborator comments as model elements in a project and work with them as you would with any other UML elements. In addition, comments are automatically synchronized between your project and a Cameo Collaborator document every time you commit changes to the server or update the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example shows how you can create comments as model elements and work with them in a modeling tool.

This new functionality opens endless possibilities allowing you to:

- Create new textual Cameo Collaborator comments and reply to existing ones right in a modeling tool.
- Modify comments, e.g., change comment status, priority, and even the commented element in the comment Specification window. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_specification.png']
- Validate if all comments have been created correctly and have all the required metadata by using the Collaborator Comments in Project validation suite.
- Create comment reports displaying Cameo Collaborator comments and their detailed information, such as an author, status, priority, etc. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_report.png']
- Calculate metrics allowing you to see how many comments are created and resolved or create your own metrics. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_metrics.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x LTR Version News' space='CC4TWC2021x']

##### Exporting Documents to PDF and HTML

In this release of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, we are happy to present a new functionality allowing you to export documents to PDF and HTML formats. This makes information exchange a lot easier because you can save a Cameo Collaborator document as a file and share it via email or any file sharing system. Also, customize a document file by adding a cover page and the table of contents, or choosing what information should be exported. You can export the whole document or just a part of it, such as selected tables, paragraphs, or a specific view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example shows a Cameo Collaborator document exported to the PDF format. Only an active document section has been exported.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x LTR Version News' space='CC4TWC2021x']

##### New Model Editing Capabilities in Cameo Collaborator

The model editing in Cameo Collaborator functionality has been extended even further. Now you can create values for stereotype tags, the Documentation property, and other textual properties (except for slot values) right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool to add new values to your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example shows how to create a new stereotype tag value right in a Cameo Collaborator document.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x LTR Version News' space='CC4TWC2021x']

##### Disk Space Usage Improvements

In this release of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the number of elements in documents has been significantly reduced and now Cameo Collaborator documents take 40-50% less space in the Teamwork Cloud server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This chart demonstrates significantly decreased disk space usage in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 20.0 after publishing projects of different sizes.

##### Improved Publishing Performance

In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2021x, the publishing performance has been significantly improved and now takes less time as shown in the charts below. In addition, the amount of memory you need to allocate to your modeling tool for publishing has decreased.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This chart compares the publishing time between product versions 19.0 SP4 and 2021x.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### In this chart, you can see that updating Collaborator documents takes less time compared to the earlier versions of the product.

##### Other

- Email notification templates have been redesigned and now have a completely new modern look. In addition, you can edit these templates to customize email notification appearance. [ATTACHMENT filename='email_notification.png']
- If you are new to working with custom document templates, check our new Cameo Collaborator Template Samples project wich you can find in your modeling tool installation directory . It provides different document template samples and gives step by step instructions on how to create them.
- Now subscribing to email notifications is more convenient because you can do it right from the Resources application. [ATTACHMENT filename='subscribing_to_email_notifications.png']

#### Other Enhancements

- Secure SSL communication can now be set up between Teamwork Cloud and Cassandra. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud advanced configuration' space='TWCloud2021x']>]]>
- The search capability in the Resources application has been improved and now works much faster.
- Empty categories are no longer displayed in the user interface.

#### Key Issues Fixed

- Assigned roles from both the user and user group can be returned.
- The LDAP import query endpoints with invalid given values are now returned with correct status codes and error messages.
- The open LDAP server is now supported that its user groups can now be displayed when searched for and imported.
- The problem regarding the false alarm message about multiple active users has been solved.
- The issue where inaccurate user assignments are displayed in the Resources application has been solved.
- The User pane in the Users application shows correct role names instead of role IDs.
- Now users with the User Manager role can import LDAP users and user groups.

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation' space='MCS2021x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='TWCloud2021x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Identify three types of suspicious usages: inconsistent version usages, cyclic usages, and unused resources.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="a5a6246d-fa03-431a-a7af-17674f746637"><p>Use filters to choose what types of usages you want to see in a usage map.</p></li><li data-uuid="8fe61809-f864-4d75-81ff-af8f99c72b8b"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Search for resources in a large usage map and view their details in the Resource pane.</span></p></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="displaying_project_usages.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Resource Usage Map application displaying the usage map of five selected resources.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Configuring Resource Usage Map URL" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about configuring Resource Usage Map URL>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Category-Level Permissions</h2><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Now you can manage Teamwork Cloud access rights more effectively by using category-level permissions. When role scope is category-specific, the role permissions apply to all resources stored in the selected category. </span>This means that new resources added to the category automatically inherit the role with all its assignments. This can save a lot of your time because you no longer have to assign new resources inside the category manually.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="category_level_permissions.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">You can assign a category to a specific role the same way you assign resources.</h6><h2>Branch-Level Permissions</h2><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Teamwork Cloud 2021x allows you to limit the scope of a role assigned to a user or user group not only to specific resources but to specific branches of a resource as well. For example, you can allow an engineer to work only on a specific branch while prohibiting access to any other resource branches he is not supposed to edit. This enables a strict branch-per-feature methodology resulting in better work discipline among different team members.</span></p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="setting_branch_level_permissions.png" /></ac:image><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">Specifying branch-level permissions.</span></h6><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h2>LDAP Server Configuration Changes</h2><p>An LDAP server query <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">for searching, retrieving, and importing users has been split into Users query and User groups query for more accurate search results when querying an LDAP server. It is important that you update User and User groups queries after upgrading your Teamwork Cloud instance to version 20.0.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="LDAP_server_queries.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of the LDAP server configuration page.</h6><h2>REST API Change Log</h2><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2021x" ri:content-title="REST API Change Log" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[REST API change log]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> concerns the following areas:</p><ul><li data-uuid="94f2437d-553f-46df-b8e6-8faa2b2fde64">Changes in data types.</li><li data-uuid="a292cd35-865a-439d-8fd6-ace034eaa88f">URLs relocated.</li><li data-uuid="d89086f1-2bdc-40c5-9311-437b5eef612d">New URLs.</li><li data-uuid="eb82f1c6-70c6-4c08-be6c-c18b5ee29117">API documentation (for more clarified information).</li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="2021x LTR Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Cameo Collaborator</h2><h3>Saving Comments as Model Elements</h3><p>In the previous version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you could work with Collaborator comments in a modeling tool by using the comments panel. However, comments were stored in a Cameo Collaborator document, so you could not use all the benefits of your modeling tool capabilities. In this version of the product, it is possible to save Cameo Collaborator comments as model elements in a project and work with them as you would with any other UML elements. In addition, comments are automatically synchronized between your project and a Cameo Collaborator document every time you commit changes to the server or update the project.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="working_with_comments_in_project.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This example shows how you can create comments as model elements and work with them in a modeling tool.</h6><p>This new functionality opens endless possibilities allowing you to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="795156f0-1aa7-40f1-ae2f-885f62b50c9a">Create new textual Cameo Collaborator comments and reply to existing ones right in a modeling tool.</li><li data-uuid="86dab53e-7580-4734-a44c-94d0833806f6">Modify comments, e.g., change comment status, priority, and even the commented element in the comment Specification window.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_specification.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="74c14b22-dcab-4311-bf99-e17cd45bb548">Validate if all comments have been created correctly and have all the required metadata by using the <em>Collaborator Comments in Project</em> validation suite.</li><li data-uuid="7c3d5eb4-84d1-4ece-9a5a-ae8f9164f14b">Create comment reports displaying Cameo Collaborator comments and their detailed information, such as an author, status, priority, etc.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_report.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="97e0d3a4-84bb-4824-9cea-96c72c8fa9ca">Calculate metrics allowing you to see how many comments are created and resolved or create your own metrics.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_metrics.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="2021x LTR Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Exporting Documents to PDF and HTML</h3><p>In this release of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, we are happy to present a new functionality allowing you to export documents to PDF and HTML formats. This makes information exchange a lot easier because you can save a Cameo Collaborator document as a file and share it via email or any file sharing system. Also, customize a document file by adding a cover page and the table of contents, or choosing what information should be exported. You can export the whole document or just a part of it, such as selected tables, paragraphs, or a specific view.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_documents_to_PDF_and_HTML.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This example shows a Cameo Collaborator document exported to the PDF format. Only an active document section has been exported.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="2021x LTR Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>New Model Editing Capabilities in Cameo Collaborator</h3><p>The model editing in Cameo Collaborator functionality has been extended even further. Now you can create values for stereotype tags, the Documentation property, and other textual properties (except for slot values) right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool to add new values to your model.</p><p><br /></p><p class="item" style="text-align: center;"><span class="name" style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);" title="Feature Description List"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_new_values.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 class="item" style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);">This example shows how to create a new stereotype tag value right in a Cameo Collaborator document.</span></h6><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="2021x LTR Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Disk Space Usage Improvements</h3><p>In this release of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the number of elements in documents has been significantly reduced and now Cameo Collaborator documents take 40-50% less space in the Teamwork Cloud server.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="disk_space_usage_after_publishing.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This chart demonstrates significantly decreased disk space usage in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 20.0 after publishing projects of different sizes.</h6><h3>Improved Publishing Performance</h3><p>In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2021x, the publishing performance has been significantly improved and now takes less time as shown in the charts below. In addition, the amount of memory you need to allocate to your modeling tool for publishing has decreased.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="publishing_time.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This chart compares the publishing time between product versions 19.0 SP4 and 2021x.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="first_update_time.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">In this chart, you can see that updating Collaborator documents takes less time compared to the earlier versions of the product.</h6><h3>Other</h3><ul><li data-uuid="aba1e8fa-3a1b-4b15-8d40-7167142dc94a">Email notification templates have been redesigned and now have a completely new modern look. In addition, you can edit these templates to customize email notification appearance.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="email_notification.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="80fe56eb-2632-46cc-b47d-8b1ffa0c42df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you are new to working with custom document templates, check our new </span><em>Cameo Collaborator Template Samples</em> project wich you can find in your modeling tool installation directory<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. It provides different document template samples and gives step by step instructions on how to create them.</span></li><li data-uuid="809d2a15-dfaa-4db7-8922-bab99c1be2a0">Now subscribing to email notifications is more convenient because you can do it right from the Resources application.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="subscribing_to_email_notifications.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h2>Other Enhancements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="43393fe3-e43a-4f5b-aba2-f60a9d5af238">Secure SSL communication can now be set up between Teamwork Cloud and Cassandra. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2021x" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud advanced configuration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to configure secure SSL communication >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="6dcc26f8-75d0-4b6f-86cf-192984be5f55">The search capability in the Resources application has been improved and now works much faster.</li><li data-uuid="69157815-8012-46c5-9f10-e0b87864ab48">Empty categories are no longer displayed in the user interface.</li></ul><h2>Key Issues Fixed</h2><ul><li data-uuid="359e4896-4fa7-4fdc-86b2-3ca3d7c05285">Assigned roles from both the user and user group can be returned.</li><li data-uuid="6ab8abda-4880-4204-9632-16fe77e7d71b">The LDAP import query endpoints with invalid given values are now returned with correct status codes and error messages.</li><li data-uuid="deca0874-0259-4c4a-9b28-cd1f0bcbd35b">The open LDAP server is now supported that its user groups can now be displayed when searched for and imported.</li><li data-uuid="552e95bb-3760-4b75-b272-9702f208b9c3">The problem regarding the false alarm message about multiple active users has been solved.</li><li data-uuid="7cacb867-833a-4a8d-bb49-d9455b88c5e1">The issue where inaccurate user assignments are displayed in the Resources application has been solved.</li><li data-uuid="dfb83efb-aafd-4c29-9c66-bacfae293c81">The User pane in the Users application shows correct role names instead of role IDs.</li><li data-uuid="507a3842-d24b-4e24-9a67-279d457d1795">Now users with the User Manager role can import LDAP users and user groups.</li></ul><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="6a8a2f71-655d-48ac-8c66-fd40ae10c97a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2021x" ri:content-title="Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2021x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="43219726-d49b-4139-8afb-daa56ea7752a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2021x" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud 2021x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622537 space=VN version=3 -->
## PAGE 00056: (2021x) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `261622537`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622537/2021x+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:16:48.010+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: February 12, 2021
