````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a571d67-6c72-4e26-ba35-8174d8f06643"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: February 12, 2021</h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This release introduces significant performance and scalability upgrades in key product areas: migration, converting between frameworks, building matrices, Merge, Teamwork Cloud project load, Teamwork Cloud project saving, table loading, Excel and CSV files import, and such common modeling tool operations as Copy/Paste, Find, Numbering, Element Selection, and Validation. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Additionally, this version adds support for the UAF 1.1 Profile, new diagrams and tables, and a number of usability and traceability improvements. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The released version is also packed with a few improvements that extended the usage of Simulink import and Modelica export. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">We introduce the new </span><a class="external-link" href="https://sysml.org/.res/docs/specs/OMGSysML-v1.6-19-11-01.pdf" rel="nofollow">SysML 1.6 specification</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> support, including new concepts such as Conjugated Interface Block, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">«</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">AddFlowPropertyValueOnNestedPortAction</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">» stereotype, </span>additional <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Binding Connector notation,</span> renamed compartments, enumerations, and more. </span></span></p><p>Version 2021x adds the ability to use layers in diagrams, automatically create legends, present diagrams or predefined sequences of diagrams in full screen to stakeholders, create compositions and aggregations in matrices, and use predefined element type filters when searching for the necessary model elements. Additionally, the Excel/CSV file import functionality has been reviewed and refreshed, and numerous improvements to Excel/CSV synchronization functionality in tables have been added.</p><p>Last but not least, project merge has been extended to give users the option to show only the changes in directly modified diagrams, hide Target and Equivalent Changes by default, and choose a specific ancestor calculation algorithm depending on merge scenario and expected outcome. </p><p>Contact your sales representative, and don't forget to give us your feedback on <a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc" rel="nofollow">Twitter</a> or <a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/" rel="nofollow">Facebook</a>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>UAF Features</h2><h3>Performance Improvements in UAF</h3><p>In this version, projects containing 1, 5, and 10 million elements were tested in both UPDM and UAF scopes. After receiving the results, improvements in migration, converting between frameworks, and building matrices were made for better performance.</p><h4>Migration</h4><p>The migration from UPDM2 to UAF 1.1 and from UAF 1.0 to UAF1.1 has been optimized and takes less time than in earlier versions. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_improvement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The comparison of migration time depending on the project size</h6><h4>Converting between frameworks</h4><p>The conversion between frameworks has been optimized. The following table shows the results of converting a project containing approximately 2 million elements and hundreds of framework packages, which needed to be moved or renamed:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 34.2841%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 69.3419%;" /><col class="" style="width: 30.6581%;" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th style="text-align: center;"><p>Earlier versions</p></th><th style="text-align: center;"><p>2021x version</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>The modeling tool stops responding after 30 min</td><td>~ 1 hour</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><br />Building matrices</h4><p>The scope for predefined matrices is no longer set by default. Now all matrices will start to build after the row and column scope is selected (<a href="https://jira.nomagic.com/browse/UPDM-11385">https://jira.nomagic.com/browse/UPDM-11385</a>)</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 34.4487%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 69.2185%;" /><col class="" style="width: 30.7815%;" /></colgroup><thead class=""><tr class=""><th style="text-align: center;">Earlier versions</th><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><p>2021x version</p></th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><p>Load predefined matrices in the projects with 10 million elements:</p><p>~ 1-3 hours. Sometimes more.</p></td><td><p>Create a new matrix:</p><p>   instantly (~1-2s)</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Methods and Standards</h3><h4>UAF 1.1 Profile Support</h4><p>Beginning with this version, the UAF 1.1 profile is supported. Therefore the following migrations will be necessary:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a305ff41-be0d-4239-a652-17afe53293a4">UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="6fc2c33a-83f7-4662-ace2-da79ba07400e">UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1. Note that there is no direct migration in this case. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the migration process >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="11b77757-e3db-439d-8c84-4d04f4956806">UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.1. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="910"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cea-evo-21x.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Evolution of standards</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Diagrams and Tables</h3><ul><li data-uuid="c0cec4dd-efe0-4e06-a158-e1443b6612c6">New <strong>Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart</strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">shows how actual enterprise phases change over time. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="f76b4059-4f9a-4b92-80d7-49618b689c3d">New <strong>Date and Time Summary Table</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Date and Time Summary Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="770f4522-4391-4287-90b5-c58be63ab969">New <strong>Personnel Forecast</strong>, <strong>Resources Forecast</strong>, and<strong> Standards Roadmap</strong> diagrams are implemented, improving the way they are created and used. The <strong>Personnel Forecast</strong>, <strong>Resources Forecast</strong>, and <strong>Standards Roadmap</strong> diagrams created with earlier versions of the modeling tool will be marked as deprecated and will be available only for review purposes.</li><li style="list-style-type: none;" data-uuid="c603859a-dfc5-4b4e-835c-15661045ecf8">The Deployment diagram has been enhanced. The <strong>Strategic Deployment</strong> table is renamed the <strong>Strategic Actual Deployment</strong> table and you can now create it using an <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard</strong>. Moreover, the <strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard is renamed the <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> and enhanced. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_structure_deployment_news.png" /></ac:image><ul><li><em>A technology preview </em>of the <strong>Hierarchical Measurements</strong> table. Please note that if you open a project created with earlier versions, the Measurement tables of those projects will be empty. However, you can create them anew.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Usability Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="313f04a3-b42a-4b12-a113-a550407e486f">The new Personnel Gantt Chart property <strong>Display Actual Persons in Different Lines</strong> was implemented. Now you can choose to see each Actual Person in separate lines.</li><li data-uuid="4731b60b-dbf1-4d6e-8121-9efd42af7b1e"><p>In the Gantt chart <strong>Options</strong> area, you can choose to mark a required period of time. One of the following Marking Modes are available:</p><ul><li><strong>None</strong>.<strong> </strong>Nothing will be marked.</li><li><strong>Period of Time</strong>. Choose to mark a period of time. For this mode, you can select the Start and End dates to be marked.</li><li><strong>Period of Actual Enterprise Phase</strong>. Choose to mark a period of the Actual Enterprise Phase. The time frame will be marked according to the Start and End dates set for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="5341d6c4-d5e9-4565-9978-87b789588133"><p>The <strong>Create Association on Part Creation</strong> project option is set to True. This means that when you create parts in the Part compartment of the Resource and Operational performers, the appropriate association is also created in the model.</p></li><li data-uuid="23d0ca87-900e-42be-9276-34a34b379720">A new Symbol property <strong>Show Measurements Inherited Name</strong> allows you to display the inherited measurements name through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_measurements.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Traceability Improvements</h3><p>The Implementation matrices have been enhanced. Now, b<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">y default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="8dbb4135-4f1e-443d-8a94-4360fbb16361">Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</li><li data-uuid="1b6aec1e-a353-4adf-9dc7-dc88ef7c8e42">Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</li><li data-uuid="4af70c20-b37a-44d9-ba7a-716ba8da4de1">Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</li><li data-uuid="f739908f-6c75-4113-92c8-2e1dc6ec2172">Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the </span><strong>Specification</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> window.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about working with the implementation matrices >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Other</h3><ul><li data-uuid="34aa3dd2-1242-46f4-acd0-d171b3c68504">The new version of the UJTL version (20200115) is now available. You can choose the library version when creating a new project.</li><li data-uuid="2bf8cd51-543f-4d7d-a21f-9fefcbf8c796"><p>When an old project (UAF 1.0 or UPDM2) is opened with the latest version (UAF plugin), stereotypes for nonDSL'ed associations should be applied:</p><ul><li>when the association is between OperationalAgent, the <em>&lt;&lt;OperationalAssociation&gt;&gt;</em> stereotype is applied. </li><li>when the association is between ResourcePerformer, the <em>&lt;&lt;ResourceAssociation&gt;&gt;</em> stereotype is applied.  </li></ul></li><li data-uuid="e566df8b-3315-4ea1-af1d-6e40f52e66b5">Operational Signal/Resources Signal now applies automatically for the DSL'ed signal.</li><li data-uuid="ed21d0bd-1c17-4ca0-8f93-28ae75f636dc">The derived properties of ActualResponsibleResources, ActualOrganization, ActualPost, and ActualPerson were renamed: <br /><ul><li><em>ownsActualProject</em> renamed to <em>managesActualProject, <br /></em></li><li><em>responsibleForActualResponsibility</em> renamed to <em>ownsActualResponsibility</em>, </li><li><em>responsibleForActualProjectMilestones, <br /></em></li><li><em>responsibleForActualResponsibility</em> renamed to <em>managesActualResponsibility</em>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b7f7a8b6-2f48-40ac-8ace-b34b717a47b3"><p>When working with the predefined matrices, you can make some changes to the default settings, such as changing the row or columns element type, dependency criteria, or directions. You can restore the following settings to default at any time:</p><ul><li>Description</li><li>Direction</li><li>Dependency Criteria</li><li>Show Elements</li><li>Column Element Type</li><li>Row Element Type</li></ul><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about w<span>orking with matrices</span> &gt;&gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="887d2e32-44d4-4199-aa52-4c8820889a33">New system options are implemented. One is for setting the default plugin and the other to display which plugin is installed. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CEA2021x" ri:content-title="Using UAF and UPDM2 plugins in Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>SysML Features</h2><h3>Proxy Ports in FMU and Simulink import</h3><p>FMU and Simulink import now support Proxy Port creation. Once you have imported the external FMU/Simulink model, you can select the Proxy Port to be created in your model and then specify an existing or create a new Interface Block to be used as its type.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_proxy_port.png" /></ac:image>Selecting the Proxy Port and an Interface Block upon the external model import.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Importing External Simulation Models" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about FMU and Simulink import >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Simulink model import to Activities</h3><p>You can import the Simulink model by dragging and dropping the Simulink file (*.slx) directly onto the Activity diagram. The Activity (stereotyped by <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">«</span>SimulinkBlock<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">»</span>) which is assigned as behavior for the Call Behavior Action and its Input and/or Output Pins are created in the model. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulink_import_activity_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Simulink import to Activity diagram.</h6><p><br /></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Simulink import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Simulink import >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Simulink composite signals export </h3><p>You can now select how to export <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Proxy Port or Interface Block with multiple Flow Properties (composite signals): as <span>bus Creators/Selectors or as bus In/Out ports. </span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulink_export_options.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Simulink export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Simulink export  >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></p><h3>IBD-based selective Modelica export</h3><p>You can now use the Internal Block Diagram to select what to export to Modelica. Only parts, ports, and connectors appearing in this diagram are exported, rather than the entire model. See whole vs partial export comparison in the image below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ibd_and_block_export_to_modelica.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The difference between whole (exporting Block) and partial (exporting IBD) export to the Modelica file.</span></h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Partial model export to the Modelica file" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Modelica export >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>SysML 1.6 support</h3><p>As of version 2021x the SysML Plugin supports the SysML 1.6 specification which brings the following improvements:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e376bced-fa36-4b51-9309-78d8b83a8c35">One more way to model conjugated ports is to create the Conjugated Interface Block which has the same features as its original Interface Block except that its Directed Features and Flow Properties are reversed (conjugated). <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Conjugated Interface Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Conjugated Interface Block >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d4e84d17-ed62-4ea6-a83e-bfdc13d9ff64">The ability to change the Binding Connector notation keyword from &quot;equal&quot; to sign &quot;=&quot;. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Creating Binding Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to create Binding Connector >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="5ee3e9a3-51e2-4fc1-9abe-bd95829dbc22"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The new stereotype <span>«</span>AddFlowPropertyValueOnNestedPortAction<span>» is created which enables values added to a flow property to propagate out through a specified behavioral port of an object executing the action, rather than all behavior ports exposing the flow property. It also enables values added to a flow property to propagate into objects.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="f6739c8b-2ec8-48c0-bd8e-20102810fc02"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>The AdjunctProperty principal type is changed from Element to NamedElement.</span><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="69e86882-9ca1-4cd2-bbfe-8d1b087322ed">The <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">«</span>PropertySpecificType<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">»</span> stereotype is renamed to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">«</span>pst<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">».</span></li><li data-uuid="4b3a1ade-5440-473c-9f23-3b26a8e58312">The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Value Type" /></ac:link> compartment <em>attributes</em> is renamed to <em>properties</em>.</li><li data-uuid="f5775986-fcb0-4ab7-bd96-352111c67668">The<em> initialValue </em>compartment is renamed<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>to<em> <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="Initial Values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[initial values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</em></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><br /></span></span></li><li data-uuid="921f581a-00dd-4b40-92be-211b688939dd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The s</span><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ignal receptions </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment is renamed to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <em>receptions.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="380c176c-63c2-48ea-b56a-846e279f6e73">The <em>FlowDirection, FeatureDirection and ControlValue </em>enumerations are renamed to <em>FlowDirectionKind</em>, <em>FeatureDirectionKind</em> and <em>ControlValueKind </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">respectively</span>.</li></ul><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2021x" ri:content-title="SysML 1.6 support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about SysML 1.6 support >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="margin-left: 0.0in;">Client-side Performance Improvements</h2><h3 style="margin-left: 0.0in;">Partial Data Loading in Tables</h3><p>Experience enhanced table loading and scrolling performance! Thanks to the newly-introduced Load Partially mode, data is now loaded in only visible rows. There is no longer a need to wait until data is calculated in the entire table. Additionally, fast scrolling has improved; it is now considerably smoother.</p><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_opening.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the opening time of Requirement Table having 7 standard columns with a different number of requirements.</h6><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Partial loading in tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about partial loading in tables >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Increased Excel and CSV files Import Speed</h3><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;">The import performance of data from an Excel or CSV file into the modeling tool adds a great experience while managing a large scope of data. The data import time using the table sync feature is significantly faster compared with the previous version (19.0 SP4).  Also, the d<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ata import using the new Excel/CSV Import dialog is even better in comparison to Excel/CSV Sync feature</span>. The import of 61k elements takes only 2 minutes (see the chart below).</p><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_import_performance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 0.0in;"><span>A chart comparing data import from Excel and CSV files using the Excel/CSV Sync and Excel/CSV Import dialogs.</span></h6><h3 style="margin-left: 0.0in;">Common Operations</h3><ul><li data-uuid="afd6d0f5-4d18-46a5-a44e-b8125a2f8614"><strong>Copy/Paste. </strong>Now you can copy and paste data 2-4 times faster depending on the specific scenario. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy_and_paste_in_twc_project.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>A chart comparing the copy&amp;paste operation time in various scenarios.</span></h6><ul><li data-uuid="e1abc0b3-052c-4f7e-ae5c-4db65e0ad398"><strong>Find</strong>. The search speed in all texts has increased by 70-150% compared to 19.0 SP4.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_operation.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the Find operation speed in 19.0 SP4 and 2021x projects containing 1M, 2M, and 5M elements (including used projects).</h6><ul><li data-uuid="16d6e496-566d-4259-bd6d-f140ba588b40"><strong>Numbering</strong>. Creating the first numbered element, copying/pasting numbered elements, renumbering elements recursively, and updating the last element ID is now 30-840 times faster. </li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering_operations_performance_improvement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the Numbering operations speed in 19.0 SP4 and 2021x projects containing 10M elements (including used projects).</h6><ul><li data-uuid="bc19fa03-c89e-4d0c-9952-b15470ac0017"><p><strong>Element Selection.</strong> Now you can search and select the needed elements (e.g., in the <strong>Select Element</strong> and <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialogs as well as drop-down lists) approximately 40-50 times faster.</p></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_selection_performance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the element selection speed in 19.0 SP4 and 2021x projects containing 5M elements (including used projects).</h6><ul><li data-uuid="19bdf261-24a6-4ed2-a143-9a7d1cb67143"><strong>Validation</strong>. The active validation of the model now takes less than a second (0.8s after 5M project load and 0.5s after diagram load). </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="active_validation_performance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the active validation speed after the project and diagram load in 19.0 SP4 and 2021x projects containing 5M elements (including used projects).</h6><h3>Profiling Changes in the UML 2.5.1 Metamodel</h3><p>Significant changes have been implemented in the UML metamodel for improved performance. The InstanceSpecifications, Slots, and ValueSpecifications are no longer used to store profiling data. Instead, new model element types and properties are introduced, while some have been removed (see the diagram below). </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UML 2.5.1 Profiling.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Profiling changes in the UML metamodel.</h6><p>As shown in the figure above, Element references the TaggedValue that is used to specify the Boolean, Integer, Real, String, or Element values of the applied stereotype properties. This approach uses fewer model elements to store profiling data, which leads to a decrease in the total number of elements in SysML and UAF projects by 40-80%.</p><p><strong>Note</strong>. Expressions based on the previously implemented profiling data storing metamodel no longer work, meaning that they have to be updated manually.</p><h2>Teamwork Cloud-related Performance Improvements</h2><h3>Project Merge</h3><p>Project Merge has undergone considerable performance improvements. The modeling tool version 2021x requires approximately 4-5 times less memory to successfully merge two server project branches, compared to 19.0 SP4. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merge_memory_consumption.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing merge memory consumption in different modeling tool versions.</h6><p>Furthermore, the merge operation speed has increased by 20-70% in 2021x compared to 19.0 SP4. Both frequent (between close project versions) and infrequent (between distant project versions) merges can now be performed much faster!</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merge_performance_improvements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Charts comparing merge performance in Speed and Memory modes.</h6><h3>TWC Project Load </h3><p>Project load memory and speed improvements have been implemented for Teamwork Cloud. The modeling tool now requires only 2GB of heap to load a 2M SysML project (first and second time) when working with TWC projects compared to 8GB in 19.0 SP4. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Additionally, as a result of the UML metamodel changes,</span> SysML projects are now opened 2-3 times faster.</p><p><strong>Note</strong>. Received results cover the project load only, excluding validation, diagramming, and further modeling tool feature usage.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="client_heap_size.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="first_and_second_project_loads.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Charts comparing project load memory and speed improvements between different modeling tool versions.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="40c37c77-f34e-42e0-be13-0e44299a9781">Three projects (sizes ranging from 1M to 2M) in 19.0 SP4 and the same projects in 2021x (sizes ranging from 750k to 1M) were used to run the tests.</li><li data-uuid="21df5f55-3eca-4dc6-b68c-ca23ea3272ef">Migration time is excluded because projects that had already been migrated were used for testing.</li></ul><h3>TWC Save to Local </h3><p>In 2021x, great attention is paid to the memory consumption improvements. Saving a Teamwork Cloud project locally now requires less memory. For example, a 2M SysML project now consumes only 4GB of RAM, compared to 20GB in 19.0 SP4. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_to_local_memory_consumption.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing memory consumption when saving a TWC project as a local one between different modeling tool versions.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><a style="text-align: right;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CEA2021x/2021x+Version+News#id-2021xVersionNews-top">Back to top</a></p><h5>System Requirements</h5><p>RAM and disk space requirements have been updated taking into consideration different sizes of large models.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 37.8804%;"><colgroup class=""> <col class="" style="width: 25.2874%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 24.7126%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 24.569%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 25.431%;" /> </colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">Project Elements Count<br />(including used projects)</th><th style="text-align: center;" rowspan="2"><p>Allocated Memory<sup>2</sup></p></th><th style="text-align: center;" rowspan="2">Disk Space</th></tr><tr class=""><th style="text-align: center;">Earlier versions</th><th style="text-align: center;">2021x and later<sup>1</sup></th></tr><tr class=""><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">6GB</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">6GB</td></tr><tr class=""><td style="text-align: center;">2.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">2 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">7GB</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">10GB</td></tr><tr class=""><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">3.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">11GB</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">30GB</td></tr></tbody></table><h6 style="text-align: center;"><sup>1 </sup>the project elements count is different due to the profiling data storing metamodel optimized in 2021x. <br />  <sup>2 </sup>these memory requirements are indicative and may vary by project specifics.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86c1e37a-6f77-4e5e-aa46-ea5b738c8c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mi</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3>Layers Based on Legends</h3><p>Diagrams can get extremely crowded as a model evolves. For example, when highlighting a chosen piece of information, only one specific aspect of the system becomes a challenge. For your convenience, you can now filter diagrams by the selected Legend items to make only the necessary diagram layers stand out.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_by_legend_items_.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Filtering the diagram by the selected Legend item.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about layers based on Legends >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Intelligent Legend Creation</h3><p>Do you feel that Legend creation is a tiresome activity? Not at all! From now on, you can create Legends more easily than ever before: extract them automatically from the elements displayed on your diagram instead of relying on a manual creation. Additionally, you can now automatically extract Legend items for the existing Legends. Once the Legend items are created, you can customize their adornments representation. They can now be displayed as rectangular shapes or extended lines to represent paths and shapes in greater detail.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extracting_legends.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Extracting the Port Types legend according to the elements displayed on the diagram as symbols.</h6><h3>Presentation Mode</h3><p>You can now present diagrams in full screen without showing user interface components, such as as model browser, diagram palette, status bar, and toolbars. That is not all! You can also define the sequence of diagrams to present one after another.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="presentation_mode.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Diagram in the enabled Presentation Mode.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Presentation Mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Presentation Mode >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3 style="margin-left: 0.0in;">Renewed Excel/CSV File Import</h3><p>You can import data from an Excel/CSV file directly to the model without installing Excel and CSV Import Plugins (read more about <ac:link ac:anchor="Discontinued products"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[discontinued products]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). From now on, the newly created Excel/CSV Import dialog brings an easy to use and clearly understandable graphical user interface. It allows you to import content with just a few clicks by selecting the Excel/CSV file and specifying element mapping options. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Additionally, you can save your maps to reuse them, collect saved maps in groups, manage those groups, and use an entire group instead of a single map for data import.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_import_dialog.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Excel/CSV Import dialog with specified options for satisfy relationship import.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Excel/CSV file import >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Extended Excel and CSV Sync</h3><p>Three improvements expanded the data synchronization between Excel or CSV files and a model:</p><ul><li data-uuid="57e62414-181d-4428-817d-52bc10802538">Unique elements. Elements to be synchronized can now be identified not only by their name, but also by element property or tag value.</li><li data-uuid="9b6c9be8-d9cd-43bc-97fc-3a742ffa72bf">Direct relationships. From now on, you can import direct relationships between the source and target elements as easily as other elements.</li><li data-uuid="6cf352ca-2b4d-4228-9ba7-1fe88c7a6950">Relationship ends. When importing relationships between different elements, you can select a specific property or tag to identify the source and target end of the relationship.<br /><br /></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync_dialog_new_options.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">New mapping options in the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog: Identification Property, Identify Relationship (end) Source by, Identify Relationship (end) Target by.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Excel/CSV Sync in tables >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Composition and Aggregation Relationships in Dependency Matrix</h3><p>You can now define relationships between the elements in your model more accurately! The latest release gives you the ability to create and display Composition and Aggregation relationships in a Dependency Matrix.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compossition_agreegation_association_creation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating and displaying Composition, Aggregation, and Association in a Dependency Matrix.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Selecting criteria" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Composition and Aggregation creation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c19cd83-766f-4c50-aeed-406d652bce29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Feature Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="65c47a9b-bb19-4a2b-8fc5-b9ddee3a5e5a"><p>Applying Legend items to the same element is more convenient since they are now grouped by Legend.</p></li><li data-uuid="a2cbb78f-9c49-4dfd-b44e-6da2e8ee9297"><p>A reference from the image shape to the attached image is now created when attached images are displayed as diagram shapes.</p></li><li data-uuid="39ad389d-919c-436c-b03c-26783b2637ed"><p>The Diagram Tabs Limit<strong> </strong>is set to 20 to prevent high memory utilization. You can change this environment option at any time. </p></li><li data-uuid="ca3f7145-7fae-42e2-93f3-081e55a4871b"><p>The smart autocompletion command Ctrl + Space now works for the Change Event expression in a State Machine diagram.</p></li><li data-uuid="f310cfea-6b6d-4b62-8584-aec300eeeffe"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the </span>Select Element<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> and </span>Quick Find<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> dialogs, e</span>lements can now be filtered by their types to narrow down search results. </p></li><li data-uuid="af5b1794-fd84-4bea-a614-31d36cda6bfe">The Jython 2.7.2 version is now supported.</li><li data-uuid="3de5e076-1583-4522-b179-4b2bb799224b">The MARTE Profile version 1.2 is now supported. </li><li data-uuid="c5266449-b478-4905-b71c-89d36e420fbf"><span>The Reader Edition is now available for all the 2021x version modeling tools. Contact your sales executive to request the license.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h2>Discontinued Products</h2><ul><li data-uuid="4a7a7b8f-be09-4812-9b38-0ff7cf33d09e"><p class="auto-cursor-target">The development of the following plugins is discontinued:</p><ul><li>Document Modeling Plugin (Model-based reports). For more effective model publishing and reviewing on the web, use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Collaborator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create Views and Viewpoints diagrams using the modeling tool with no plugins installed, use the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin or  the SysML plugin instead. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The DockBook Library Profile is no longer available. </span></li></ul></li><li>Methodology Wizard Plugin.</li><li>QVT Plugin.</li><li>Excel Import Plugin. We highly recommend using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> features instead.</li><li>CSV Import Plugin. We highly recommend using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> features instead.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">LieberLieber Embedded Engineer plugin.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Project Element Counter plugin.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">MBSE plugin.</span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="c9b0e24a-7428-487f-9ec8-3e73567e5612">Web portal reports are no longer available. For more effective model publishing and reviewing on the web, use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Collaborator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can also use Web Publisher (Simple HTML) or Web Publisher 2.0 ReportWizard templates to generate HTML reports.</li><li data-uuid="19c41e7a-1913-4867-a8c6-931f15b9642c">WSDL Code Engineering and WSDL diagrams are no longer available.</li><li data-uuid="77683c23-db59-45f4-a1af-86c88b600b66">C# Code Engineering and C# Professional Edition are no longer available.</li><li data-uuid="97778ac2-b817-4671-a17f-542bd907d2b9">CIL and CIL Disassembler Code Engineering are no longer available.</li><li data-uuid="54ff6da8-ed6e-4b59-9e89-6d5d61d91c7c"><p>The News Reader (RSS) is no longer available. The notification at the right bottom corner of the modeling tool will inform you about the new version release instead.</p></li><li data-uuid="923338d2-8a73-44af-b3e1-452af003091f">The <em>no_install_mac.zip</em> and <em>32-bit</em> installation files are no longer offered for download.</li><li data-uuid="0cb7820a-0669-49b3-b32f-7b00f0473fe5">The Project Usage Map Profile is no longer available. </li><li data-uuid="ad81af96-2b0c-4fe5-aa91-b1af1a2e420d">The JavaScript Nashorn is deprecated with the intention of removing it. We highly recommend using JavaScript Rhino instead.  </li><li data-uuid="cd0791fd-1789-45ad-8474-9001e83ea7dc">The following 3rd party plugins are discontinued:<ul><li>Cameo Workbench</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Cameo Inter-Op</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">ParaMagic Lite</span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="5e9b5b8e-629d-4a2f-b7cf-2a0c7f76aad8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The following no cost plugins are no longer available in modeling tools:<br /></span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">FAS. From now on, download it here: <a class="external-link" href="https://sourceforge.net/projects/fas4md" title="Follow link">https://sourceforge.net/projects/fas4md</a>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">SYSMOD. From now on, <span>download it here: <a href="https://sourceforge.net/projects/sysmod4md/">https://sourceforge.net/projects/sysmod4md/</a>.</span></span></li></ul></li></ul><h3>From Teamwork Server to Teamwork Cloud</h3><p>Teamwork Server is no longer supported in the latest release. Teamwork Cloud should be used as a product for collaborative modeling and model version control. Customers still using Teamwork Server must migrate their server projects to TWC 19.0 series first and then upgrade to TWC 2021x.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2021x" ri:content-title="Migrating from Teamwork Server" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to migrate from Teamwork Server to Teamwork Cloud >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>From Cameo Collaborator for Alfresco to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud</h3><p>From version 2021x, Cameo Collaborator for Alfresco is no longer supported. We recommend using Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud instead.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for TWC vs Cameo Collaborator for Alfresco" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn the benefits of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud compared to Cameo Collaborator for Alfresco >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021x" ri:content-title="Migration from Alfresco server" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to migrate your documents and comments from the Alfresco server to Teamwork Cloud >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to To]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Collaboration</h2><h3>Detecting Changes in Diagrams During Merge</h3><p>You can now specify in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog whether to compare only the direct or all the changes in diagrams during the Project Merge and Project Comparison operations. By default, only the changes made by the user in loaded (open) diagrams are analyzed, leaving out unintentional diagram changes that occurred due to indirect changes in the model. This feature not only eases both merge and project comparison processes for the user, since the number of diagrams with changes is considerably reduced but also optimizes modeling tool performance. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options_direct_changes.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An Environment option to specify the changes to detect in diagrams.</h6><p>Learn more about diagram changes in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Understanding change concept" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[model merge]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Understanding change types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project comparison]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> &gt;&gt;</p><h3>Further Merge Usability Improvements</h3><p>The model merge has undergone further usability improvements. From now on, changes from the Target and Equivalent Changes are automatically hidden by default; only the Source changes are shown. As a result, fewer changes need to be reviewed by the user.</p><p>Additionally, the model merge has been extended by adding a capability to select which ancestor calculation algorithm to use. You now have the freedom to choose a Branch Point as an ancestor for your merges. This is especially useful when merge results become too complex to understand and analyze due to previously rejected changes. By default, the Lowest Common Parent ancestor calculation algorithm is used.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_branch_point_in_merge_projects_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting the ancestor for future merges in the Merge Project dialog.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Understanding merge types in Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about merge types in Teamwork Cloud >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Other Features</h2><h3>Report Wizard</h3><ul><li data-uuid="6ed5e186-d177-4891-b627-295c48238a4f"><p>With a significant improvement in report generations for large-scale MagicDraw projects, Report Wizard 2021x has now been equipped to more efficiently generate reports using Web Publisher 2.0 templates of projects, with less time required and supporting more elements than earlier versions of the product.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A bar chart displaying the performance testing of report generation from Report Wizard 19.0 SP4 and 2021x using Web Publisher 2.0.</h6></li><li data-uuid="9b1d3755-0c9b-4b07-9ae6-58c1dc6b3a85">You can view the overall progress of generating reports deploying Web Publisher 2.0 through the progress bar and its displayed success percentage.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Progress Bar.png" /></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">The progress bar with the success percentage shown above when generating reports using Web Publisher 2.0.</h6></li><li data-uuid="b258f808-7f58-4dd3-acea-993f65a70a7f"><p>A more effective tool of the Document Report Designer diagram is now available for conveniently drafting and designing new extensive templates of the Microsoft Word Document (DOCX) format. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Document Report Designer diagram" /><ac:link-body>Discover more about Document Report Designer &gt;&gt;<br /><br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Document Report Designer.png" /></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Document Report Designer for drafting and designing new templates of the Microsoft Word Document (DOCX) format.<span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br /></span></span></h6></li><li data-uuid="a71fa856-3c27-457d-ba36-7c76768deac0">With <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the Spreadsheet Report Designer </span>provided in Report Wizard 2021x, you are now capable of effectively designing and generating Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet (XLSX) format. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Find out how to use Spreadsheet Report Designer >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><br /><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Spreadsheet Report Designer.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Spreadsheet Report Designer for designing and generating the Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet (XLSX) format.</h6></li><li data-uuid="2592c14b-98b9-4c59-b7f4-386f5cc9417e"><p>Web Publisher 2.0 now has the <strong>Quick properties</strong> panel, at the lower left (similar to MagicDraw), to show the documentation settings in the <strong>Documentation</strong> tab and properties in the <strong>Properties</strong> tab of elements selected in the diagram and from the Containment tree. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Features and layout" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about the new Quick properties panel &gt;&gt;<br /><br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Quick Properties Panel.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Quick properties panel: Documentation and Properties tabs.</h6></li><li data-uuid="e633a0b5-7334-49ed-ac6c-b764e2ee6c03"><p>The capability of <strong>$report.isEmpty(obj)</strong> has been specially improved to check HTML String whether it is empty or not. <ac:link ac:anchor="$report.isEmpty(obj)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Report tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about using this report tool >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="638263b2-1ca1-4d90-8784-2a6a6702756b"><p>The multi-field sorter is now available as a function for sorting a collection of elements in ascending or descending order by deploying multiple sorting properties, rather than merely a single property as in previous versions. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Sorter tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Find out more about the multi-field sorter >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="29da09e9-03d0-40c5-9054-a8f4a720968b"><p>You can now sort elements by using a tag in applied stereotypes, regardless of being customized. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Sorter tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about this capability of the sorter tool >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="7cec641a-753a-4854-af79-8871f94fa9ad"><p>You can utilize a new sorter function for sorting elements as they appear in the Containment tree. <ac:link ac:anchor="$sorter.containmentTreeSort(collection)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Sorter tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Discover how to use this sorter tool >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="ab90d3a9-7851-47c2-9632-972ce56496eb">With a new option reducing XML meta-data file name length, the error of Web Publisher 2.0 files with lengthy names has been fixed. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Know more about this report variable in Web Publisher 2.0 >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="eb6d2d1d-061d-4380-95fa-19ff2da92195">Single or double quotes can now be interchangeably used in both of the <strong>#includesection</strong> and <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> directives and <strong>$import.includeSection(templateName, sectionName)</strong> of the Import Tool.</li><li data-uuid="727d9317-7e80-463e-8959-77a95cd14cee">You can now access and list property objects (including DSL) classified in the PropertyGroup of customized stereotypes. <ac:link ac:anchor="$report.getPropertyGroupsByStereotype(stereotype)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Report tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See how the report tool works this way >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1b2910fa-eff2-4bfb-9329-5749a2873c85">Reference bookmarks and their links can be created for DOCX numbered items. <ac:link ac:anchor="$bookmark.createRef(referenceID)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Bookmark tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn about using these bookmark tools >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="b15286ce-3ea3-4989-b39b-36758d33ce3c">Functions for displaying lists of properties in property groups for given elements and stereotypes are ready to use. <ac:link ac:anchor="$report.getPropertyGroupsByStereotype(stereotype)"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Report tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to use this group of report tools >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Other</h3><ul><li data-uuid="b48f32dc-e07f-48bf-b5b4-0ee678b9b152">The automatic UAF, SysML, and BPMN project migration using the Teamwork Cloud <strong>Migrate Projects to Version</strong> functionality has been enhanced. The projects are migrated silently and now no additional confirmation is required. However, if you have both, UAF and UPDM projects in your repository, be advised to perform the <strong>Migrate Projects to Version</strong> using the same environment as your project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2021x" ri:content-title="Migrating projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Key issues fixed</h2><ul><li data-uuid="3d4f9ff1-a891-4f0a-8b03-a0e1d2d9cb7f">The issues concerning the All Views report have been fixed. The matrices and Producing/Consuming OperationalActivities/Functions are now included.</li><li data-uuid="f7e0ee74-c2f0-4f88-94da-7e9362ee2f7f">The issue where the PartProperty stereotype instead of ReferenceProperty stereotype was applied for the role when the Aggregation relation is created has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="c99642c1-a5d7-4fe5-8bf2-79d0d6beebb9">The additional stereotype is no longer applied for the element with custom stereotypes after migration from UPDM2 to UAF.</li><li data-uuid="ce491896-6e49-4c35-aae0-d4f497547302">[UPDM2] The issue where redundant Block stereotypes were applied for specific Environment elements has been fixed. Now only Value Type is applied for Condition, Climate, and LightCondition elements in SysML Compliance mode.</li><li data-uuid="066900af-ccd4-494b-b131-fc33dc3be983">[UPDM2] The validation rule COR2071 no longer fails in a valid situation.</li><li data-uuid="6f3e9cd6-49e9-4d7d-b138-27e165b62503">[UPDM2] The issue where incorrect metaclass element is created with Impelemnts stereotype applied when solving validation rules COM2023 and COM2047. Now the Abstract relation with the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">«</span>Implements<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">»</span> stereotype is created.</li><li data-uuid="300c27c1-e2f8-471b-acfd-f6d198821d1e">A resources tag value of Call Activity now is set when the BPMN compliance mode is ON.</li><li data-uuid="025c9f3e-1f2f-4712-9367-4315a6cfc89d">The issue where Operational Object Flow was not keeping project settings when BPMN compliance mode is ON has been fixed.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="562cc43d-d9c9-4492-a968-b5abd0c04ec1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSOSA2021x" ri:content-title="Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Systems of Systems Architect 2021x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="7b5ac0f0-71ee-4691-832d-9eb3346e4c07"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CEA2021x" ri:content-title="Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2021x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622754 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00061: (2021x) SysML v1 Plugin

- page_id: `261622754`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622754/2021x+SysML+v1+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-09T14:19:54.300+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

The 2021x release is packed with a few improvements that extend the usage of Simulink import and Modelica export. We introduce the new[SysML 1.6 specification](https://sysml.org/.res/docs/specs/OMGSysML-v1.6-19-11-01.pdf)support, including new concepts such as Conjugated Interface Block, «AddFlowPropertyValueOnNestedPortAction» stereotype, additional Binding Connector notation,renamed compartments, enumerations, and more.

Download it today at [nomagic.com](http://www.nomagic.com/) or contact your sales representative, and don't forget to give us your feedback on [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc) or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/).

##### Proxy Ports in FMU and Simulink import

FMU and Simulink import now support Proxy Port creation. Once you have imported the external FMU/Simulink model, you can select the Proxy Port to be created in your model and then specify an existing or create a new Interface Block to be used as its type.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Selecting the Proxy Port and an Interface Block upon the external model import.

[Learn more about FMU and Simulink import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64958559/Importing+External+Simulation+Models)

##### Simulink model import to Activities

You can import the Simulink model by dragging and dropping the Simulink file (*.slx) directly onto the Activity diagram. The Activity (stereotyped by «SimulinkBlock») which is assigned as behavior for the Call Behavior Action and its Input and/or Output Pins are created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Simulink import to Activity diagram.

[Learn more about Simulink import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64958560/Simulink+import)

##### Simulink composite signals export

You can now select how to export Proxy Port or Interface Block with multiple Flow Properties (composite signals): as bus Creators/Selectors or as bus In/Out ports.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[Learn more about Simulink export >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64958199/Simulink+export)

##### IBD-based selective Modelica export

You can now use the Internal Block Diagram to select what to export to Modelica. Only parts, ports, and connectors appearing in this diagram are exported, rather than the entire model. See whole vs partial export comparison in the image below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The difference between whole (exporting Block) and partial (exporting IBD) export to the Modelica file.

[Learn more about Modelica export >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64958540/Partial+model+export+to+the+Modelica+file)

##### SysML 1.6 support

As of version 2021x the SysML Plugin supports the SysML 1.6 specification which brings the following improvements:

- One more way to model conjugated ports is to create the Conjugated Interface Block which has the same features as its original Interface Block except that its Directed Features and Flow Properties are reversed (conjugated). Learn more about Conjugated Interface Block >>
- The ability to change the Binding Connector notation keyword from "equal" to sign "=". Learn how to create Binding Connector >>
- The new stereotype «AddFlowPropertyValueOnNestedPortAction» is createdwhich enables values added to a flow property to propagate out through a specified behavioral port of an object executing the action, rather than all behavior ports exposing the flow property. It also enables values added to a flow property to propagate into objects.
- The AdjunctProperty principal type is changed from Element to NamedElement.
- The « PropertySpecificType » stereotype is renamed to « pst ».
- The Value Type compartment attributes is renamed to properties .
- The initialValue compartment is renamed to *[initial values](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64959265/Initial+Values).*
- The s ignal receptions compartment is renamed to *receptions.*
- The FlowDirection, FeatureDirection and ControlValue enumerations are renamed to FlowDirectionKind , FeatureDirectionKind and ControlValueKind respectively .

[Learn more about SysML 1.6 support >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64958651/SysML+1.6+support)

##### Key Issues Fixed

The following key issues have been fixed with this version:

- The « SimulinkBlock » references now to Simscape components.
- Initial Values are now exported to Modelica and are recognized by their name and initial values set.
- The Modelica model file starts with a package definition.
- From now on, the RequirementDerivedPropertyValueUpdater.isTransient() returns the true value for DerivedFrom tag.
- The validation now checks if the types of connected ports are different (e.g. Real and String ) and informs about incompatible Flow Properties.
- The Structure option is set by default in the Row Query of the SysML Allocation Matrix.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2021x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

&gt;&gt;</a></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">IBD-based selective Modelica export</h3><p style="text-align: left;">You can now use the Internal Block Diagram to select what to export to Only parts, ports, and connectors appearing in this diagram are exported, rather than the entire model. See whole vs partial export comparison in the image below.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ibd_and_block_export_to_modelica.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-magenta-bolder,#50253f);">The difference between whole (exporting Block) and partial (exporting IBD) export to the Modelica file.</span></h6><p style="text-align: left;"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64958540/Partial+model+export+to+the+Modelica+file" rel="nofollow">Learn more about Modelica export &gt;&gt;</a></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">SysML 1.6 support</h3><p style="text-align: left;">As of version 2021x the SysML Plugin supports the SysML 1.6 specification which brings the following improvements:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="bec8b883-b27a-4cf5-b6e1-cc307fe733e4">One more way to model conjugated ports is to create the Conjugated Interface Block which has the same features as its original Interface Block except that its Directed Features and Flow Properties are reversed (conjugated).<span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2021x/pages/64958908/Conjugated+Interface+Block" rel="nofollow">Learn more about Conjugated Interface Block &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="6ea01613-f90f-4800-8894-7acb5c727174">The ability to change the Binding Connector notation keyword from &quot;equal&quot; version:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="a8f052e3-24aa-4917-856d-5f0fd2913ec1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The «</span>SimulinkBlock<span for <strong>DerivedFrom</strong> tag. </li><li data-uuid="7cf1a20c-27f4-4f72-bd66-c7d1cc135be5">The validation now checks if the types of connected ports are different (e.g. <em>Real</em> and <em>String</em>) and informs about incompatible Flow Properties. </li><li data-uuid="12cfa4c1-5844-4fd4-b9c7-bf28e5b3df64">The <strong>Structure </strong>option is set by
