<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622818 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00062: (2021x) UAF Plugin

- page_id: `261622818`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622818/2021x+UAF+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-09T14:19:56.276+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

This release introduces significant performance and scalability upgrades in key product areas: migration, converting between frameworks, building matrices, Merge, Teamwork Cloud project load, Teamwork Cloud repository disk footprint, table loading, and copy/paste operations.

Additionally, this version adds support for the UAF 1.1 Profile, new diagrams and tables, and a number of usability and traceability improvements.

Contact your sales representative, and don't forget to give us your feedback on [Twitter](https://twitter.com/nm_inc) or [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/).

#### Performance Improvements

In this version, projects containing 1, 5, and 10 million elements were tested in both UPDM and UAF scopes. After receiving the results, improvements in migration, converting between frameworks, and building matrices were made for better performance.

##### Migration

The migration from UPDM2 to UAF 1.1 and from UAF 1.0 to UAF1.1 has been optimized and takes less time than in earlier versions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comparison of migration time depending on the project size

##### Converting between frameworks

The conversion between frameworks has been optimized. The following table shows the results of converting a project containing approximately 2 million elements and hundreds of framework packages, which needed to be moved or renamed:

| Earlier versions | 2021x version |
| --- | --- |
| The modeling tool stops responding after 30 min | ~ 1 hour |

##### Building matrices

The scope for predefined matrices is no longer set by default. Now all matrices will start to build after the row and column scope is selected.

| Earlier versions | 2021x version |
| --- | --- |
| Load predefined matrices in the projects with 10 million elements:~ 1-3 hours. Sometimes more. | Create a new matrix: instantly (~1-2s) |

#### Methods and Standards

##### UAF 1.1 Profile Support

Beginning with this version, the UAF 1.1 profile is supported. Therefore the following migrations will be necessary:

- UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1' space='UAFP2021x']>]]>
- UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1. Note that there is no direct migration in this case. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1' space='UAFP2021x']>]]>
- UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1' space='UAFP2021x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Evolution of standards

#### Diagrams and Tables

- New Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart shows how actual enterprise phases change over time. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart' space='UAFP2021x']>]]>
- New Date and Time Summary Table depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Date and Time Summary Table' space='UAFP2021x']>]]>
- New Personnel Forecast , Resources Forecast , and Standards Roadmap diagrams are implemented, improving the way they are created and used. The Personnel Forecast , Resources Forecast , and Standards Roadmap diagrams created with earlier versions of the modeling tool will be marked as deprecated and will be available only for review purposes.
- The Deployment diagram has been enhanced. The Strategic Deployment table is renamed the Strategic Actual Deployment table and you can now create it using an Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard . Moreover, the Deployment Milestones wizard is renamed the Actual Resources Deployment Wizard and enhanced. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAFP2021x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A technology preview of the Hierarchical Measurements table. Please note that if you open a project created with earlier versions, the Measurement tables of those projects will be empty. However, you can create them anew.

#### Usability Improvements

- The new Personnel Gantt Chart property Display Actual Persons in Different Lines was implemented. Now you can choose to see each Actual Person in separate lines.
- In the Gantt chart **Options** area, you can choose to mark a required period of time. One of the following Marking Modes are available:
  - None . Nothing will be marked.
  - Period of Time . Choose to mark a period of time. For this mode, you can select the Start and End dates to be marked.
  - Period of Actual Enterprise Phase . Choose to mark a period of the Actual Enterprise Phase. The time frame will be marked according to the Start and End dates set for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.
- The **Create Association on Part Creation** project option is set to True. This means that when you create parts in the Part compartment of the Resource and Operational performers, the appropriate association is also created in the model.
- A new Symbol property Show Measurements Inherited Name allows you to display the inherited measurements name through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. [ATTACHMENT filename='dot_notation_measurements.png']

#### Traceability Improvements

The Implementation matrices have been enhanced. Now, by default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the**Specification**window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

>]]>

#### Other

- The new version of the UJTL version (20200115) is now available. You can choose the library version when creating a new project.
- When an old project (UAF 1.0 or UPDM2) is opened with the latest version (UAF plugin), stereotypes for nonDSL'ed associations should be applied:
  - when the association is between OperationalAgent, the <<OperationalAssociation>> stereotype is applied.
  - when the association is between ResourcePerformer, the <<ResourceAssociation>> stereotype is applied.
- Operational Signal/Resources Signal now applies automatically for the DSL'ed signal.
- The derived properties of ActualResponsibleResources, ActualOrganization, ActualPost, and ActualPerson were renamed:
  - ownsActualProject renamed to managesActualProject,
  - responsibleForActualResponsibility renamed to ownsActualResponsibility ,
  - responsibleForActualProjectMilestones,
  - responsibleForActualResponsibility renamed to managesActualResponsibility .
- When working with the predefined matrices, you can make some changes to the default settings, such as changing the row or columns element type, dependency criteria, or directions. You can restore the following settings to default at any time: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAFP2021x']Learn more about working with matrices >>>
  - Description
  - Direction
  - Dependency Criteria
  - Show Elements
  - Column Element Type
  - Row Element Type

#### Key issues fixed

- The issues concerning the All Views report have been fixed. The matrices and Producing/Consuming OperationalActivities/Functions are now included.
- The issue where the PartProperty stereotype instead of ReferenceProperty stereotype was applied for the role when the Aggregation relation is created has been fixed.
- The additional stereotype is no longer applied for the element with custom stereotypes after migration from UPDM2 to UAF.
- [UPDM2] The issue where redundant Block stereotypes were applied for specific Environment elements has been fixed. Now only Value Type is applied for Condition, Climate, and LightCondition elements in SysML Compliance mode.
- [UPDM2] The validation rule COR2071 no longer fails in a valid situation.
- [UPDM2] The issue where incorrect metaclass element is created with Impelemnts stereotype applied when solving validation rules COM2023 and COM2047. Now the Abstract relation with the « Implements » stereotype is created.
- A resources tag value of Call Activity now is set when the BPMN compliance mode is ON.
- The issue where Operational Object Flow was not keeping project settings when BPMN compliance mode is ON has been fixed.

You can check [the list](https://support.nomagic.com/secure/IssueNavigator.jspa?reset=true&&pid=10171&resolution=7&fixfor=11483&sorter/field=issuekey&sorter/order=DESC&sorter/field=priority&sorter/order=DESC) of publicly available issues or your own reported issues fixed in UAF Plugin 2021x. 
**Note:** You will be required to login. Use the same username and password as for your account for downloading products.

#### Documentation

- 

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: February 12, <span>2021</span></span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This release introduces significant performance and scalability upgrades in key product areas: migration, converting between frameworks, building matrices, Merge, Teamwork Cloud project load, Teamwork Cloud repository disk footprint, table loading, and copy/paste operations.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Additionally, this version adds support for the UAF 1.1 Profile, new diagrams and tables, and a number of usability and traceability improvements.</span></p><p>Contact your sales representative, and don't forget to give us your feedback on <a class="external-link" href="https://twitter.com/nm_inc" rel="nofollow">Twitter</a> or <a class="external-link" href="https://www.facebook.com/magicdraw/" rel="nofollow">Facebook</a>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Performance Improvements</h2><p>In this version, projects containing 1, 5, and 10 million elements were tested in both UPDM and UAF scopes. After receiving the results, improvements in migration, converting between frameworks, and building matrices were made for better performance.</p><h3>Migration</h3><p>The migration from UPDM2 to UAF 1.1 and from UAF 1.0 to UAF1.1 has been optimized and takes less time than in earlier versions. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_improvement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The comparison of migration time depending on the project size</h6><h3>Converting between frameworks</h3><p>The conversion between frameworks has been optimized. The following table shows the results of converting a project containing approximately 2 million elements and hundreds of framework packages, which needed to be moved or renamed:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 34.2841%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 69.3419%;" /><col style="width: 30.6581%;" /></colgroup><thead><tr><th style="text-align: center;"><p>Earlier versions</p></th><th style="text-align: center;"><p>2021x version</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>The modeling tool stops responding after 30 min</td><td>~ 1 hour</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><br />Building matrices</h3><p>The scope for predefined matrices is no longer set by default. Now all matrices will start to build after the row and column scope is selected.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 34.4487%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 69.2185%;" /><col style="width: 30.7815%;" /></colgroup><thead><tr><th style="text-align: center;">Earlier versions</th><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><p>2021x version</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Load predefined matrices in the projects with 10 million elements:</p><p>~ 1-3 hours. Sometimes more.</p></td><td><p>Create a new matrix:</p><p>   instantly (~1-2s)</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Methods and Standards</h2><h3>UAF 1.1 Profile Support</h3><p>Beginning with this version, the UAF 1.1 profile is supported. Therefore the following migrations will be necessary:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2592acb9-2d45-4f0c-be36-e33590ebab15">UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="eb256e16-4681-4eb0-a0fc-a40d258a564f">UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1. Note that there is no direct migration in this case. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the migration process >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="15bba4bf-e0a4-463a-945e-8698b599f303">UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.1. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="910"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cea-evo-21x.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Evolution of standards</h6><h2>Diagrams and Tables</h2><ul><li data-uuid="22e2a035-c25c-4c1c-8c1a-20632f4e7d64">New <strong>Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart</strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">shows how actual enterprise phases change over time. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li data-uuid="2d983e5a-c699-42a8-9ddb-742bf807d663">New <strong>Date and Time Summary Table</strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Date and Time Summary Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li data-uuid="ba352762-b6d8-4d12-9d48-3e7000cce0b8">New <strong>Personnel Forecast</strong>, <strong>Resources Forecast</strong>, and<strong> Standards Roadmap</strong> diagrams are implemented, improving the way they are created and used. The <strong>Personnel Forecast</strong>, <strong>Resources Forecast</strong>, and <strong>Standards Roadmap</strong> diagrams created with earlier versions of the modeling tool will be marked as deprecated and will be available only for review purposes.</li><li data-uuid="78c937d2-a56b-4e93-b772-dfacba6dd110">The Deployment diagram has been enhanced. The <strong>Strategic Deployment</strong> table is renamed the <strong>Strategic Actual Deployment</strong> table and you can now create it using an <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard</strong>. Moreover, the <strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard is renamed the <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> and enhanced. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_structure_deployment_news.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="c944e283-038b-4062-895c-bf34cac12911"><em>A technology preview </em>of the <strong>Hierarchical Measurements</strong> table. Please note that if you open a project created with earlier versions, the Measurement tables of those projects will be empty. However, you can create them anew.</li></ul><h2>Usability Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="b5efee0e-a00d-428c-a916-d17ec4c85005">The new Personnel Gantt Chart property <strong>Display Actual Persons in Different Lines</strong> was implemented. Now you can choose to see each Actual Person in separate lines.</li><li data-uuid="40ab924e-811f-4439-825b-21adf2a8697a"><p>In the Gantt chart <strong>Options</strong> area, you can choose to mark a required period of time. One of the following Marking Modes are available:</p><ul><li><strong>None</strong>.<strong> </strong>Nothing will be marked.</li><li><strong>Period of Time</strong>. Choose to mark a period of time. For this mode, you can select the Start and End dates to be marked.</li><li><strong>Period of Actual Enterprise Phase</strong>. Choose to mark a period of the Actual Enterprise Phase. The time frame will be marked according to the Start and End dates set for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="145852ae-26fb-4887-9aa0-e24b2ea35e3e"><p>The <strong>Create Association on Part Creation</strong> project option is set to True. This means that when you create parts in the Part compartment of the Resource and Operational performers, the appropriate association is also created in the model.</p></li><li data-uuid="d3fb5bb2-8443-4fbe-b2c3-4d9314f8359f">A new Symbol property <strong>Show Measurements Inherited Name</strong> allows you to display the inherited measurements name through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_measurements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h2>Traceability Improvements</h2><p>The Implementation matrices have been enhanced. Now, b<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">y default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="856489b7-528f-4eb9-aac9-0c6cda1ea473">Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</li><li data-uuid="1cfbcd1b-a06a-4ea1-b785-614e528d1e3f">Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</li><li data-uuid="655c00fd-32ae-4a87-b580-c53b782a8370">Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</li><li data-uuid="4250f003-b354-49cd-bae2-4287913d567d">Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the </span><strong>Specification</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> window.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2021x) UAF Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about working with the implementation matrices >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><h2>Other</h2><ul><li data-uuid="c5c5b17a-b291-4595-81ea-6d6e5a1d5a17">The new version of the UJTL version (20200115) is now available. You can choose the library version when creating a new project.</li><li data-uuid="51e31eda-183a-4a58-b776-9da88b757448"><p>When an old project (UAF 1.0 or UPDM2) is opened with the latest version (UAF plugin), stereotypes for nonDSL'ed associations should be applied:</p><ul><li>when the association is between OperationalAgent, the <em>&lt;&lt;OperationalAssociation&gt;&gt;</em> stereotype is applied. </li><li>when the association is between ResourcePerformer, the <em>&lt;&lt;ResourceAssociation&gt;&gt;</em> stereotype is applied.  </li></ul></li><li data-uuid="3e225560-2a6d-4e35-9bd0-11162cd542d7">Operational Signal/Resources Signal now applies automatically for the DSL'ed signal.</li><li data-uuid="b9ea2c2d-ffd3-4857-9289-9dfa33db84bd">The derived properties of ActualResponsibleResources, ActualOrganization, ActualPost, and ActualPerson were renamed: <br /><ul><li><em>ownsActualProject</em> renamed to <em>managesActualProject, <br /></em></li><li><em>responsibleForActualResponsibility</em> renamed to <em>ownsActualResponsibility</em>, </li><li><em>responsibleForActualProjectMilestones, <br /></em></li><li><em>responsibleForActualResponsibility</em> renamed to <em>managesActualResponsibility</em>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="402bfac7-8653-4b7d-9620-9865e4c8fbc5"><p>When working with the predefined matrices, you can make some changes to the default settings, such as changing the row or columns element type, dependency criteria, or directions. You can restore the following settings to default at any time:</p><ul><li>Description</li><li>Direction</li><li>Dependency Criteria</li><li>Show Elements</li><li>Column Element Type</li><li>Row Element Type</li></ul><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAFP2021x" ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about w<span>orking with matrices</span> &gt;&gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h2>Key issues fixed</h2><ul><li data-uuid="0d8f6f44-3908-4e4e-b8c2-ff5205dbb7b4"><span>The issues concerning the All Views report have been fixed. The matrices and Producing/Consuming OperationalActivities/Functions are now included.</span></li><li data-uuid="6ce64182-e6d2-4fd9-ae46-a2c28a5db820">The issue where the PartProperty stereotype instead of ReferenceProperty stereotype was applied for the role when the Aggregation relation is created has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="dd94b1de-5ba9-4911-9fec-57b03fff7ed9">The additional stereotype is no longer applied for the element with custom stereotypes after migration from UPDM2 to UAF.</li><li data-uuid="cff55775-9ffa-4250-b201-b4a597118e05">[UPDM2] The issue where redundant Block stereotypes were applied for specific Environment elements has been fixed. Now only Value Type is applied for Condition, Climate, and LightCondition elements in SysML Compliance mode.</li><li data-uuid="1277955c-dc6d-446d-a78d-d41964b2a4ae">[UPDM2] The validation rule COR2071 no longer fails in a valid situation.</li><li data-uuid="dc7fc6ee-bd2a-4ea8-b8f6-3baf9f2a3f84">[UPDM2] The issue where incorrect metaclass element is created with Impelemnts stereotype applied when solving validation rules COM2023 and COM2047. Now the Abstract relation with the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">«</span>Implements<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">»</span> stereotype is created.</li><li data-uuid="72f74d02-68eb-4fc5-8818-e8c53056112c">A resources tag value of Call Activity now is set when the BPMN compliance mode is ON.</li><li data-uuid="964efca3-24c9-4efc-bc31-6cb6d6fb6a2f">The issue where Operational Object Flow was not keeping project settings when BPMN compliance mode is ON has been fixed.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5995f486-71ef-4847-9ef2-71545df1f999"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">You can check <a href="https://support.nomagic.com/secure/IssueNavigator.jspa?reset=true&amp;&amp;pid=10171&amp;resolution=7&amp;fixfor=11483&amp;sorter/field=issuekey&amp;sorter/order=DESC&amp;sorter/field=priority&amp;sorter/order=DESC">the list</a> of publicly available issues or your own reported issues fixed in UAF Plugin 2021x. <br /><strong>Note:</strong> You will be required to login. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622825 space=VN version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00063: (2021x) UPDM 2 Plugin

- page_id: `261622825`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622825/2021x+UPDM+2+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T17:17:50.656+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2021x) 2021x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: February 12, 2021

##### Discontinued Products

- The development of the following plugins is discontinued:
  - Document Modeling Plugin (Model-based reports). For more effective model publishing and reviewing on the web, use Cameo Collaborator .
    - To create Views and Viewpoints diagrams using the modeling tool with no plugins installed, use the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin or the SysML plugin instead.
    - The DockBook Library Profile is no longer available.
  - Methodology Wizard Plugin.
  - QVT Plugin.
  - Excel Import Plugin. We highly recommend using Excel/CSV Sync or Excel/CSV Import features instead.
  - CSV Import Plugin. We highly recommend using Excel/CSV Sync or Excel/CSV Import features instead.
  - LieberLieber Embedded Engineer plugin.
  - Project Element Counter plugin.
  - MBSE plugin.
- Web portal reports are no longer available. For more effective model publishing and reviewing on the web, use Cameo Collaborator . You can also use Web Publisher (Simple HTML) or Web Publisher 2.0 ReportWizard templates to generate HTML reports.
- WSDL Code Engineering and WSDL diagrams are no longer available.
- C# Code Engineering and C# Professional Edition are no longer available.
- CIL and CIL Disassembler Code Engineering are no longer available.
- The News Reader (RSS) is no longer available. The notification at the right bottom corner of the modeling tool will inform you about the new version release instead.
- The no_install_mac.zip and 32-bit installation files are no longer offered for download.
- The Project Usage Map Profile is no longer available.
- The JavaScript Nashorn is deprecated with the intention of removing it. We highly recommend using JavaScript Rhino instead.
- The following 3rd party plugins are discontinued:
  - Cameo Workbench
  - Cameo Inter-Op
  - ParaMagic Lite
- The following no cost plugins are no longer available in modeling tools:
  - FAS. From now on, download it here: [https://sourceforge.net/projects/fas4md](https://sourceforge.net/projects/fas4md).
  - SYSMOD. From now on, download it here: [https://sourceforge.net/projects/sysmod4md/](https://sourceforge.net/projects/sysmod4md/).

As of 19.0 version, the Document Modeling Plugin is no longer required when installing theUPDM 2 plugin. Despite that you can still create and work with the Views and Viewpoints diagrams. You must install the Document Modeling Plugin if you wan to generate the following web reports:

- DoDAF Web Report
- MODAF Web Report
- NAF Web Report
- NAF 4.0 Web Report

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing project as web portal' space='UPDM2P2021x']>]]>

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation' space='UPDM2P2021x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

For more effective model publishing and reviewing on the web, use <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CC4TWC2021x/Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud+Documentation" style="text-decoration: none;" rel="nofollow">Cameo Collaborator</a>. You can also use Web Publisher (Simple HTML) or Web Publisher 2.0 ReportWizard templates to generate HTML reports.</li><li>WSDL Code Engineering and WSDL diagrams are no longer available.</li><li>C# Code Engineering and C# Professional Edition are no longer available.</li><li>CIL and CIL Disassembler Code Engineering are no longer available.</li><li><p>The News Reader (RSS) is no longer available. The notification at the right bottom corner of the modeling tool will inform you about the new version release instead.</p></li><li>The <em>no_install_mac.zip</em> and <em>32-bit</em> installation files are no longer offered for download.</li><li>The Project Usage Map Profile is no longer available. </li><li>The JavaScript Nashorn is deprecated with the intention of removing it. We highly recommend using JavaScript Rhino instead.  </li><li>The following 3rd party plugins are discontinued:<ul><li>Cameo Workbench</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Cameo Inter-Op</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">ParaMagic Lite</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The following no cost plugins are no longer available in modeling tools:<br /></span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">FAS. From now on, download it here: <a class="external-link" href="https://sourceforge.net/projects/fas4md" style="text-decoration: none;" title="Follow link">https://sourceforge.net/projects/fas4md</a>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">SYSMOD. From now on, download it here: <a class="external-link" href="https://sourceforge.net/projects/sysmod4md/" style="text-decoration: none;">https://sourceforge.net/projects/sysmod4md/</a>.</span></li></ul></li></ul> <p>As of 19.0 version, the <span class="confluence-link">Document Modeling Plugin</span> is no longer required when <span class="confluence-link">installing the<span class="confluence-link"> UPDM 2 plugin</span></span>. Despite that you can still create and work with the Views and Viewpoints diagrams. You must install the Document Modeling Plugin if you wan to generate the following web reports:</p><ul><li>DoDAF Web Report</li><li>MODAF Web Report</li><li>NAF Web Report</li><li>NAF 4.0 Web Report</li></ul><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2021x" ri:content-title="Publishing project as web portal" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to generate web reports >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="8442ec3d-a516-46c4-b35e-edd34672a1e8"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2021x" ri:content-title="UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UPDM 2 Plugin 2021x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741835 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00064: (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1

- page_id: `272741835`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741835/2022x+Refresh1+2022x+Refresh1+Hot+Fix+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:36.907+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News > (2022x Refresh1) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on: December 23, 2022

2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes Collaboration Powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE platform improvements, an improved launcher for MAC OS Ventura, and several bug fixes.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='NMDOC']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

- Used Project handling principles have been redesigned internally for better performance and stability.
- An error that appeared when trying to add a new project to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform has been fixed.
- The issue where branches could not be created in the 3DEXPERIENCE platform has been fixed.
- The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin has been disabled and does not work with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform.

##### Improved Launcher for MAC OS Ventura

- Application launching with APP is supported on MAC OS Ventura for modeling tools version 2021x Refresh2 HF4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw does not start after upgrading to MAC OS Ventura' space='FAQ']>>]]>

##### Other

###### Unsupported plugins

If collaboration powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE platform is enabled, the followingplugins and features are not supported:

- Cameo Collaborator Publisher
- Model Obfuscator.
- Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact Customer Support for the workaround.
- Publishing MDzipx file to Teamwork Cloud for CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis.

###### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- The issue related to the Activity loop that includes SendSignalAction has been fixed.

###### Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.2

- The issue where the exported .xlsx file was not working correctly has been fixed.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741589 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00066: (2022x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `272741589`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741589/2022x+Refresh1+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:18.853+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloudintroduces the export of comments to the PDF/HTML format, which allows you to archive discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes in the model, a resizable navigation pane in Cameo Collaborator documents, and other improvements.

##### Exporting Comments to PDF/HTML

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x Refresh1 introduces a new feature allowing you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Cameo Collaborator comments exported to PDF.

##### Resizable Navigation Pane

The navigation pane in Cameo Collaborator documents is now resizable, so you'll be able to adjust the document portal according to your needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resizing the navigation pane in a Cameo Collaborator document.

##### Other

- When you simulate a model with a UI mockup or Time Series chart , a new UI window opens automatically during model execution. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Now you can change the number of decimals displayed in Cameo Collaborator documents. When you specify the **Decimal Places** project option in your project, the setting is remembered and used for the documents published from that project as well. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If you use the data markings functionality tomark a project with a custom-defined access level, the same data marking is now automatically applied to all the documents published from that project.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='CC4TWC2022xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741636 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00067: (2022x Refresh1) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `272741636`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741636/2022x+Refresh1+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:22.820+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

In version 2022x Refresh1, Cameo DataHub introduces many new features and improvements. Now you can create synchronization between existing data with newly introduced operation and customize node types in the DOORS data source by importing a custom JSON file. You can also benefit from the two-way synchronization of multi-value properties and mapping enumerations with empty values when working with ENOVIA Requirements.

##### Creating Synchronization Between Existing Data

Create Sync, a new type of operation, is added in the list of Cameo DataHub operations. The Create Sync operation will create synchronization (DHLink) on existing data. Before, in order to create synchronization (DHLinks), you were required to copy data from the data source to the model (Cameo). This new operation allows you to create synchronization(DHLinks) without replicating the data. A wizard will help define source, target, andkey attributes based on which synchronization between source and target data sources are created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Newly created Create Sync operation available in the list of Cameo DataHub operations

##### Two-way multi-value properties synchronization with ENOVIA Requirements

Cameo DataHub connector for ENOVIA Requirements is enhanced with the new capability to enable two-way synchronization for multi-valued attributes. You can map and synchronize enumerated values of the attributes on both sides of the connection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Multi-valued attribute mapping.

##### Mapping Enumerations with Empty Values

Cameo DataHub connector for ENOVIA Requirements is enhanced with the new capability to enable empty valued attributes. Now, you can select <no value> to be mapped to an attribute which does not have any value assigned. This scenario comes in handy when no value is a legitimate value on the list of possible values for a selected attribute.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Mapping properties with empty value attribute

##### Customizing node type according to the JSON template

A new feature is introduced in Cameo Datahub, which allows you to import a custom created Json file for DOORS data source. You can create rules for an attribute's values in order to customize node type using a Json file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting Import Configuration command for adding the custom .json file.

##### Support for ENOVIA Requirements 22xFD03

Cameo DataHub connector for ENOVIA Requirements is now compatible with the platform version 2022x FD03.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo DataHub Documentation' space='CDH2022xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741822 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: (2022x Refresh1) Data Markings and Classification Plugin

- page_id: `272741822`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741822/2022x+Refresh1+Data+Markings+and+Classification+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:35.434+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

The 2022x Refresh1 version introduces new and improved functionality in the**Data Markings and Classification Plugin**, including:

- Display Containment Markings . You can now choose to have Containment Markings that are displayed on the owner element, indicating the owned elements' Data Markings. They are displayed in parentheses as postfixes next to the element name. Use the newly introduced Element Types with Containment Markings project option to specify which element types can have Containment Markings displayed on them. This feature allows you to quickly see the owned elements' Data Markings by simply glancing at the owner element. Learn more about Containment Markings >> [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Containment Markings are updated recursively for the package*Sensitive Information*and its contained elements.

- Sensitive Element Redaction . The Sensitive Element Redaction model transformation allows you to narrow down the full model by removing elements with the selected Data Markings. Controlling what elements should constitute the model based on their Data Markings allows you to provide a redacted model to suppliers who can only have access to some model elements. Learn more about Sensitive Element Redaction >> [IMAGE alt='' src=''] A model transformed via Sensitive Element Redaction by removing elements with*Secret (S)*and*Top Secret (TS)*Data Markings.

- Add Data Markings recursively . From now on, in addition to adding Data Markings to individual elements, you can add them recursively to multiple project elements via the owner element. Use the newly introduced Sensitive Element Types project option to specify which element types can have Data Markings added to them recursively. This feature allows you to add Data Markings to multiple elements conveniently and efficiently. Learn more about adding Data Markings recursively >> [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Adding Data Markings recursively to the Block*Radar*and its Value and Part Properties.

- New validation rules . The new features listed above are supported with additional validation rules.
  - The Invalid Containment Markings rule checks if the Containment Markings match the Data Markings of owned elements.
  - The Missing Data Marking checks if there are elements that are specified to have Data Markings in the Sensitive Element Types project option but do not have any Data Markings added.
  - When a property is typed by a sensitive element, the Inconsistent Data Markings (Property) validation rule checks whether the property has a matching Data Marking or if the sensitivity level of the added Data Marking is not lower than the sensitivity level of the Data Marking added to the type. Learn more about validation rules >>

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

[Back to top](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DMP2022xR1/pages/106638479/2022x+Refresh1+Version+News#id-2022xRefresh1VersionNews-top)

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMP2022xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: November 18, 2022</span></h6><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">The 2022x Refresh1 version introduces new and improved functionality in the<span> </span><strong>Data Markings and Classification Plugin</strong>, including:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="10adc14f-857b-4b1c-8345-b6fb207cc37e"><strong>Display Containment Markings</strong>. You can now choose to have Containment Markings that are displayed on the owner element, indicating the owned elements' Data Markings. They are displayed in parentheses as postfixes next to the element name. Use the newly introduced<span> </span><strong>Element Types with Containment Markings</strong><span> </span>project option to specify which element types can have Containment Markings displayed on them. This feature allows you to quickly see the owned elements' Data Markings by simply glancing at the owner element.<span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DMP2022xR1/pages/106627139/Working+with+Containment+Markings" rel="nofollow">Learn more about Containment Markings &gt;&gt;</a><br /><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Containment_Markings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Containment Markings are updated recursively for the package<span> </span><em>Sensitive Information<span> </span></em>and its contained elements.</h6></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="b4ddeb0f-981c-4358-acc6-5bfad91c2a19"><strong>Sensitive Element Redaction</strong>. The<span> </span><strong>Sensitive Element Redaction<span> </span></strong>model transformation allows you to narrow down the full model by removing elements with the selected Data Markings. Controlling what elements should constitute the model based on their Data Markings allows you to provide a redacted model to suppliers who can only have access to some model elements.<span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DMP2022xR1/pages/106627147/Sensitive+Element+Redaction" rel="nofollow">Learn more about Sensitive Element Redaction &gt;&gt;</a><br /><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Performing_Sensitive_Redaction_6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><h6 style="text-align: center;">A model transformed via Sensitive Element Redaction by removing elements with<span> </span><em>Secret (S)</em><span> </span>and<span> </span><em>Top Secret (TS)</em><span> </span>Data Markings.</h6></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="63eab0ec-f838-447d-abf5-5b9ce5bca13f"><strong>Add Data Markings recursively</strong>. From now on, in addition to adding Data Markings to individual elements, you can add them recursively to multiple project elements via the owner element. Use the newly introduced<span> </span><strong>Sensitive Element Types<span> </span></strong>project option to specify which element types can have Data Markings added to them recursively. This feature allows you to add Data Markings to multiple elements conveniently and efficiently.<span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DMP2022xR1/pages/106627317/Adding+Data+Markings+Recursively" rel="nofollow">Learn more about adding Data Markings recursively &gt;&gt;</a><br /><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Adding_Data_Markings_Recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adding Data Markings recursively to the Block<span> </span><em>Radar<span> </span></em>and its Value and Part Properties.</h6></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="0acba56c-c6f3-494e-ae34-a049dd7f3332"><strong>New validation rules</strong>. The new features listed above are supported with additional validation rules.<ul><li>The<span> </span><strong>Invalid Containment Markings</strong><span> </span>rule checks if the Containment Markings match the Data Markings of owned elements.</li><li>The<span> </span><strong>Missing Data Marking<span> </span></strong>checks if there are elements that are specified to have Data Markings in the Sensitive Element Types project option but do not have any Data Markings added.</li><li>When a property is typed by a sensitive element, the<span> </span><strong>Inconsistent Data Markings (Property)<span> </span></strong>validation rule checks whether the property has a matching Data Marking or if the sensitivity level of the added Data Marking is not lower than the sensitivity level of the Data Marking added to the type.<br /><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DMP2022xR1/pages/106627334/Data+Markings+validation+rules" rel="nofollow">Learn more about validation rules &gt;&gt;</a></li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8305527d-53b5-498f-a96e-3e2ce300cef4"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CT7bQ700dMM&amp;ab_channel=3DSCATIAMBSE" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p style="text-align: right;"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DMP2022xR1/pages/106638479/2022x+Refresh1+Version+News#id-2022xRefresh1VersionNews-top" rel="nofollow">Back to top</a></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="f38cd4b0-9fd9-402d-9a27-4a416769718d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741508 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: (2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `272741508`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741508/2022x+Refresh1+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:15.557+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud 2022x Refresh1 includes exciting new features, such as customizable announcements that can be displayed to users on login and project load, renewed and enhanced Data markings capability, case-insensitiveauthentication against LDAP servers, and password complexity requirements that considerably improve system security. Last but not least, Magic Collaboration Studio services are extended with initial Webhooks support.

In addition, the Collaborator web application introduces the export of comments to the PDF/HTML format, which allows you to archive discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes in the model, a resizable navigation pane in Collaborator documents, and other improvements.

#### Announcements

The 2022x Refresh1 version of Magic Collaboration Studio introduces the ability to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing announcements' space='MCS2022xR1']to be shown to users. These announcements can be displayed as notifications when users log into the server or open specified resources both through the modeling tool and web applications. With this feature in place, you can quickly and easily announce early server upgrades, project deadlines, or any other organization-specific information within the tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a new announcement.

#### Upgraded Data Markings

With the initial [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space='MCS2022xR1'] release, we introduced a way of marking Magic Collaboration Studio users, user groups, resources, and categories with end-user predefined security levels. To allow for an even more granular and flexible access control, the new version of Magic Collaboration Studio allows you to define hierarchical, non-hierarchical (flat) classifiers or a combination of both. This also means that from now on you can assign multiple data markings to a resource, category, user, or group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting a flat data markings group and viewing a user with both hierarchical and flat markings.

#### Password Complexity Requirements

Previously, it was possible to create internal user accounts without any restrictions on their passwords, which might have led to potential security issues. Beginning with the 2022x Refresh1 version, administrators can set the rules for internal user password complexity. These rules apply to existing passwords, as well: users are forced to change their password if new complexity rules get rolled out.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Setting password complexity requirements in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

#### Webhooks (Technology Preview)

From day one, Magic Collaboration Studio offers services and APIs to access and use repository data. With the 2022x Refresh1 release we are stepping forward and introducing a technology preview of the so-called ”inverse API“ – webhooks. With webhooks support, you can get notifications pushed to a specified endpoint on predefined events in Magic Collaboration Studio instead of constantly polling for new data through REST APIs. The initial webhooks release allows you to listen to commit-type events in chosen resources, with more exciting capabilities coming soon!

To learn more about webhooks services, please see the OpenAPI specification of Magic Collaboration Studio REST API.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### SAML Integration Updates

SAML metadata content and URL are changed. Customers using[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SAML integration' space='TWCloud2022xR1']should update SAML metadata URL or metadata file itself on the side of the SAML Identity Provider.

#### Collaborator Application

##### Exporting Comments to PDF/HTML

The Collaborator web application introduces a new feature allowing you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaborator comments exported to PDF.

##### Resizable Navigation Pane

The navigation pane in Collaborator documents is now resizable, so you'll be able to adjust the document portal according to your needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resizing the navigation pane in a Collaborator document.

##### Other

- When you simulate a model with a UI mockup or Time Series chart , a new UI window opens automatically during model execution. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Now you can change the number of decimals displayed in Collaborator documents. When you specify the **Decimal Places** project option in your project, the setting is remembered and used for the documents published from that project as well. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If you use the data markings functionality tomark a project with a custom-defined access level, the same data marking is now automatically applied to all the documents published from that project.

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation' space='MCS2022xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='TWCloud2022xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: November 18, 2022</h6><p><br /></p><p>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud 2022x Refresh1 includes exciting new features, such as customizable announcements that can be displayed to users on login and project load, renewed and enhanced Data markings capability, <span>case-insensitive </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">authentication against LDAP servers</span>, and password complexity requirements that considerably improve system security<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. Last but not least, Magic Collaboration Studio services are extended with initial Webhooks support.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In addition, the Collaborator web application introduces the export of comments to the PDF/HTML format, which allows you to archive discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes in the model, a resizable navigation pane in Collaborator documents, and other improvements.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Announcements</h2><p>The 2022x Refresh1 version of Magic Collaboration Studio introduces the ability to<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2022xR1" ri:content-title="Managing announcements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ set up custom announcements ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>to be shown to users. These announcements can be displayed as notifications when users log into the server or open specified resources both through the modeling tool and web applications. With this feature in place, you can quickly and easily announce early server upgrades, project deadlines, or any other organization-specific information within the tool.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_announcement.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a new announcement.</h6><h2>Upgraded Data Markings</h2><p>With the initial <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2022xR1" ri:content-title="Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Data Markings]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> release, we introduced a way of marking Magic Collaboration Studio users, user groups, resources, and categories with end-user predefined security levels. To allow for an even more granular and flexible access control, the new version of Magic Collaboration Studio allows you to define hierarchical, non-hierarchical (flat) classifiers or a combination of both. This also means that from now on you can assign multiple data markings to a resource, category, user, or group.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting a flat data markings group and viewing a user with both hierarchical and flat markings.</h6><h2>Password Complexity Requirements</h2><p>Previously, it was possible to create internal user accounts without any restrictions on their passwords, which might have led to potential security issues. Beginning with the 2022x Refresh1 version, administrators can set the rules for internal user password complexity. These rules apply to existing passwords, as well: users are forced to change their password if new complexity rules get rolled out.</p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="setting_password_complexity_requirements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Setting password complexity requirements in Teamwork Cloud Admin.</h6><h2>Webhooks (Technology Preview)</h2><p>From day one, Magic Collaboration Studio offers services and APIs to access and use repository data. With the 2022x Refresh1 release we are stepping forward and introducing a technology preview of the so-called ”inverse API“ – webhooks. With webhooks support, you can get notifications pushed to a specified endpoint on predefined events in Magic Collaboration Studio instead of constantly polling for new data through REST APIs. The initial webhooks release allows you to listen to commit-type events in chosen resources, with more exciting capabilities coming soon!</p><p>To learn more about webhooks services, please see the OpenAPI specification of Magic Collaboration Studio REST API.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhooks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>SAML Integration Updates</h2><p>SAML metadata content and URL are changed. Customers using<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2022xR1" ri:content-title="SAML integration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SAML integration ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>should update SAML metadata URL or metadata file itself on the side of the SAML Identity Provider.</p><h2>Collaborator Application</h2><h3>Exporting Comments to PDF/HTML</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Collaborator web application <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">introduces a new feature allowing you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes</span>. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics.</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_comments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Collaborator comments exported to PDF.</h6><h3><br />Resizable Navigation Pane</h3><p>The navigation pane in Collaborator documents is now resizable, so you'll be able to adjust the document portal according to your needs.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigation_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Resizing the navigation pane in a Collaborator document.</h6><h3><br />Other</h3><ul><li data-uuid="304ec7b9-c591-475b-8f86-bafa99a11a01"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When you simulate a model with a<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">UI mockup or Time Series chart</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, a new UI window opens automatically during model execution.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UI_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="ffa1b8e8-62d7-4967-809c-32216b46be98"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Now you can change the number of decimals displayed in Collaborator documents. When you specify the <strong>Decimal Places</strong> project option in your project, the setting is remembered and used for the documents published from that project as well.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="decimal_places.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="f13563e0-331e-45e3-ab43-9a6560231f07"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If you use the data markings functionality to<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> mark a project with a custom-defined access level, the same data marking is now automatically applied to all the documents published from that project.</span></span></li></ul><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2022xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="0685a30a-6ad2-4a7d-866f-034403023049"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2022xR1" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

##### Security improvements

- Addressed security vulnerabilities in logback library.

##### Bug fixes

- Annotations on annotations are no longer ignored.
- An unspecified multiplicity on a «Property Chain» Property no longer causes a validation error.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Concept Modeler Documentation' space='MCM2022xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeler Documentation' space='CCMP2022xR1']

## PAGE 00074: (2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `272741333`
- space_key: `VN`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

The 2022x Refresh1 release introduces several improvements to Item Flow management. These include an option to select which diagrams containing elements that realize Item Flows to update so they show the newly created flows. Also, a quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram, see the element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard and auto-removal of not realized Item Flows. In addition, you can now perform the Requirements verification analysis in the Instance table.

The 2022x Refresh1versionbrings a number of server-side simulation functionality improvements, such as a task manager for managing all currently running simulations, the ability to run server-side simulations from the Resources application, live Time Series Charts, and others. In addition, the Watch pane has been improved to allow adding runtime values directly from Internal Block Definition and Parametric Diagrams and editing them.

The 2022x Refresh1 release brings improvements to the Data Markings and Classification Plugin. The plugin is now enhanced with the ability to display Containment Markings, perform Sensitive Element Redaction model transformation, and add Data Markings recursively.

The client-side performance improvements include the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality that will help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performance caused by the execution of expressions. Additionally, the 2022x Refresh1 version provides a separate installation file for ARM architecture-based macOS (i.e., Apple M1) that allows the modeling tool to run up to 2-3 times faster than with the x86 build.

For general modeling, this release presents an improved error notification display, fully featured Structured Expressions dialogs without the separate Standard/Expert UI modes,a new 'UI Font' environment option for changing the default application font, and an ability to create custom project disclaimer configurations.

Additionally, collaborative modeling is now improved with an ability to show custom centralized announcements in the modeling tool informing users on planned server-wide or project-specific events.

Last but not least, the 2022x Refresh1 release introduces the initial Power'by solution – an alternative collaborative modeling environment that is native to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform and provides an additional set of platform-specific capabilities such as advanced configuration management, traceability across disciplines, and others.

To download the latest version, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. For further information, check the product documentation.

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. Itincludes Collaboration Powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform improvements, an improved launcher for MAC OS Ventura, and several bug fixes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### SysML Features

##### Improved Item Flow Management ExperienceImproved Item Flow Management Experience

SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1 brings several improvements to Item Flow management, which include:

- Selecting diagrams to update flows. From now on, a new Item Flow realization does not end up with unexpected results in existing diagrams (previously, the flow may have been updated or not), as a simple diagram update control mechanism is introduced across the project. The Item Flow Manager dialog and the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard are now enriched with the ability for you to choose which diagrams with the realizing elements to update. [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting_diagrams_to_update_flows.png']

- A quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram. Display/hide the flowing items quickly using the dedicated action in the smart manipulator. [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png']

- Element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection. Selecting the direction for the Item Flow in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard is now easier as the icons indicating element types are displayed next to the element names, allowing you to identify the sender and receiver more easily. [ATTACHMENT filename='Element_type_representation_in__Item_Flow_Direction_selection.png']

- Auto-remove not realized Item Flows. You can now specify the newly introduced Remove Not Realized Item Flows SysML project option to automatically remove Item Flows after the last element that realizes them is deleted. Additionally, you can run the validation to check whether there are any Item Flows in the model that are not realized.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow management' space='SYSMLP2022xR1']>]]>

##### Requirements Verification in Instance TablesRequirements Verification in Instance Tables

In addition to performing requirements verification in Requirement tables, now you can also carry it out in Instance tables. While the verification in a Requirement table allows you to perform the analysis for a single instance set as the context, the verification in an Instance table allows you to perform the analysis for multiple instances at once via the **Enable Patterns-Based Verification** option. It allows you to analyze multiple instances conveniently and efficiently.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Performing the Requirements verification analysis in the Requirement table vs. the Instance table.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

[Learn more about requirements verification in Instance Tables >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2022xR1/Requirements+verification#Requirementsverification-PerformingautomatedRequirementsverificationinInstanceTable)

##### Other ImprovementsOther Improvements

- When you create an Action directly inside the Structured Activity Node, the Allocated To property is specified automatically according to the allocation mode.

#### Simulation Features

##### Server-Side Simulation Improvements

Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2022x Refresh1 brings multiple improvements to the server-side simulation capability released in the previous version of the product.

**Simulation Task ManagerSimulation Task Manager**

Now you can conveniently view and manage all server-side simulations in the Task Manager. The Task Manager not only provides the information about all currently running and queued simulations but also allows you to terminate them and open the simulation UI window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Simulation Task Manager.

**Running simulations via the Resources applicationRunning simulations via the Resources application**

Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2022x Refresh1 introduces a new way of simulating your models on the web. In addition to using REST API, a Python client, or Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you can now run server-side simulations via the Resources web application user interface as well. If a model has a Simulation Configuration and the specified UI, you will be able to run the simulation right from the resource's menu, and the UI window will open once the simulation is started.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Running simulation via the Resources web application UI.

**Live Time Series ChartsLive Time Series Charts**

From version 2022x Refresh1, server-side simulation supports live Time Series Charts. Once you start the simulation and the UI window opens, you can view the Time Series plot being updated in real-time as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Live Time Series Chart.

**Other2Other**

- When you simulate a model with a UI mockup or Time Series chart, the UI window that opens during model execution now has a Control panel allowing you to start, pause, resume, or terminate the simulation. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Now the Timeline and Time Series Chart data can be exported to the HTML/CSV format and stored in the Documentation property of the result Instance Specification.
- Teamwork Cloud has a new Simulation Manager role which allows you to get and review simulation results as well as terminate the simulations executed by other users.

##### Enhanced Watch PaneEnhanced Watch Pane

The Watch pane has been improved to allow adding runtime values directly from Internal Block Definition and Parametric Diagrams. In addition, you can now edit the values added to the Watch pane and see how the simulation results change accordingly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding runtime values to the Watch pane directly from an Internal Block Diagram.

#### Data MarkingsData Markings and Classification Plugin Improvements

The 2022x Refresh1 version introduces new and improved functionality in the **Data Markings and Classification Plugin**, including:

- Display Containment MarkingsDisplay Containment Markings . You can now choose to have Containment Markings that are displayed on the owner element, indicating the owned elements' Data Markings. They are displayed in parentheses as postfixes next to the element name. Use the newly introduced Element Types with Containment Markings project option to specify which element types can have Containment Markings displayed on them. This feature allows you to quickly see the owned elements' Data Markings by simply glancing at the owner element. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Containment Markings' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Containment_Markings.png'] The Containment Markings are updated recursively for the package *Sensitive Information*and its contained elements.
- Sensitive Element RedactionSensitive Element Redaction . The Sensitive Element Redaction model transformation allows you to narrow down the full model by removing elements with the selected Data Markings. Controlling what elements should constitute the model based on their Data Markings allows you to provide a redacted model to suppliers who can only have access to some model elements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sensitive Element Redaction' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Performing_Sensitive_Redaction_6.png'] A model transformed via Sensitive Element Redaction by removing elements with *Secret (S)* and *Top Secret (TS)* Data Markings.
- Add Data Markings recursivelyAdd Data Markings recursively . From now on, in addition to adding Data Markings to individual elements, you can add them recursively to multiple project elements via the owner element. Use the newly introduced Sensitive Element Types project option to specify which element types can have Data Markings added to them recursively. This feature allows you to add Data Markings to multiple elements conveniently and efficiently. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Data Markings Recursively' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Adding_Data_Markings_Recursively.png'] Adding Data Markings recursively to the Block *Radar*and its Value and Part Properties.
- New validation rulesNew validation rules . The new features listed above are supported with additional validation rules.
  - The Invalid Containment Markings rule checks if the Containment Markings match the Data Markings of owned elements.
  - The Missing Data Marking checks if there are elements that are specified to have Data Markings in the Sensitive Element Types project option but do not have any Data Markings added.
  - When a property is typed by a sensitive element, the Inconsistent Data Markings (Property) validation rule checks whether the property has a matching Data Marking or if the sensitivity level of the added Data Marking is not lower than the sensitivity level of the Data Marking added to the type. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings validation rules' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]>

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

#### Client side performance improvementsClient-side Performance Improvements

##### Evaluate Operation PerformanceEvaluate Operation Performance

If you are tired of seeing progress bars while performing various operations in the tool, you can now use the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality, which allows you to view performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performance if it is caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. You can also analyze the building of matrixes and tables.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adorning diagram symbols operation performance evaluation.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

Additionally, you can now see the language recommendations based on their performance. The recommendations are visible via the 'recommended' indication and the performance indicator icon in the language selection menu. The icon color reflects the performance: green for fast (Binary, Structured Expression, Groovy), yellow for medium, and red for slow. It allows you to see the languages' performance when choosing one for your operations and make an informed decision.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Evaluating operation performance' space='MD2022xR1']>]]>

##### Other Improvements2Other Improvements

- **Installation file for Apple M1/M2**. 2022x Refresh1 release provides two different installation files for macOS based on its processor architecture: in addition to the x86 one, now there is also a separate installation file for the one based on ARM (i.e., Apple M1). It is bundled with ARM architecture Java. The modeling tool runs up to 2-3 times faster with ARM architecture Java on Apple M1.

#### miModeling and Infrastructure

- A new UI Font environment option is now available, allowing you to change the default font the application should be displayed in. This change also supports the correct display of non-Latin scripts.
- The Standard/Expert UI modes are now removed from the Structured Expressions dialogs. Instead, the dialogs are now fully featured by default without the need to switch between modes.
- From now on, you can create project disclaimer configurations to display custom disclaimers upon opening a project. The disclaimers can contain any information you want project users to be aware of before opening the project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project disclaimer configuration' space='MD2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='disclaimer_configuration.png']
- Instead of displaying individual error notifications of the same type, they are now grouped based on their type (expression creation failure, expression evaluation failure, model change and diagram load in expressions), allowing a more compact display of notifications (see the image below). [ATTACHMENT filename='Grouping_Multiple_Instances_of_Same_Type_Notifications.png']

#### CollaborationCollaboration

##### Teamwork Cloud AnnouncementsTeamwork Cloud Announcements

The 2022x Refresh1 version of the modeling tool can show custom announcements to users. [Managed through Teamwork Cloud Admin](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2022xR1/Managing+announcements), these announcements can contain information on server-wide upgrades, project-specific deadlines, or any other important news. An announcement is displayed in the modeling tool UI until a user acknowledges it by closing.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Teamwork Cloud announcement displayed on the Welcome screen of a modeling tool and a specific project.

##### Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE PlatformCollaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

We introducePower'by – anew way to collaborate on models within the 3DEXPERIENCE platform environment. For programs and projects requiring models to span across multiple engineering disciplines, Power'by allows leveraging native 3DEXPERIENCE platform capabilities, such as access control, advanced configuration management, traceability through the digital thread, and many others. The initial release aims to provide core collaboration features: storing and loading projects, working on them simultaneously, and committing and updating changes as part of configuration management control.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Systems Modeler Documentation' space='CSM2022xR1']
