ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9462b2e8-ed7f-45be-93c3-5a15162fa1cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Enhanced Watch Pane</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Enhanced Watch Pane</h3><p>The Watch pane has been improved to allow adding runtime values directly from Internal Block Definition and Parametric Diagrams. In addition, you can now edit the values added to the Watch pane and see how the simulation results change accordingly.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_to_watch_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adding runtime values to the Watch pane directly from an Internal Block Diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94159b72-a6b9-4805-9f7e-19570e285a03"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Data Markings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Data Markings and Classification Plugin Improvements</h2><p>The 2022x Refresh1 version introduces new and improved functionality in the <strong>Data Markings and Classification Plugin</strong>, including:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e45408ee-1995-4b21-951b-151fe2c61e46"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c5c6c6a-6d88-4fdb-b0a0-098f0b877f5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Display Containment Markings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Display Containment Markings</strong>. You can now choose to have Containment Markings that are displayed on the owner element, indicating the owned elements' Data Markings. They are displayed in parentheses as postfixes next to the element name. Use the newly introduced <strong>Element Types with Containment Markings</strong> project option to specify which element types can have Containment Markings displayed on them. This feature allows you to quickly see the owned elements' Data Markings by simply glancing at the owner element. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Containment Markings" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Containment Markings >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Containment_Markings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Containment Markings are updated recursively for the package <em>Sensitive Information </em>and its contained elements.</h6></li><li data-uuid="a291a5af-35a4-4ff4-adba-780364d914b1"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3061cace-92d1-4830-87c8-c96a4d1ac17b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Sensitive Element Redaction</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Sensitive Element Redaction</strong>. The <strong>Sensitive Element Redaction </strong>model transformation allows you to narrow down the full model by removing elements with the selected Data Markings. Controlling what elements should constitute the model based on their Data Markings allows you to provide a redacted model to suppliers who can only have access to some model elements. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Sensitive Element Redaction" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Sensitive Element Redaction >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Performing_Sensitive_Redaction_6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">A model transformed via Sensitive Element Redaction by removing elements with <em>Secret (S)</em> and <em>Top Secret (TS)</em> Data Markings.</h6></li><li data-uuid="3ebbd615-50d0-4b0d-9a75-40bb47c3a6f1"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="add65576-98bf-4218-89cd-e02a7953b7f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Add Data Markings recursively</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Data Markings recursively</strong>. From now on, in addition to adding Data Markings to individual elements, you can add them recursively to multiple project elements via the owner element. Use the newly introduced <strong>Sensitive Element Types </strong>project option to specify which element types can have Data Markings added to them recursively. This feature allows you to add Data Markings to multiple elements conveniently and efficiently. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Adding Data Markings Recursively" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about adding Data Markings recursively >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Adding_Data_Markings_Recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adding Data Markings recursively to the Block <em>Radar </em>and its Value and Part Properties.</h6></li><li data-uuid="355c0a2a-a9d4-4910-bfb9-54c47f25c67d"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3efc6b0a-7243-4844-b935-dc8800f2de75"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New validation rules</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New validation rules</strong>. The new features listed above are supported with additional validation rules.<ul><li>The <strong>Invalid Containment Markings</strong> rule checks if the Containment Markings match the Data Markings of owned elements.</li><li>The <strong>Missing Data Marking </strong>checks if there are elements that are specified to have Data Markings in the Sensitive Element Types project option but do not have any Data Markings added.</li><li>When a property is typed by a sensitive element, the <strong>Inconsistent Data Markings (Property) </strong>validation rule checks whether the property has a matching Data Marking or if the sensitivity level of the added Data Marking is not lower than the sensitivity level of the Data Marking added to the type. <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Data Markings validation rules" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about validation rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p>To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e4e392-429a-4518-b95d-59abb2cdec0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CT7bQ700dMM&amp;ab_channel=3DSCATIAMBSE" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5cb0ce0-ea4d-49e5-942e-eb94c9eb9e18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Client side performance improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Client-side Performance Improvements</h2><h3><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b1be07f-bd03-41fc-8b45-9b36bc1af507"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Evaluate Operation Performance</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Evaluate Operation Performance</span></h3><p>If you are tired of seeing progress bars while performing various operations in the tool, you can now use the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality, which allows you to view performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind <span>degraded tool performance</span> if it is caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. You can also analyze the building of matrixes and tables. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Evaluating_operation_performance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adorning diagram symbols operation performance evaluation.</h6><p>To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="530946df-cb5e-4e98-97fd-663d244e5812"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rBAj6JfYlqg&amp;ab_channel=NoMagic" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br />Additionally, you can now see the language recommendations based on their performance. The recommendations are visible via the 'recommended' indication and the performance indicator icon in the language selection menu. The icon color reflects the performance: green for fast (Binary, Structured Expression, Groovy), yellow for medium, and red for slow. It allows you to see the languages' performance when choosing one for your operations and make an informed decision. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="language_performance_indicators.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="Evaluating operation performance" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about operation performance evaluation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21cb4638-78db-48b6-8761-784a19d38c9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Improvements2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="e2250270-2010-4481-8da0-3ff23a263a66"><span><strong>Installation file for Apple M1/M2</strong>. 2022x Refresh1 release provides two different installation files for macOS based on its processor architecture: in addition to the x86 one, now there is also a separate installation file for the one based on ARM (i.e., Apple M1). It is bundled with ARM architecture Java. The modeling tool runs up to 2-3 times faster with ARM architecture Java on Apple M1.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86c1e37a-6f77-4e5e-aa46-ea5b738c8c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mi</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><ul><li data-uuid="c1ec3131-dbe4-4312-9d26-3bb5827ac66e">A new <strong>UI Font</strong> environment option is now available, allowing you to change the default font the application should be displayed in. This change also supports the correct display of non-Latin scripts. </li><li data-uuid="a15c0bf6-e9c5-4577-8d5a-ba4eec647f02">The Standard/Expert UI modes are now removed from the Structured Expressions dialogs. Instead, the dialogs are now fully featured by default without the need to switch between modes.  </li><li data-uuid="f82bc0a9-7b3d-450b-b3d1-2ce07f0d2175">From now on, you can create project disclaimer configurations to display custom disclaimers upon opening a project. The disclaimers can contain any information you want project users to be aware of before opening the project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="Project disclaimer configuration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about project disclaimer configurations >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="disclaimer_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="01aa4c66-5219-41a4-b95d-f2a654e88c2e">Instead of displaying individual error notifications of the same type, they are now grouped based on their type (expression creation failure, expression evaluation failure, model change and diagram load in expressions), allowing a more compact display of notifications (see the image below).<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Grouping_Multiple_Instances_of_Same_Type_Notifications.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2a3e277-67ff-49eb-8666-39ed7c6d17c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Collaboration</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h2><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a45e30d1-959f-4a66-88d6-94a540d8ca72"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Teamwork Cloud Announcements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud Announcements</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The 2022x Refresh1 version of the modeling tool can show custom announcements to users. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2022xR1/Managing+announcements">Managed through Teamwork Cloud Admin</a>, these announcements can contain information on server-wide upgrades, project-specific deadlines, or any other important news. An announcement is displayed in the modeling tool UI until a user acknowledges it by closing.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="announcements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Teamwork Cloud announcement displayed on the Welcome screen of a modeling tool and a specific project.</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bd6f950-1613-4071-ba00-fceea35c88c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We introduce</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Power'by – a</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> new way to collaborate on models within the 3DEXPERIENCE platform environment. For programs and projects requiring models to span across multiple engineering disciplines, Power'by allows leveraging native 3DEXPERIENCE platform capabilities, such as access control, advanced configuration management, traceability through the digital thread, and many others. The initial release aims to provide core collaboration features: storing and loading projects, working on them simultaneously, and committing and updating changes as part of configuration management control.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_through_3DEXPERIENCE.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="e46fe60a-b1de-4a37-86d8-bdcbb9fb5573"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCSE2022xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Cyber Systems Engineer Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Cyber Systems Engineer 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="17a9df73-3bfd-4b5e-9c51-c147e255edd9"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CSM2022xR1" ri:content-title="Cameo Systems Modeler Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Systems Modeler 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741734 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: (2022x Refresh1) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- page_id: `272741734`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741734/2022x+Refresh1+Magic+Model+Analyst+Cameo+Simulation+Toolkit
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:27.479+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit2022x Refresh1 introduces a number of server-side simulation functionality improvements, such as a task manager for managing all currently running simulations, the ability to run server-side simulations from the Resources application, live Time Series Charts, and others. In addition, the Watch pane has been improved to allow adding runtime values directly from Internal Block Definition and Parametric Diagrams and editing them.

#### Server-Side Simulation Improvements

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2022x Refresh1 brings multiple improvements to the server-side simulation capability released in the previous version of the product.

##### Simulation Task Manager

Now you can conveniently view and manage all server-side simulations in the Task Manager. The Task Manager not only provides the information about all currently running and queued simulations but also allows you to terminate them and open the simulation UI window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Simulation Task Manager.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing active simulations' space='CST2022xR1']>]]>

##### Running simulations via the Resources application

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit2022x Refresh1 introduces a new way of simulating your models on the web. In addition to using REST API, a Python client, or Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you can now run server-side simulations via the Resources web application user interface as well. If a model has a Simulation Configuration and the specified UI, you will be able to run the simulation right from the resource's menu, and the UI window will open once the simulation is started.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Running simulation via the Resources web application UI.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation in the Resources application' space='CST2022xR1']>]]>

##### Live Time Series Charts

From version 2022x Refresh1, server-side simulation supports live Time Series Charts. Once you start the simulation and the UI window opens, you can view the Time Series plot being updated in real-time as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Live Time Series Chart.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation with UI' space='CST2022xR1']>]]>

##### Other

- When you simulate a model with a UI mockup or Time Series chart, the UI window that opens during model execution now has a Control panel allowing you to start, pause, resume, or terminate the simulation. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Now the Timeline and Time Series Chart data can be exported to the HTML/CSV format and stored in the Documentation property of the result Instance Specification.
- Teamwork Cloud has a new Simulation Manager role which allows you to get and review simulation results as well as terminate the simulations executed by other users.

#### Enhanced Watch Pane

The Watch pane has been improved to allow adding runtime values directly from Internal Block and Parametric Diagrams. In addition, you can now edit the values added to the Watch pane and see how the simulation results change accordingly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding runtime values to the Watch pane directly from an Internal Block Diagram.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Watch pane' space='CST2022xR1']>]]>

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Model Analyst Documentation' space='MSI2022xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='CST2022xR1']

style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Teamwork Cloud has a new Simulation Manager role which allows you to get and review simulation results as well as terminate the simulations executed by other users.</span></li></ul><h2>Enhanced Watch Pane</h2><p>The Watch pane has been improved to allow adding runtime values directly from Internal Block and Parametric Diagrams. In addition, you can now edit the values added to the Watch pane and see how the simulation results change accordingly.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_to_watch_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adding runtime values to the Watch pane directly from an Internal Block Diagram.</h6><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding Watch items using an Internal Block or Parametric Diagram"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR1" ri:content-title="Watch pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to add runtime values from Internal Block and Parametric Diagrams >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="e6971643-fc58-475b-9351-85124ff30272"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSI2022xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Model Analyst Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Model Analyst 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="63c07823-7f7a-44c7-8b7d-80e0228b6cbe"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR1" ri:content-title="Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741150 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: (2022x Refresh1) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- page_id: `272741150`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741150/2022x+Refresh1+Magic+Software+Architect+MagicDraw
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:32:57.836+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

The 2022x Refresh1 release introduces client-side performance improvements, general modeling, and collaborative modeling enhancements.

The 2022x Refresh1 release brings improvements to the Data Markings and Classification Plugin. The plugin is now enhanced with the ability to display Containment Markings, perform Sensitive Element Redaction model transformation, and add Data Markings recursively.

The client-side performance improvements include the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality that will help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performance caused by the execution of expressions. Additionally, the 2022x Refresh1 version provides a separate installation file for ARM architecture-based macOS (i.e., Apple M1) that allows the modeling tool to run up to 2-3 times faster than with the x86 build.

For general modeling, this release presents an improved error notification display, fully featured Structured Expressions dialogs without the separate Standard/Expert UI modes,a new 'UI Font' environment option for changing the default application font, and an ability to create custom project disclaimer configurations.

Additionally, collaborative modeling is now improved with an ability to show custom centralized announcements in the modeling tool, informing users on planned server-wide or project-specific events.

Last but not least, the 2022x Refresh1 release introduces the initial Power'by solution – an alternative collaborative modeling environment that is native to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform and provides an additional set of platform-specific capabilities such as advanced configuration management, traceability across disciplines, and others.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2'].

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. Itincludes Collaboration Powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform improvements, an improved launcher for MAC OS Ventura, and several bug fixes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### Data MarkingsData Markings and Classification Plugin Improvements

The 2022x Refresh1 version introduces new and improved functionality in the **Data Markings and Classification Plugin**, including:

- Display Containment MarkingsDisplay Containment Markings . You can now choose to have Containment Markings that are displayed on the owner element, indicating the owned elements' Data Markings. They are displayed in parentheses as postfixes next to the element name. Use the newly introduced Element Types with Containment Markings project option to specify which element types can have Containment Markings displayed on them. This feature allows you to quickly see the owned elements' Data Markings by simply glancing at the owner element. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Containment Markings' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Containment_Markings.png'] The Containment Markings are updated recursively for the package *Sensitive Information*and its contained elements.

- Sensitive Element RedactionSensitive Element Redaction . The Sensitive Element Redaction model transformation allows you to narrow down the full model by removing elements with the selected Data Markings. Controlling what elements should constitute the model based on their Data Markings allows you to provide a redacted model to suppliers who can only have access to some model elements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sensitive Element Redaction' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Performing_Sensitive_Redaction_6.png'] A model transformed via Sensitive Element Redaction by removing elements with *Secret (S)* and *Top Secret (TS)* Data Markings.

- Add Data Markings recursivelyAdd Data Markings recursively . From now on, in addition to adding Data Markings to individual elements, you can add them recursively to multiple project elements via the owner element. Use the newly introduced Sensitive Element Types project option to specify which element types can have Data Markings added to them recursively. This feature allows you to add Data Markings to multiple elements conveniently and efficiently. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Data Markings Recursively' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Adding_Data_Markings_Recursively.png'] Adding Data Markings recursively to the Block *Radar*and its Value and Part Properties.

- New validation rulesNew validation rules . The new features listed above are supported with additional validation rules.
  - The Invalid Containment Markings rule checks if the Containment Markings match the Data Markings of owned elements.
  - The Missing Data Marking checks if there are elements that are specified to have Data Markings in the Sensitive Element Types project option but do not have any Data Markings added.
  - When a property is typed by a sensitive element, the Inconsistent Data Markings (Property) validation rule checks whether the property has a matching Data Marking or if the sensitivity level of the added Data Marking is not lower than the sensitivity level of the Data Marking added to the type. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings validation rules' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]>

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

#### Client side performance improvementsClient-side Performance Improvements

##### Evaluate Operation PerformanceEvaluate Operation Performance

If you are tired of seeing progress bars while performing various operations in the tool, you can now use the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality, which allows you to view performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performance if it is caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. You can also analyze the building of matrixes and tables.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adorning diagram symbols operation performance evaluation.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

Additionally, you can now see the language recommendations based on their performance. The recommendations are visible via the 'recommended' indication and the performance indicator icon in the language selection menu. The icon color reflects the performance: green for fast (Binary, Structured Expression, Groovy), yellow for medium, and red for slow. It allows you to see the languages' performance when choosing one for your operations and make an informed decision.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Evaluating operation performance' space='MD2022xR1']>]]>

##### Other ImprovementsOther Improvements

- **Installation file for Apple M1/M2**. 2022x Refresh1 release provides two different installation files for macOS based on its processor architecture: in addition to the x86 one, now there is also a separate installation file for the one based on ARM (i.e., Apple M1). It is bundled with ARM architecture Java. The modeling tool runs up to 2-3 times faster with ARM architecture Java on Apple M1.

#### miModeling and Infrastructure

- A new UI Font environment option is now available, allowing you to change the default font the application should be displayed in. This change also supports the correct display of non-Latin scripts.
- The Standard/Expert UI modes are now removed from the Structured Expressions dialogs. Instead, the dialogs are now fully featured by default without the need to switch between modes.
- From now on, you can create project disclaimer configurations to display custom disclaimers upon opening a project. The disclaimers can contain any information you want project users to be aware of before opening the project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project disclaimer configuration' space='MD2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='disclaimer_configuration.png']
- Instead of displaying individual error notifications of the same type, they are now grouped based on their type (expression creation failure, expression evaluation failure, model change and diagram load in expressions), allowing a more compact display of notifications (see the image below). [ATTACHMENT filename='Grouping_Multiple_Instances_of_Same_Type_Notifications.png']

#### CollaborationCollaboration

##### TWCaTeamwork Cloud Announcements

The 2022x Refresh1 version of MagicDraw can show custom announcements to users. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing announcements' space='TWCloud2022xR1'], these announcements can contain information on server-wide upgrades, project-specific deadlines, or any other important news. An announcement is displayed in MagicDraw UI until a user acknowledges it by closing.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Teamwork Cloud announcement displayed on the Welcome screen of a modeling tool and a specific project.

##### 3DexpCollaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

We introducePower'by – anew way to collaborate on models within the 3DEXPERIENCE platform environment. For programs and projects requiring models to span across multiple engineering disciplines, Power'by allows leveraging native 3DEXPERIENCE platform capabilities, such as access control, advanced configuration management, traceability through the digital thread, and many others. The initial release aims to provide core collaboration features: storing and loading projects, working on them simultaneously, and committing and updating changes as part of configuration management control.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **resources**Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Software Architect Documentation' space='MSA2022xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2022xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="">Collaboration</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h2><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7479aae-f428-41d3-a1db-95b49fe78529"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWCa</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud Announcements</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managed through Teamwork Cloud Admin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, these announcements can contain information on server-wide upgrades, project-specific deadlines, or any other important news. An announcement is displayed in MagicDraw UI until a user acknowledges it by closing.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="announcements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Teamwork Cloud announcement displayed on the Welcome screen of a modeling tool and a specific project.</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52f1d354-8c32-410e-93c0-b1e1a0ec910f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">3Dexp</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We introduce</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Power'by – a</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> new way to collaborate on models within the 3DEXPERIENCE platform environment. For programs and projects requiring models to span across multiple engineering disciplines, Power'by allows leveraging native 3DEXPERIENCE platform capabilities, such as access control, advanced configuration management, traceability through the digital thread, and many others. The initial release aims to provide core collaboration features: storing and loading projects, working on them simultaneously, and committing and updating changes as part of configuration management control.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_through_3DEXPERIENCE.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb63ce8f-8c35-4c05-ab0e-c34c56a1bf70"><ac:parameter ac:name="">resources</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="3fd124f9-e8e1-4428-8e62-6999e79a5bb0"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSA2022xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Software Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Software Architect 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2ff50d4f-41a8-4e14-a667-ce9739d7661d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="MagicDraw Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
